races.race_name: Queen Mother's Cup Handicap (For Female Amateur Jockeys) (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Humble Spark
runners.spotlight: Often a slow starter; couldn't get into it at Carlisle (1m3f, good to firm) a fortnight ago but runner-up twice in a row at Hamilton (1m5f/1m3f, good) before that; could be involved if today's race pans out favourably.
runners.comment: Slow starter; couldn't get into it at Carlisle but runner-up twice in a row previously

runners.name: No Knee Never
runners.spotlight: His last three wins have come in amateur riders' races, the latest at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW), and he shaped when staying on for third at Nottingham (1m2f, good to firm) ten days ago as though this trip, over which he's unraced, may be within range; first-time hood replaces cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Kept on for third over 1m2f at Nottingham ten days ago and this trip may be within range

runners.name: Dunkeld Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Three nursery wins last year (7.4f-8.4f) and back on the scoresheet at Haydock (1m, good to firm) in April; again ran well when fourth at Nottingham (1m2f, good) recently; in the mix if seeing out this new trip.
runners.comment: 3yo who ran well over 1m2f recently and can be in the mix if seeing out this new trip

runners.name: Mayaada
runners.spotlight: 3yo who was runner-up at Epsom (8.5f, good) in April then went one better Salisbury (good) on her first crack at 1m2f; disappointing at Chester seven days later but that run may have come too soon; stayed on well at Salisbury and could be suited by the step up to 1m4f; in calculations.
runners.comment: Stayed on well to win over 1m2f at Salisbury and subsequent run may have come too soon

runners.name: Prince Of The Seas
runners.spotlight: Below best this season for new yard but last month's 4l sixth of ten at Ripon (1m2f, good) was a step back in the right direction and he continues to drop down the weights; won over 1m4f last August; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Below best this season for new yard but dropping down the weights and not ruled out

runners.name: Arqoob
runners.spotlight: Two wins last year (1m4f/1m6f, AW/good to firm) and reappeared with close fifth of eight at Newmarket (1m4f, good) last month; Brodie Hampson is a positive booking; could build on that latest effort provided the reapplied cheekpieces aren't a negative (pulled up over hurdles when worn once previously).
runners.comment: Close fifth on reappearance at Newmarket and could build on that latest effort

runners.name: Secret Force
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced Flat/hurdle winner in Ireland for Gavin Cromwell; close fourth on stable debut at Hamilton (1m3f, good) four weeks ago and Irish amateur Georgie Benson is on board; could play a leading role.
runners.comment: Close fourth on stable debut at Hamilton and Irish amateur Georgie Benson on board; player

runners.name: Parlando
runners.spotlight: Good third over 1m4f on Kempton AW in March and again ran well when filling the same position at Pontefract (1m2f, good) in April; a case can be made on those efforts but subsequent evidence suggests he's gone off the boil.
runners.comment: A case can be made on form as recent as April but he may have gone off the boil

runners.name: Chillingham
runners.spotlight: Awarded this race last year when also ridden by Becky Smith, and 6lb lower this time around; can't be totally written off but he's gone the wrong way over the last 12 months and was well beaten at 100-1 here last month.
runners.comment: Awarded this race last year when 6lb higher but well beaten at 100-1 here last month

races.race_name: Sky Bet Supporting Macmillan Handicap

runners.name: Extremely Zain
runners.spotlight: Won by 7l at Newcastle (6f, AW) last December on sole 2yo start and made it 2-2 with narrow win in Newbury novice (7f, good) last month; he didn't have an ideal passage last time and still looked inexperienced, and this Sussex Stakes entry could prove to be a lot better than his opening mark.
runners.comment: 2-2 in novice races; could prove to be better than opening mark; a 3yo won this last year

runners.name: Aalto
runners.spotlight: His peak RPRs have come in the last two runnings of the Bunbury Cup at Newmarket in July (win/close second); however, he was an encouraging ninth of 28 on reappearance in the Victoria Cup at Ascot (7f, good) and positives today are his reduced mark and the booking of Ryan Moore.
runners.comment: Encouraging reappearance run at Ascot; on a reduced mark and Ryan Moore is booked

runners.name: Daydreama
runners.spotlight: 3yo who has a very mixed record across his ten starts and was well beaten at Carlisle a fortnight ago; however, he's run well over C&D on all three previous visits to York, finishing second of 14 earlier last month; chance.
runners.comment: Inconsistent and well beaten at Carlisle last time but the York factor gives him a chance

runners.name: Recency Bias
runners.spotlight: Won Newcastle novice (7f, AW) in March on third start and runner-up in handicaps on last two outings, beaten a neck at Newcastle (7f, AW) then keeping on well behind a progressive winner at Carlisle (6f, good to firm); the return to 7f may well be a positive and he's on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Runner-up on last two starts, most recently 6f, and return to 7f may well be a positive

runners.name: Mirabeau
runners.spotlight: Snapped a losing sequence in big field at Newbury (6f, good) in April and good second there (6f, good) since; won over C&D in 2024 2yo campaign and he should be fine back up to 7f; bang in form and he's one to consider.
runners.comment: C&D winner who has been in fine form at Newbury of late and is one to consider
runners.owner: The Ffrench Revolution & Mrs T Hamilton

runners.name: Sujet
runners.spotlight: Two wins for Dermot Weld last summer and went close at Chester (7f, good) last month, when racing prominently on his second stable start; disappointing at Thirsk ten days later but that may have come too soon; not written off.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Chester last month on second stable start; next run may have come too soon

runners.name: Son
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in the spring on first two starts for this yard (7f, good) and each-way claims on that form; however, two lesser runs have followed and he's 0-18 since winning in June 2023 on his second outing.
runners.comment: Runner-up in spring on first two starts for this yard but two lesser runs have followed

runners.name: White Crown Star
runners.spotlight: He's on a competitive mark on his best form for Andrew Balding, which includes a good third of 22 over C&D (good) last May; market check advised on first run since last July, having been gelded and changed hands for 62,000gns in the interim, but he needs to be at the very top of his game.
runners.comment: Market check advised on stable/seasonal debut but has to be at the very top of his game

runners.name: Midnight Strike
runners.spotlight: Ex-Joseph O'Brien; he's dropping down the weights and is one to watch in the betting but he's failed to beat a rival at Chester on his first two starts for this yard and a major turnaround is necessary.
runners.comment: Dropping down the weights but has failed to beat a rival on first two starts for this yard
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.name: Akkadian Thunder
runners.spotlight: His losing sequence is up to 13 but he's 4lb lower than when runner-up at Royal Ascot last June and was denied a clear run when third at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) last Saturday; bang there if getting the breaks.
runners.comment: Denied a clear run when third at Doncaster last Saturday and could be bang there

runners.name: Oriental Prince
runners.spotlight: Seven wins last year, six of them over sprint trips on AW and one over 7f at Haydock; on a handy mark on his best form and he could improve for last month's respectable return to action at Hamilton, but today's company may prove too competitive; last of 20 over C&D on Friday.
runners.comment: Seven wins last year but this may prove too competitive; last of 20 over C&D on Friday

runners.name: Lir Speciale
runners.spotlight: There's a suspicion he might ideally need slow ground to produce his best form at 7f, especially here, but he began this season in good form (1m/7.5f, good to soft/good) and there was some mitigation when down the field at Newbury on penultimate start (1m, good; denied clear run); went close over C&D (good) on Friday; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Slow ground would be preferable at this trip but not ruled out; went close here on Friday

runners.name: Brighton Boy
runners.spotlight: Won at Thirsk (7f, good) last month on third stable start and close second at Chester (7f, good) subsequently; won here in September 2024 on sole previous course visit and this in-form 4yo could again be thereabouts.
runners.comment: 4yo who arrives in good form and won here in 2024 on sole previous course visit

runners.name: Andesite
runners.spotlight: Hints of promise when slowly away on last two starts, and he might not be a forlorn hope each-way if breaking on terms; however, he's 0-11 since winning on debut here in May 2024 and others are less risky.
runners.comment: Hints of promise on last two starts, when slowly away each time, but others are less risky

runners.name: Darkness
runners.spotlight: He won two 7f handicaps last season and took a step back in the right direction with close sixth of 12 at Haydock in April; however, well beaten at Newmarket since and others arrive with less to prove.
runners.comment: Step back in right direction at Haydock in April but well beaten at Newmarket since

runners.name: Glenfinnan
runners.spotlight: Returned to form for a neck win at 12-1 at Beverley (7.4f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; back up in grade today but he's just 1lb higher and remains well treated on last season's best efforts; could go well once more.
runners.comment: Returned to form for neck win at Beverley recently; higher grade here but back up just 1lb

runners.name: Gweedore
runners.spotlight: 9yo who has won 13 times, the latest off today's mark at Musselburgh last November; however, he's failed to shine on his three outings this season.
runners.comment: Won off today's mark at Musselburgh last November but this 9yo hasn't shone this season

runners.name: Milford
runners.spotlight: 0-15 since debut win in May 2024 but often ran well off marks in the 90s in some quality handicaps last season, including over C&D; respectable sixth of 14 on recent reappearance at Thirsk and could build on that run today.
runners.comment: Often ran well last season, including over C&D, and could build on recent reappearance 6th

runners.name: Persuasion
runners.spotlight: Won at Musselburgh (7f, good) in April, making it 2-3 at the Edinburgh venue, and he's just 2lb higher today; can't be totally dismissed but this 9yo is inconsistent and was soundly beaten at Thirsk (7f, good) last time.
runners.comment: Won at Musselburgh in April but this 9yo is inconsistent and was soundly beaten at Thirsk

runners.name: Yanifer
runners.spotlight: This is his first run since last October but he is capable of running well after an absence and he's been runner-up in the last two editions of this race (good to soft/good to firm); interesting candidate on return to action.
runners.comment: Absent since last October but runner-up in last two editions of this race; interesting

races.race_name: Sky Bet Race To The Ebor Grand Cup Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Al Qareem
runners.spotlight: Uncharacteristically below par when well beaten at Chester last month and the track wasn't an excuse; four Group 3/Listed wins last year, including when making all in this under a 5lb penalty (same today), and he reappeared with a Listed win at Musselburgh in April; leading claims if back on song.
runners.comment: Uncharacteristically below par latest; leading claims if back on song; won this last year

runners.name: Tabletalk
runners.spotlight: Won the Melrose Handicap here in 2024 (good to firm; for 3yos) and another good run over C&D (good to firm) when third in this last year, 4l behind Al Qareem; not at his best at Ascot last month following a break, and there's a suspicion he may find one or two too good even if back to his peak, but he might not be far away.
runners.comment: 4l third to Al Qareem in this last year; might not be far away if back to his best

runners.name: Epic Poet
runners.spotlight: 0-11 since winning at Meydan last January but he has run really well in Group races in the interim, including the last two runnings of the Yorkshire Cup over C&D; drops into a Listed race today and could be bang there.
runners.comment: He's run really well in defeat over C&D in the last 2 Yorkshire Cups; could be bang there

runners.name: Mount Atlas
runners.spotlight: 5yo who has been an improved performer on his two starts this season, going close behind Al Qareem at Musselburgh (1m6f, soft; Listed, same terms today) then runner-up in the Group 3 Ormonde Stakes at Chester (13.3f, good) in which Al Qareem flopped; he's firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Runner-up in Listed/Group 3 races on his two starts this season and firmly in calculations

runners.name: Roaring Legend
runners.spotlight: Fine efforts when runner-up in 2m AW handicaps in February on first two starts since rejoining this yard; reportedly made a respiratory noise at Musselburgh in April and he's had a third wind operation since; however, even if returning to form, a career-best if needed from this 6yo today.
runners.comment: Wind op since heavy Musselburgh defeat but a career-best is needed from this 6yo

runners.name: Gregory
runners.spotlight: Won Group 2 Queen's Vase at Royal Ascot (1m6f, good) in 2023 and third in Group 2 Lonsdale Cup here (2m, good) in August 2024; absent since disappointing run in Saudi Arabia in February 2025 (wind op since) and returns with something to prove, but he's a major player if retaining his peak ability.
runners.comment: Returns from long absence but he's a major player if retaining his peak ability

races.race_name: Churchill Tyres Supporting Macmillan Sprint Handicap

runners.name: Man Of Vision
runners.spotlight: Comfortably won Wolverhampton novice (7f, AW) last December on third start and beaten just a neck on handicap/seasonal debut at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) last month; his dam was a Group 2 winner and he's in excellent hands to continue to progress; firmly in calculations up 3lb.
runners.comment: Beaten a neck at Newmarket and is in excellent hands to continue to progress

runners.name: First Legion
runners.spotlight: Made it 0-10 since winning on debut over C&D last May when underwhelming at Leicester 19 days ago; however, he was in good form previously, finishing 3l fifth of 14 over 7f here (good) earlier last month when not quite seeing out the trip; edging down the weights and could be involved.
runners.comment: 0-10 since debut win over C&D last May but ran well here last month and could be involved

runners.name: Chicago Pope
runners.spotlight: Built upon previous promise to win a 14-runner handicap at Naas (6f, good) last month on his second start of the season (prominent throughout); this Irish raider is open to further improvement and entitled to respect.
runners.comment: Won 14-runner handicap at Naas last month; Irish raider who is open to further improvement
runners.owner: Anthony Paul Smurfit & Samuel M Mencoff

runners.name: Ruby's Angel
runners.spotlight: Won big-field nursery over C&D (good to firm) at last August's Ebor meeting and continued the good work at Chester (6f, good) last month, finishing second then first; defied the widest stall last time; in with a chance.
runners.comment: Won big-field nursery over C&D last August; strong form at Chester last month; chance

runners.name: Fortification
runners.spotlight: Came with a storming run from the rear to win at 40-1 here (5f, good) last month on his stable debut, and again finished strongly when 1l fourth of 16 here (5f, good) eight days later; he could improve further for this first crack at 6f and is high on the list.
runners.comment: Strong finishes over 5f on first two starts for yard; could improve further tackling 6f

runners.name: Back To Me
runners.spotlight: Made it 2-3 when winning on stable debut in Southwell novice (7f, AW) in April and improved upon his handicap debut sixth when close second of eight at Hamilton (6f, good to soft) recently; unexposed but another step forward is necessary in this higher grade following a 3lb rise.
runners.comment: Close 2nd at Hamilton recently; unexposed but needs another step forward now up in grade
runners.owner: Castle Racing Co Ltd & Mps Racing Ltd

runners.name: Thunder Call
runners.spotlight: Promising second at Doncaster (6f, heavy) last November on second start and he won by 6l on last month's reappearance at Kempton (6f, AW); this close relation to connections' Group 3 winner Skardu has the potential to be better than his opening mark and crack Australian jockey Zac Lloyd has made a good start to his stint in Britain.
runners.comment: Won AW maiden by 6l last month and has the potential to be better than his opening mark

runners.name: Alaminos
runners.spotlight: Very lightly colt who was denied a clear run and looked an unlucky loser behind Cape Ashizuri on his handicap and seasonal debut at Pontefract (6f, good) in April; failed to build on that when favourite at Chester (6f, good) but the track may not have suited; he remains one to be interested in.
runners.comment: Looked an unlucky loser at Pontefract and it's possible Chester didn't suit him last time
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: First Time
runners.spotlight: Won Newmarket maiden (6f, good to firm) for Harry Charlton last August on fourth start and runner-up to Advertised at Newcastle (7f, AW) on subsequent nursery debut; well beaten here (7f, good to firm) last month on seasonal and stable debut and something to prove after that, but not written off.
runners.comment: Well beaten here on seasonal/stable debut but not written off in view of his 2yo promise

runners.name: Five Ways
runners.spotlight: Won 6f AW Group 3 last September and fourth in Group 1 Middle Park at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) three weeks later; below best in Group races on both starts this year but wasn't devoid of promise at Newmarket, and Haydock last time was a first run on slow ground; not discounted on handicap debut.
runners.comment: 4th in Group 1 last September; hasn't shone this year but not discounted on handicap debut

runners.name: Postmodern
runners.spotlight: Impressive on debut at Yarmouth (6f, good to firm) last May and tapped back into that promise when short-headed in a handicap at Meydan (6f, good) in March; gelded since and remains lightly raced; his stable is in excellent form and he's one to be interested in back in Britain following a break.
runners.comment: Short-headed at Meydan in March and returns from a break with stable in excellent form

runners.name: Silent Strike
runners.spotlight: Made debut in January and all four starts over 6f on AW; promise on first three runs then down the field on handicap debut at Newcastle in April; this is a hot race in which to try to bounce back but his prominent early position proved a negative last time; retains potential; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Disappointing on handicap debut at Newcastle but there was an excuse; retains potential

runners.name: Red Spells Danger
runners.spotlight: Beaten over 7f on first six starts before bolting up at 1-16 in maiden at Ripon (6f, good) last month; built on that promise when impressive in 14-runner handicap at Hamilton (6f, good) nine days later; in a higher grade this afternoon off 10lb higher but that rise might not stop him.
runners.comment: Impressive at Hamilton four weeks ago and a 10lb rise might not stop him this afternoon

runners.name: Golden Brown
runners.spotlight: It took him nine attempts to get off the mark, justifying even-money favouritism in a maiden at Salisbury (6f, good) three weeks ago; however, a case can be made judged on his Listed second at Redcar (6f, good to soft) last October; not discounted.
runners.comment: Off mark in Salisbury maiden last month; Listed runner-up last October; not discounted

runners.name: Advertised
runners.spotlight: Very close fourth in valuable final here (7f, good) last October on last 2yo start and reappeared with second of 13 in handicap at Newmarket (6f, good); had an excuse (stumbled leaving the stalls) when down the field at Newmarket last time and has been gelded since; possible player.
runners.comment: Second of 13 at Newmarket on reappearance and had an excuse there since; possible player

runners.name: Kind Touch
runners.spotlight: His two 2yo starts yielded a win at Ripon (5f, good to firm) and close second at Pontefract (6f, good to soft), and he returned with a respectable third on handicap debut at Musselburgh (5f, good); in a hot contest today and improvement is necessary, but that can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: In a hot contest today and improvement is necessary, but that can't be ruled out

runners.name: Cape Ashizuri
runners.spotlight: Won at Ayr (6f, good to soft) on the first of his three 2yo starts and back on the scoresheet on his handicap/seasonal debut at Pontefract (6f, good) in April; 4lb higher in a deeper race today but he's unexposed and his illustrious pedigree hints that he could still have more to offer.
runners.comment: Won on reappearance at Pontefract; in a deeper race today but well bred and unexposed

runners.name: Go Vince Go
runners.spotlight: Three 2yo wins, including here, and ran well when third of 14 at Hamilton (6f, good) last month; however, was no match for winning stablemate Red Spells Danger and has since finished unplaced at Thirsk; others preferred.
runners.comment: Three 2yo wins, including here; not devoid of promise this year but others preferred

runners.name: Underwriter
runners.spotlight: Won maiden/novice races at Ayr (6f, good to firm/good) last year, either side of finishing down the field at Royal Ascot; however, soundly beaten on last three starts, most recently on handicap/seasonal debut at Sandown.
runners.comment: Maiden/novice wins last year but soundly beaten on handicap/seasonal debut at Sandown

runners.name: Reciprocated
runners.spotlight: Won last June on first two starts (6f, good/soft) and encouraging third in first-time cheekpieces at Pontefract (6f, good) on his reappearance; soundly beaten over 7f at Goodwood since when minus the cheekpieces (same today) and, while the drop back to 6f could help, others are more compelling.
runners.comment: Encouraging 3rd on comeback at Pontefract but soundly beaten at Goodwood since (albeit 7f)
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Super Soldier
runners.spotlight: Eight runs last season, the highlight his 6f Group 2 near-miss at Chantilly in July; however, he was inconsistent and finished down the field on his reappearance at Doncaster in March (Listed); others appeal more.
runners.comment: Group 2 runner-up last July but inconsistent and finished down the field on reappearance

runners.name: Boston Dan
runners.spotlight: Two of his best 2yo runs came over C&D, including a Listed second in October (good); however, he's been a long way below that level on both starts this term, most recently in a 5f Listed race here (good) last month.
runners.comment: Strong C&D form last season but a long way below that level on both starts this term

races.race_name: Tomahawk Restaurants Supporting Macmillan EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Stardom Glory
runners.spotlight: 90,000gns yearling by Starspangledbanner; shaped nicely when 1l second of eight to a Karl Burke-trained newcomer on debut over C&D (good; 5-1) three weeks ago, doing best work late on after slow start; should improve.
runners.comment: Pleasing second on debut over C&D three weeks ago, doing best work late on; should improve

runners.name: Inner City Blues
runners.spotlight: 180,000euros foal, 375,000gns yearling, 900,000euros 2yo; Blue Point half-brother to 5f/6f 2yo winner Ironwill (RPR 84); dam 6f AW 2yo winner (92), half-sister to 7f Group 3 winner Dr Zempf, out of 6f 2yo Group 3 winner; represents top connections and went through the sales ring three times for large sums; clearly a significant newcomer.
runners.comment: Went through sales ring three times for large sums; significant newcomer for leading yard

runners.name: Athos
runners.spotlight: 12,000gns yearling; half-brother to six winners including Dynamic (1m-1m2f including Listed; RPR 104) and Cay Dancer (7f-1m4f; 100); dam 1m/1m1f winner; gelded son of Perfect Power; worth a market check with stable running two.
runners.comment: Gelded son of Perfect Power; worth a market check with stable running two

runners.name: Neverbeatdeswalker
runners.spotlight: £27,000 yearling; by Caturra; dam 5f AW winner (RPR 62), half-sister to winners Rural Celebration (5f-6f including 2yo) and Yourartisonfire (6f-1m including 2yo ); already gelded; stable's sole 2yo runner this year was unplaced, although the yard did win this race with a 20-1 shot in 2019.
runners.comment: £27,000 yearling; by Caturra; dam 5f winner; yard's sole 2yo runner this year was unplaced

runners.name: Ten Clarets
runners.spotlight: 35,000euros yearling; by Ten Sovereigns; dam 1m2f winner (RPR 81), half-sister to winners Space Ship (1m2f Flat and 2m hurdle) and Pleiades (7.4f-1m2f), out of US 1m1f/9.5f Grade 1 winner; excellent stable is having another fine season with its 2yos; worth monitoring in the market.
runners.comment: Excellent stable having another fine season with its 2yos; worth monitoring in the market

runners.name: Conciliate
runners.spotlight: Bred for speed and showed some when seventh of eight on debut at Yarmouth (6f, good; 16-1) last month, fading and eased; improvement possible but necessary.
runners.comment: Showed some speed when seventh on debut, fading and eased; improvement possible but needed

runners.name: Sue's Last Chance
runners.spotlight: Too green to do himself justice on debut here (6f, good; 22-1) last month, finishing encouragingly into sixth of the 15 runners; may well improve a chunk and he's impossible to discount.
runners.comment: Too green to do himself justice when sixth here on debut; may well improve a chunk

races.race_name: Ice Co Supporting Macmillan Handicap

runners.name: Point Of Contact
runners.spotlight: Fifth of 15 in a hot 3yo handicap at last year's Ebor meeting (1m, good to firm); another solid 1m course effort when sixth of 18 in Class 2 on Dante meeting reappearance; that rates a good launchpad for the season from this still unexposed 4yo; makes obvious appeal under Ryan Moore.
runners.comment: Another solid York effort when sixth of 18 at Dante meeting; obvious appeal under Moore

runners.name: Pearl Eye
runners.spotlight: Just his third run for Jim Goldie when taking advantage of his reduced mark at Hamilton (1m1f, good) nine days ago; only nudged up 2lb and remains well treated on old form (won last year's Spring Mile at Doncaster off 10lb higher); top of the list.
runners.comment: It hasn't taken Jim Goldie long to find key to him and remains well treated on old form

runners.name: Hale End
runners.spotlight: Returned from two-month break with a win at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) in April; good runner-up efforts at Thirsk (1m, good) and Ayr (1m, good to firm) since; the line came a couple of strides too soon for his strong finish at Ayr and the extra yardage this afternoon may help; enters calculations.
runners.comment: In form, winning at Wetherby and second at Thirsk and Ayr this spring (all 1m); considered

runners.name: Cristo
runners.spotlight: Both wins on Chelmsford's polytrack but placed efforts at Lingfield (1m2f, good to firm) and Salisbury (1m, good) show he's well capable on turf; making headway when clipping heels and unseating his rider approaching the final furlong at Goodwood last week.
runners.comment: Both wins on AW but acts on turf; making headway when unseating at Goodwood last week

runners.name: Financer
runners.spotlight: Runner-up three times last season, including over 1m2f here off 5lb higher; well held at the Dante meeting but subsequent 1m2f Ripon fourth was creditable; drawn widest.
runners.comment: Runner-up here off 5lb higher last summer but this year's efforts no more than respectable
runners.owner: S Bulmer, P Hebdon & R Taylor

runners.name: Harswell Ruby
runners.spotlight: Won at Thirsk (1m, good) in April; has notched three creditable efforts in defeat off this mark since but may be vulnerable for win purposes again.
runners.comment: Thirsk win in April; good efforts in defeat off this mark since but vulnerable for the win

runners.name: They All Know Me
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at the ninth time of asking when coming from well off the pace to shade a tight finish at Carlisle (7f, soft) 26 days ago; second over the extended 1m at Hamilton last year so this longer trip ought to be within range.
runners.comment: Shaded tight finish over 7f at Hamilton latest but has got form over further; up 2lb

runners.name: Leadenhall
runners.spotlight: Back to winning ways at Nottingham (extended 1m, good) 13 days ago; successful off 4lb higher at Haydock last summer (also close third in this race off same mark) so he retains obvious handicapping scope; considered.
runners.comment: Close third in this off 4lb higher last year and arrives on back of recent Nottingham win

runners.name: Kaleido
runners.spotlight: Ran five times for the Gosdens, winning a maiden at Yarmouth (7f, good) last July; no threat in two outings for David O'Meara this year and best watched until showing more.
runners.comment: No threat in two outings for David O'Meara and best watched until showing more

runners.name: Empire Of Light
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who completed a hat-trick when seeing off 12 rivals at Thirsk (1m, good) four weeks ago; the runner-up franked the form with a good second at Epsom last week, while reopposing third Frankie's Dream has also run well since; the draw hasn't been kind given his run style but it's likely his mark is still workable.
runners.comment: On a four-timer and form of latest Thirsk win boosted; drawn wide but still respected

runners.name: Frankies Dream
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a fine 2025 when a six-time winner (from 5f to 7f); has returned in similarly good form, finishing third at Thirsk (1m, good; to Empire Of Light) and Beverley (7.5f, good to firm) last month; each-way claims again.
runners.comment: Six wins last year and has returned in good form, finishing third last twice

runners.name: Grizedale
runners.spotlight: AW novice winner who was a creditable third of nine on turf and handicap debut at Ascot (1m, soft) last autumn; a lesser effort followed at Newmarket but he reappears as an unexposed 4yo who may have more to offer; has been gelded; Zac Lloyd partnered a winner for the stable last weekend.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo who has been gelded ahead of return; Zac Lloyd rode winner for yard recently

runners.name: Freddie Steady Go
runners.spotlight: Cosy winner of bumper (good) on debut; had wind surgery after a moderate hurdle debut and, following a couple of fair Flat runs, he caught his rivals on the hop when making all over 7f at Carlisle (soft) 26 days ago; has more to prove off his opening mark but should have some potential.
runners.comment: Bumper winner; third start on Flat when making all in 7f novice latest; handicap debut
runners.owner: Castle View, A Bell, S Oliver & M Severs

runners.name: Capital Guarantee
runners.spotlight: Three-time 7f/1m winner in 2025; good second at Beverley (7.5f, good to firm) in April but well beaten behind some of today's rivals on his two outings since.
runners.comment: Good second at Beverley but well held twice since; others are more obvious

runners.name: Billyb
runners.spotlight: Course winner (1m, good) last summer and signed off 2025 with 1m success at Southwell; better effort this year when respectable seventh of 11 at Beverley (7.5f, good) a fortnight ago; may come on again.
runners.comment: Course winner who could be approaching peak fitness after two runs this spring

runners.name: Break The Bank
runners.spotlight: Useful handicapper for Andrew Balding but stumbled and unseated rider at the start at Goodwood in August 2024 and not seen since; returns for a new yard (same owner); a watching brief has to be the call.
runners.comment: Off for 658 days and a watching brief has to be the call on yard debut

runners.name: Fantastic Fox
runners.spotlight: Smart handicapper at his peak but his form tailed off in the second half of 2025; has a reduced mark to work with as a result but the betting is perhaps the best guide after 198 days off.
runners.comment: Lost his way in second half of 2025 but well treated if break has had reviving effect

races.race_name: Fordy Marshall Handicap

runners.name: Winged Messenger
runners.spotlight: Keeps getting better and signed off his 4yo campaign by beating Woven a neck in the Ayr Bronze Cup (6f, soft) last autumn; made a pleasing return to action when fourth at Redcar (6f, good to firm) 19 days ago; that should have blown away the cobwebs and, with his usual cheekpieces now returning, he looks ready to strike.
runners.comment: 2025 Ayr Bronze Cup winner; Redcar fourth should have blown away the cobwebs; major player

runners.name: Grant Wood
runners.spotlight: In and out since two Newcastle wins early in the year, down the field at Carlisle most recently; others preferred.
runners.comment: In and out since two Newcastle wins, down the field at Carlisle latest; others preferred

runners.name: Kats Bob
runners.spotlight: Tends to lead and he made all to beat 18 rivals in this race (good to firm) 12 months ago; bubbling under this season, beaten off only late on at Doncaster last Friday; won't mind if rain softens the ground (used to be considered a mud-lover); should make a bold bid for a repeat success.
runners.comment: Won this 12 months ago; bubbling under this year; should make bold bid for repeat success

runners.name: Irish Nectar
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far on AW at Kempton last time but previous turf run was a poor one; would benefit from plenty of rain (all three wins on soft).
runners.comment: Not beaten far on AW last time but previous turf run was poor; needs plenty of rain

runners.name: Territorial Knight
runners.spotlight: Faded at Doncaster six weeks ago but had previously shaped well when fifth in a big field at the Lincoln meeting there; has C&D form and his midsummer efforts last season read well; not ruled out by any means.
runners.comment: Pick of his form reads well; shaped nicely in big field at Lincoln meeting; not ruled out

runners.name: El Bufalo
runners.spotlight: Has done most of his racing on AW (4-20) but seems at least as effective on turf and he went close at Chepstow three weeks ago; all wins over 5f but he tends to finish strongly and should be just as good at 6f; may not be fully exposed and the booking of young Aussie star Zac Lloyd catches the eye.
runners.comment: All wins on AW but turf is fine; close second last time; booking of Zac Lloyd catches eye

runners.name: Manila Scouse
runners.spotlight: Exposed 7yo but he arrives in good form, beaten only a neck at Thirsk two weeks ago; 0-8 at York but has run well here on several occasions; should be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Exposed 7yo but arrives in form, beaten neck at Thirsk last time; should be thereabouts

runners.name: Sports Coach
runners.spotlight: Hasn't built on 5f success at Southwell in March, fading and hanging on this course three weeks ago; unproven at 6f; questions to answer.
runners.comment: Hasn't built on 5f win at Southwell in March and is unproven at 6f; questions to answer

runners.name: Kodiac Thriller
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; has done well since joining Mark Walford, first home on his side in the Great St Wilfrid at Ripon (6f, good to firm) last summer and winning over the same C&D (good to soft) in April; has to bounce back from poor run at Ascot last time but that's certainly possible.
runners.comment: Ripon winner in April; needs to bounce back from poor Ascot run but that's possible

runners.name: Secret Guest
runners.spotlight: Infrequent winner nowadays; faded into fifth of 13 at Hamilton four weeks ago; well below his last winning mark but that reflects a decline in his form.
runners.comment: Infrequent winner nowadays; well below last winning mark but that reflects decline in form

runners.name: Bonnie's Boy
runners.spotlight: Just one win (5f AW at Newcastle) from 17 goes; not seen since respectable fourth over same C&D on New Year's Day; others have stronger claims.
runners.comment: 1-17; not seen since respectable fourth on New Year's Day; others have stronger claims

runners.name: Almarada Prince
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won since 2024 but he's starting to look well handicapped; heavily backed when sixth of 20 behind Lord Roxby and Yes I'm Mali over C&D three weeks ago; bit more needed but he could be building up to something.
runners.comment: On a losing sequence but showing more signs of life, sixth of 20 over C&D three weeks ago

runners.name: Yes I'm Mali
runners.spotlight: Has not been with David O'Meara long; found only Lord Roxby too strong over C&D (good) last month and wasn't disgraced at Thirsk the following week; likely to be in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: Second in big field over C&D last month and not disgraced next time; in the mix again

runners.name: Lethal Nymph
runners.spotlight: Only eighth over C&D last time when quite well fancied but there's nothing wrong with his previous fourth here at the Dante meeting; now below his last winning mark and may well get back on track.
runners.comment: Only eighth here last time but previous fourth reads well; may well get back on track

runners.name: Duran
runners.spotlight: In better heart than recent form-figures suggest, shaping encouragingly at Beverley on reappearance and poorly drawn at Ascot next time; usually runs at 5f but also successful at 6f; falling in weights and has each-way hopes.
runners.comment: Excuses both starts this year; falling in weights and has each-way hopes
runners.owner: D Caslin, P Wilcock & M Cantillon

runners.name: Musical Touch
runners.spotlight: Sixth to Kats Bob in this race 12 months ago; made a winning return at Southwell in April but disappointing at Ripon next time and needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Made winning return at Southwell but disappointing at Ripon next time; must bounce back

runners.name: Lord Roxby
runners.spotlight: Surged back to form when beating Yes I'm Mali 1l over C&D (good ground) three weeks ago; no obvious reason why he won't give his running again but a 6lb rise means he'll probably need a personal best to follow up.
runners.comment: C&D winner three weeks ago; 6lb rise means he'll probably need personal best to follow up
runners.owner: N Skinner D Jenkins J Glover

runners.name: Woven
runners.spotlight: Veteran who retains plenty of ability; fourth in big field at Doncaster on return and fared best of those drawn high when seventh over C&D next time; back to his last winning mark and impossible to discount.
runners.comment: Veteran; best of those drawn high when seventh here last time; impossible to discount
runners.owner: John Sagar And Dunham Trading Ltd

runners.name: Archduke Ferdinand
runners.spotlight: Last two wins have come over 6f at Redcar and he returned to form when third over that C&D last month; however, this is warmer and he has been unplaced on all four of his starts at York.
runners.comment: Goes well at Redcar, third there last month; unplaced all four runs at York

runners.name: Rousing Encore
runners.spotlight: Tends to come from well off the pace and sometimes meets trouble; did well in the circumstances when a never-nearer fourth of 14 at Carlisle two weeks ago; won over C&D last spring; big player if the gaps appear.
runners.comment: Comes from behind; fourth at Carlisle two weeks ago; big player if the gaps appear

runners.name: Westmorian
runners.spotlight: Gained three 6f wins on the AW last spring but went off the boil and refused to race in the Ayr Silver Cup on final outing; 0-13 on turf and never a factor on reappearance (66-1); others appeal more.
runners.comment: Refused to race final 6yo start; never a factor on reappearance; others appeal more

runners.name: Bergerac
runners.spotlight: Fine stable servant with eight wins to his name, two of them here; probably needed the run when down the field at the Dante meeting; the usual headgear returns today and Jack Nicholls claims a handy 5lb; quite interesting.
runners.comment: Dual course winner; probably needed run first time out; Jack Nicholls claims a handy 5lb

races.race_name: Download The BetMGM App Handicap

runners.name: Law Court
runners.spotlight: Has shown steady progress that may well continue; opened his account in Sandown handicap (1m, good) most recently, beating The Joker and taking record this term to 221; solid claims.
runners.comment: Has shown steadily progress that may well continue; won at Sandown most recently

runners.name: Loblolly
runners.spotlight: Recorded sole win on AW but belied his 50-1 odds with good third behind Law Court and The Joker at Sandown (1m) most recently; in the mix, provided he backs up that effort.
runners.comment: Ran well behind Law Court and The Joker at Sandown last time; in the mix

runners.name: Arbaawy
runners.spotlight: Similar type to The Joker, being a consistent maiden who ties in with Law Court on last-time running (over 1m at Newbury in first-time cheekpieces); again should give his running.
runners.comment: Consistent maiden; ties in with Law Court on Newbury running last time

runners.name: Mohaaraj
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced gelding whose form has substance, 2-2 this year, the latest success at Haydock (1m, good) on handicap debut; commands respect with further progress plausible.
runners.comment: Thrice-raced gelding whose form has substance; 2-2 this year; commands respect
runners.owner: Jastar Capital, K Lawrence & P Merritt

runners.name: The Joker
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden who has largely consistent form, latterly 332 since handicapping, latest effort behind Law Court at Sandown (1m); along with that rival, looks a solid contender.
runners.comment: Consistent maiden; good second to Law Court at Sandown most recently; solid

races.race_name: BetMGM Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: City Queen
runners.spotlight: Showed promise on turf in sole 2yo start; has built up a solid record on AW this year, most recently close third over 8.5f when bidding to make it 2-2 in handicaps; gained her win under Toby Moore; should go well.
runners.comment: Has built up a solid record on AW this year; gained her win under Toby Moore

runners.name: Queen's Companion
runners.spotlight: Dubawi filly who has the same form figures as Hatour and, like that rival, is open to improvement in handicaps; her novice efforts (1m) have substance; possibilities if taking well to first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Dubawi filly whose novice efforts have substance; possibilities if taking to headgear
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: Lady Milton
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly on AW; has a record of 1112 since handicapping but needs to prove she can transfer the progress back to turf and indeed handle these deeper waters; steps up further in class.
runners.comment: Needs to prove she can transfer AW progress back to turf and cope with this grade

runners.name: Valkyrie Storm
runners.spotlight: Commenced her campaigned with a couple of Class 5 wins at Nottingham (1m) but came up short off current mark in Class 4 at Windsor in hat-trick bid; that defeat exposes her limitations somewhat.
runners.comment: Came up short in hat-trick bid, exposing her limitations somewhat; mark unaltered

runners.name: Distant Shore
runners.spotlight: Gained her debut win over 6f but the way she shaped over a stiff 7f last time (handicap debut) suggests this step up to 1m will suit; pedigree suggests she should do better still; not dismissed.
runners.comment: Latest effort (7f) suggests this step up to 1m will suit; bred to do better still

runners.name: Karvala
runners.spotlight: Twice-raced filly whose dam finished second in the 1,000 Guineas, carrying these silks; seems likely to build on Thirsk reappearance win (7f) and show additional improvement upped further in trip; one to consider.
runners.comment: Seems likely to build on Thirsk reappearance win and show additional improvement
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Forbidden Colours
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced daughter of Kingman; has shown similar form in finishing fourth in both turf attempts, latest upped to 1m; one of several runners who could well show progress now handicapping.
runners.comment: Daughter of Kingman; one of several runners who could well progress now handicapping

runners.name: Hatour
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced filly who has consistent form figures, latterly on AW this year; showed her 2yo promise in a turf race won by a useful sort; represents major stable and looks open to progress in handicaps; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Thrice-raced filly who represents major yard and looks open to progress in handicaps

runners.name: Guitar Solo
runners.spotlight: Not totally solid on her 7f figures and has a bit to prove off her opening mark; however, may improve for the combination of new trip and getting a crack at handicap level.
runners.comment: Not totally solid on her 7f figures but may improve for new trip; handicap debut
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Flash Of Fire
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced filly whose 2yo turf form comprised a success on debut and solid effort in 1m match race against a progressive rival who has since extended his winning streak; interesting on reappearance.
runners.comment: Thrice-raced filly whose 2yo turf form makes her interesting on reappearance

runners.name: If I Could Dream
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; perhaps unsuited by AW on reappearance but finished last of 11 and consequently brings a doubt over her current form; also lacks the potential of some other rivals.
runners.comment: Maiden who lacks potential and brings a doubt over current form to boot

runners.name: Moonlit Surf
runners.spotlight: Has failed to land a blow in both runs this term but looks quite interesting switched to this scenario on handicap debut, being half-sister to the winner of the maiden race on this card last year; may improve.
runners.comment: Half-sister to the winner of the maiden race on this card last year; handicap debut

races.race_name: BetMGM Scurry Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Words Of Truth
runners.spotlight: Very useful 6f gelding whose last two performances suggest he's perhaps ready for this drop to 5f; may yet tap back into the progress that culminated in Mill Reef success at Newbury (good to soft); leading player, even with 5lb penalty, on return from another layoff.
runners.comment: Very useful 6f gelding who seems ready for a crack at 5f; leading player even with 5lb pen

runners.name: Argentine Tango
runners.spotlight: Useful filly who ran respectably against her elders in the Temple Stakes at Haydock most recently and is one of the main players on ratings back down in grade; capable of taking a serious hand but rather lacks room for progress and is 0-10 since Listed win last June.
runners.comment: Player on ratings but lacks room for progress and is 0-10 since Listed win last June

runners.name: Lady Youmzain
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced filly whose form comprises two novice wins (5f/6f, soft/good) and a good third, despite drifting right, in Haydock Listed event (6f); plausibly open to further improvement and has possibilities back down in distance.
runners.comment: Gained two novice wins then ran well in Listed contest; plausibly open to further progress

runners.name: Military Code
runners.spotlight: Unraced on ground softer than good; closely matched with stablemate Words Of Truth on adjusted ratings; form last year featured two wins early doors and two good efforts in Listed grade, namely head second over C&D and at the York Ebor festival; major contender on reappearance.
runners.comment: Went close in Listed races over C&D and at York last term; major contender on reappearance

runners.name: Our Cody
runners.spotlight: Gained her maiden win a year ago; subsequent form features two creditable efforts at Listed level and, most recently, good third in C&D handicap on seasonal debut; towards the bottom of this pack on ratings, however.
runners.comment: Good third in C&D handicap on seasonal debut but this looks a stiffer assignment

runners.name: Manatee Mehmas
runners.spotlight: Unraced at two; has looked an above-average gelding in two starts (5f) this spring, winning well at Doncaster (good to firm) then creditable fourth in Listed grade at York (good); still open to progress and can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Twice-raced gelding; won well at Doncaster than creditably in York Listed event

runners.name: Poatan
runners.spotlight: Successful at Windsor (6f) and third in Doncaster sales race (6.5f) since having wind surgery, and the form stacks up well; gelded since the last run nine months ago; showed promise over 5f on debut; interesting with further improvement possible.
runners.comment: Form since having wind surgery stacks up well; gelded since last run; interesting

runners.name: Palmeira
runners.spotlight: Appeared to run creditably when a 50-1 shot in Listed races the last twice, albeit taking record to 0-12; technically in form but she's the only maiden in this field and has the worst chance at the weights.
runners.comment: Technically in form but remains a maiden and has the worst chance at the weights

races.race_name: Read Meg Nicholls' Blog At betmgm.co.uk Handicap

runners.name: High Degree
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who was gelded prior to good second, improving his RPR, at Newbury (1m2f, good) on seasonal debut; may well build on that effort and take another step forward; effective over 1m last term, including maiden success; major contender.
runners.comment: Gelded prior to good second at Newbury on reappearance; may well build on that effort

runners.name: Raammee
runners.spotlight: Two from two (on AW) in his first season last year; paid the price for going too freely when beaten favourite in C&D handicap (good) on reappearance but may settle better with the outing under his belt and hood now fitted; still open to further progress and remains of interest.
runners.comment: 2-2 last term; too free over C&D on reappearance but now goes in hood; still of interest

runners.name: Beagle Bay
runners.spotlight: Doing well over 7f this term and justified favouritism with 4l success at Yarmouth (soft) on handicap debut most recently, taking overall record to 2-4; went close over 1m on debut (in 2025); progressive gelding who likely hasn't reached his ceiling yet; respected.
runners.comment: Scored by 4l at Yarmouth last time, taking record to 2-4; likely to improve further

runners.name: Shafdar
runners.spotlight: Met trouble and was unable to land a blow in major 7f handicap last time; broadly progressive at about 1m otherwise this year for current stable, winning twice (AW/turf); could go well, back up in distance.
runners.comment: Best to forgive 7f effort last time; progressive at about 1m otherwise for new yard

runners.name: Circus Of Rome
runners.spotlight: Campaigned over further since the start of last term and gained his handicap double at about 1m4f (stays 1m6f); never dangerous over 1m2f on reappearance; drop back to 1m looks a big negative.
runners.comment: Gained his handicap double at about 1m4f; drop back to 1m looks a big negative

runners.name: Hard Endeavor
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage colt who showed progress last year but was perhaps flattered in French AW Listed event on final start and failed by a long way to repeat the form on handicap/seasonal debut; mark seems stiff.
runners.comment: Low-mileage colt; possibly flattered by apparent best effort; mark seems stiff

runners.name: Cogitate
runners.spotlight: Losing run is mounting up but he ran respectably over C&D most recently (final start for Charles Hills) and ties in with unexposed Raammee on that form; possibilities off same mark, provided he takes well to first-time headgear; remains with same owner.
runners.comment: Ran respectably over C&D last time; possibilities if taking well to headgear; new yard

runners.name: Joolianoss
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW scorer who prevailed at Ascot (1m, good to firm) back on turf last time, looking better than ever, and remains unexposed in this sphere; upped in class but receives weight all round; possible danger.
runners.comment: Prevailed at Ascot last time, looking better than ever, and remains unexposed on turf

runners.name: Hot Cash
runners.spotlight: Back in form and threatening to regain the winning thread, having finished a creditable second in last two starts, latest at Epsom (8.5f, good to soft) on Oaks day; back up another 2lb but can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Creditable second in last two starts, latest at Epsom on Oaks day; not ruled out

runners.name: Tilted Kilt
runners.spotlight: Has gained both wins on AW, including on reappearance; has bits of fairly useful form on turf but produced one of his lesser efforts last time, taking record in this sphere to 0-9; others preferred.
runners.comment: Both wins on AW; latest effort took his turf record to 0-9; others preferred

runners.name: Sean
runners.spotlight: Useful on his day but has become inconsistent, latterly at Meydan, notwithstanding a January performance that's his sole win for current yard; the 9yo can be opposed back on British soil; tongue-tie fitted.
runners.comment: Has become inconsistent, latterly at Meydan; can be opposed back on British soil

runners.name: Cash
runners.spotlight: Capable of very useful form, such as keeping-on second off current mark at Doncaster (1m, good) when last seen nine months ago; just looks far from certain to go one better, being 0-12 since debut success in 2021.
runners.comment: Capable of very useful form but is 0-12 since debut win in 2021; seasonal debut

races.race_name: Bet £10 Get £40 With BetMGM Handicap

runners.name: Crown Office
runners.spotlight: Plenty went wrong in his only blip last season and he won well at Leicester (6f, soft); raced freely and dropped out on reappearance in a 7f handicap at Newbury (good; 15-2); could be a different proposition now gelded; market useful.
runners.comment: A winner at two; raced too freely on comeback and now gelded; market useful

runners.name: Sticktoyourguns
runners.spotlight: Came from off the pace to make a winning debut over C&D on soft last August before going down narrowly under a penalty at Newbury (good to soft); the winner of that race was 125-1 and has become disappointing, but this colt retains potential.
runners.comment: Beaten by a 125-1 chance on his second start but this colt retains potential; h'cap debut

runners.name: Wezzeer
runners.spotlight: Won his second race on the AW but was turned over by a 150-1 chance when penalised at Carlisle (7f, soft); that was actually his best run yet on RPRs but not sure a repeat will see him defy his current mark; the drier ground may help, however.
runners.comment: Not sure his Carlisle form would see him defy this mark, but drier ground may help

runners.name: Utmost Good Faith
runners.spotlight: Won a turf claimer in Ireland and has added two AW handicaps (1m/7f) for current connections; however, has failed to beat a rival in his last three races and a tongue-tie joins his regular hood; risky.
runners.comment: Three wins but failed to beat a rival in his last three races; tongue-tie now added

runners.name: Ironwill
runners.spotlight: Won two minor sprints last year but fairly well held in 6f/7f good-ground handicaps this term and has only dropped 4lb; others appeal more, especially over this stiff 7f.
runners.comment: Well held in 6f/7f good-ground handicaps this term and has only dropped 4lb

runners.name: Spirit Of Saxony
runners.spotlight: Only fifth on his handicap debut at Ascot (1m, good) before justifying odds-on favouritism in an AW novice over 7f; encouraging to see the second win as he liked next time, so this gelding could be on the upgrade.
runners.comment: Fifth on handicap but since improved to win an AW novice at odds-on; form has been franked

runners.name: Archer Royal
runners.spotlight: Owes this high a mark to his third in a Group 3 at Newmarket last September and he's threatening to go the wrong way, with his struggles continuing when trailing home last in the rescheduled Silver Bowl at Carlisle; the cheekpieces tried there are swiftly replaced by blinkers.
runners.comment: Third in a Group 3 last season but threatening to go the wrong way; tries blinkers

runners.name: Cashbox
runners.spotlight: Won back-to-back nurseries in the autumm, over 6f and 7f; gelded in January; he was last on comeback run at Newmarket, since when he's also finished out the back at Windsor only to be declared a non-runner for having his leg stuck in the gates when the stalls opened; plenty to prove now and goes in blinkers.
runners.comment: Won two nurseries but plenty to prove now and goes in blinkers

runners.name: Malachy's Wish
runners.spotlight: Maiden (heavy) and novice (AW) wins last season before coming up well short in a sales race at York in October, again over 7f; gelded since but may need that procedure to have brought him on as this mark doesn't look a gift by any means.
runners.comment: Won twice last season before tackling a sales race; gelded since; mark looks no gift

runners.name: Sovereign Ocean
runners.spotlight: Only defeat last year came in a Group 3 at York and, having left previous yard for 50,000gns, he beat a subsequent winner to justify favouritism in an AW handicap on Boxing Day (7f); won that nicely enough to believe a 7lb rise is manageable, and clearly open to further progress.
runners.comment: 2-3 and won his handicap well enough to believe a 7lb rise is manageable

races.race_name: Darley British EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Turty Tree
runners.spotlight: Comes with some risk having looked far from straightforward under pressure at Newmarket last time, albeit was perhaps partly unsuited by the track and either way may be aided by first-time headgear today; has shown consistent promise otherwise and holds particularly strong claims on penultimate effort, namely third in above-average maiden at Newbury (1m2f, good) that has worked out well.
runners.comment: Seems quirky but otherwise has a very strong chance on penultimate effort; headgear fitted

runners.name: Upper East
runners.spotlight: Showed ability, despite finishing only fourth of five, in Doncaster contest (1m, good to firm) last week; may improve with the outing/experience under his belt, albeit there are mixed messages in his pedigree as regards this longer trip.
runners.comment: Showed ability, despite finishing only fourth of five, at Doncaster last week; may improve

runners.name: Humphrey
runners.spotlight: Newcomer by Pinatubo; closely related to 1m3f Listed winner Lionel (RPR 113), half-brother to 1m3f-1m6f winner Gregory (including Group 2; 116); dam 1m4f-14.5f winner (including Group 2; 106), half-sister to 1m6f Group 1 winner Duncan; a family that has served his connections well; may need longer trips in due course but market confidence should be heeded.
runners.comment: From a family that has served his connections well; market support should be heeded

runners.name: Kinetic Impact
runners.spotlight: Showed promise with fourth of eight, against mostly rivals with experience, under 7lb apprentice in quite a useful novice event at Chepstow (1m2f, good to firm); open to improvement and Billy Loughnane is on board today; possibilities.
runners.comment: Showed promise under 7lb apprentice at Chepstow; Billy Loughnane takes over; possibilities

runners.name: Kudos Too
runners.spotlight: Aclaim half-brother to winners Prontamente (French Group-placed 1m 2yo; RPR 95) and Johnny Reb (6f/7f AW; 81); dam unraced sister to 1,000 Guineas winner Speciosa; one of two newcomers in the field; market instructive.
runners.comment: Aclaim half-brother to two winners; one of two newcomers in the field; market helpful
runners.owner: Mrs R F Greener And North Farm Stud

runners.name: Tropbien
runners.spotlight: Brings the worst form having finished only ninth of ten at Newbury (1m2f, good) where he was easy to back; however, this Frankel colt may take a significant step forward with the experience under his belt; again take note of the betting.
runners.comment: Brings the worst form but he may take a significant step forward from that Newbury effort

races.race_name: Gamble Responsibly At BetMGM Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Double Meaning
runners.spotlight: His two wins last season (1m6f and 2m) both came on AW and by wide margins; also fully effective on turf and he made a most encouraging seasonal return when keeping on strongly in a 1m4f handicap (good ground) at Newmarket four weeks ago; still capable of better and the return to 1m6f will suit; Tom Marquand a good booking (a win and a fourth from two rides for the stable in the last five years); strong claims.
runners.comment: Progressive stayer last year; promising reappearance over 1m4f; more to come back at 1m6f

runners.name: Percy Shelley
runners.spotlight: Three Flat wins in France in 2022 (1m1f-1m3f); two hurdle wins last year (one a walkover) and he didn't run badly when fourth of seven, beaten 2l, at Goodwood last month on his return to the Flat (2m, good; 5-2 joint favourite); Oisin Murphy booked; contender.
runners.comment: Promising return to the Flat at Goodwood last month (2m); Oisin Murphy booked; contender

runners.name: Marnier
runners.spotlight: His only win from nine starts came in a three-runner AW maiden (1m4f) last November; he has run to a useful level in three handicaps this year (1m3f-1m4f) but stepping up to 1m6f needs to prompt further progress
runners.comment: Consistent in handicaps this year but needs to pull out more for the extra yardage

runners.name: Pyleates
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to connections' top-class Pyledriver; promise in four runs at 1m4f, including her handicap debut at Wolverhampton last month, and this trip should suit even better; more to come but this is a rise in class.
runners.comment: Unexposed and should benefit from the new trip; this is a step up in class though

runners.name: Macari
runners.spotlight: Four Flat wins to his name, including over C&D, and he was second of four at Lingfield 18 days ago (1m6f, good); should run his race but perhaps vulnerable for win purposes.
runners.comment: C&D winner; good second at Lingfield last month but needs more for the win
runners.owner: R Vaughan, R Bisiker, M Jones And S West

runners.name: Trojan Storm
runners.spotlight: Now 1lb lower than for his Ascot win last summer (1m6f, good to firm) but that is a result of some modest efforts this year, including when back over more suitable trips on his last three starts; others look safer.
runners.comment: On a good mark but not sure he is in the form required to capitalise

runners.name: Personal Best
runners.spotlight: Her best form has come on slower than good and she looks on a fair mark when granted such conditions; latest Newbury third (2m, good to firm) was a good effort as she was keeping on well before being hampered by the winner 120 yards from home; each-way claims despite the likely drying conditions.
runners.comment: Best on slow ground; ran much better than distance beaten suggests on good to firm latest
runners.owner: Hot To Trot 1 & Mrs R Pease

runners.name: Free World
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced 5yo; not particularly solid on his Meydan form and he suffered a heavy defeat at York (1m4f) on debut for new stable; best watched unless the market suggests better is likely.
runners.comment: Little solid form and he was tailed off on his stable/handicap debut last month

runners.name: Squire Danagher
runners.spotlight: Four AW wins in Ireland in 2024 (10.7f-1m4f), all in blinkers; modest hurdle form for Ben Pauling in 2025; makes his stable debut after 205 days off and he goes without headgear.
runners.comment: On a fair mark on 2024 Irish AW form; blinkers absent on stable debut

races.race_name: Saturday Social Handicap

runners.name: Station X
runners.spotlight: His two turf wins (6f) came on soft and heavy; in good form this year and he made all at Southwell (6f, AW) in April; latest fourth at Carlisle was a fair effort on Tuesday and he's well drawn to attack.
runners.comment: Made all at Southwell (6f) in April; forecast ground fine and he's well drawn to attack

runners.name: Havana Rum
runners.spotlight: His year started well when bustling up Saturday's Epsom "Dash" winner at Pontefract (5f, good to soft) in April but his two runs last month didn't come close and he has the widest stall.
runners.comment: His year started well but two runs last month didn't come close and has the widest stall

runners.name: Ran Amok
runners.spotlight: Both wins over 6f (good to soft and good) and he ran well from off the pace here (5.5f, soft) in September; fairly encouraging return when fifth of 12 at Carlisle (6f, good to soft) last month after which he had wind surgery; handy stall but being 5lb 'wrong' tempers enthusiasm.
runners.comment: Encouraging return and ran well here before; had wind surgery; 5lb 'wrong' but not ignored

runners.name: Brummell
runners.spotlight: Acts on good to soft and softer but his best form is at 5f and latest last of six at Haydock needs forgiving; cheekpieces go on after that; nothing wrong with this mark if they do have a positive effect, however.
runners.comment: Best form at 5f and latest last of six needs forgiving; cheekpieces go on after that

runners.name: Miraculous
runners.spotlight: Two starts here yielded a win and a second for Roger Varian and he looked a shade unlucky when fifth on his return to Chester for this yard in May; hasn't built on that subsequently but his latest midfield finish came in a competitive 20-runner York handicap; unraced on ground slower than good but a good show wouldn't be a surprise.
runners.comment: Goes well here and latest midfield finish came in a competitive 20-runner York handicap

runners.name: Bolo Neighs
runners.spotlight: Wide-margin winner of a valuable sales race at the Curragh as a 2yo and it was a first win since when taking good-ground Ascot handicap (6f) on his return to turf last month; no problem if this ground is softer and sound claims up 4lb.
runners.comment: Won at Ascot (6f, good) latest; no problem if this ground is softer; sound claims up 4lb

runners.name: Accrual
runners.spotlight: Speedy sort but his last two wins have been at 6f (AW); acts on good to soft and encouraging his best turf effort came on his sole start here (5f) back in 2024; last two efforts haven't been his best, however, and the draw hasn't been kind either.
runners.comment: Speedy sort but last two wins have been at 6f (AW); draw hasn't been kind, though

runners.name: Tough Enough
runners.spotlight: There were hopes he was rediscovering his level when chasing home Station X at Southwell (6f, AW) in April but his return to turf didn't go particularly well when again slowly away before finishing down the field at Doncaster last Friday; out of the weights today and others more solid.
runners.comment: Chased home Station X (6f, AW) in April but return to turf didn't go well last Friday

runners.name: Baldomero
runners.spotlight: His turf wins (5f to 6.5f) have come on good or slower; well held in three runs this spring but he offered more at Doncaster one week ago, when third despite blowing the start; mark fine and he needs respecting on this first visit to Chester on his 70th start.
runners.comment: Offered more one week ago, when third despite blowing the start; mark fine; respected

runners.name: Smart Vision
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner who went close here (5f, heavy) in September; yet to get going this term but there is the slightest suspicion he's potentially running into form; attractively handicapped and not completely dismissed at likely rewarding odds.
runners.comment: Yet to get going this term but might be running into form; not completely dismissed

races.race_name: Ladbrokes Get More With Racing Bet Builder Handicap

runners.name: Comic Hero
runners.spotlight: Plenty of promise on his final two starts as a juvenile and he'd been gelded when reappearing with Kempton novice win (7f, AW) in February; suffered from a lack of daylight but still only beaten 2l on his handicap debut (good to soft) in April and he was just pegged at Newmarket (again 1m, good to firm) six weeks ago; likely capable of better again.
runners.comment: Just pegged at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) six weeks ago; likely capable of better again

runners.name: Startled
runners.spotlight: Made it 3-3 in turf handicaps when overcoming trouble to win a shade cosily at York in May and it was a good second of 17 in valuable event at Goodwood (also 7f, good) ten days later; still progressing and he holds leading claims again.
runners.comment: Good second in valuable event last time shows he's still on the up; holds leading claims

runners.name: Masked Warrior
runners.spotlight: Promise when placed in both starts last year (good to soft and soft) but it was much his best effort when cheekpieces and/or AW sparked a win in Kempton novice (7f) 17 days ago; handicapper didn't miss that but still warrants plenty of respect on his debut in this sphere.
runners.comment: Best effort when cheekpieces and/or AW sparked a win 17 days ago; respected on h'cap debut

runners.name: Sir Albert
runners.spotlight: Likes to push the pace and seems versatile ground-wise; his win over slightly shorter here (good) was the middle leg of a summer hat-trick last year and it was a fine effort when close third in Listed Flying Scotsman at Doncaster in September; has a good stall to go forward but his no-show on return to turf (66-1; drawn wide) a fortnight ago needs glossing over.
runners.comment: Last year's form gives him a shot but no-show last time (66-1; drawn wide) needs forgiving

runners.name: Jeddaal
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on his sixth start when bagging Catterick maiden (7f) on his reappearance in April and it was a solid handicap debut when third at Haydock (1m, also good) one month later; latter form has been nicely advertised subsequently and he's on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Solid handicap debut form has been nicely advertised subsequently; on the shortlist

runners.name: Penny Time
runners.spotlight: All starts at 1m on AW; very lively in the market when passing the post first on his debut at Kempton in March; subsequently disqualified after his rider weighed in light but put that right at Southwell ten days later and similar form when second over latter C&D one month on; has been gelded during subsequent break and pedigree suggests turf should be fine ahead of this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Won 1m AW novice; similar form next time; gelded and brings potential to turf/h'cap debut

runners.name: Proud Nation
runners.spotlight: Odds-on when going close on his July debut and he wasted no time getting off the mark at York (also 7f, good; made all) in September on his second start; extended mile possibly stretched him when last of three at Hamilton on last month's return and he still carries potential into this handicap debut; considered.
runners.comment: Made all (7f) on 2nd start; 1m may have stretched on return; considered on handicap debut

runners.name: Jungle Ruler
runners.spotlight: First five starts on AW; made it 2-2 when scoring at Southwell (6f) on his stable debut in January and he's continued to run respectably, including when beaten 3l on his turf debut at Beverley (7.5f, good to soft) six weeks ago; needs more from widest stall in this warmer event but yard did have a welcome winner at Epsom last Saturday.
runners.comment: Beaten 3l on turf debut six weeks ago; needs more from widest stall in this warmer event

runners.name: Just A Girl
runners.spotlight: Made all for a good win in Lingfield novice (7.5f, good) in August and it was a narrow career best when neck second of nine at Carlisle (7f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; might make her presence felt.
runners.comment: Narrow career best when second (7f, good to firm) latest; might make her presence felt

runners.name: Do Bronxs
runners.spotlight: No wins since a 10-1 success on his debut at Ayr about a year ago but he was set some tall orders for the remainder of last term and his reappearance/handicap debut here (also 6f, good) was encouraging; didn't build on that next time, though, and he's just 1lb lower.
runners.comment: Encouraging reappearance/handicap debut here wasn't built on next time; just 1lb lower

races.race_name: Opera Grill Chester Selling Stakes

runners.name: Navy Light
runners.spotlight: Kept on for fourth on his debut at Ripon (6f, good) in April and was done for a change of gear when dropped to 5f and second at Beverley (good to firm) last month; new trip promises to suit and he's a contender.
runners.comment: Done for a change of gear when dropped to 5f latest; new trip a probable plus; player

runners.name: Katie's Cavalier
runners.spotlight: Modest form in two starts, latterly when sixth of 11 in Bath maiden (5.5f, good) last Friday; those efforts still need respecting in this company and the step up in trip promises to suit on pedigree.
runners.comment: Latest sixth (5.5f) last Friday puts him in the mix; step up in trip a plus on pedigree

runners.name: Dream On Kent
runners.spotlight: 33-1 when eighth of 11 in course novice (6f, good) a fortnight ago; late foal who should be capable of better and even that bare form puts him in the picture.
runners.comment: Eighth in course novice (6f) a fortnight ago; even that bare form puts him in the picture

runners.name: Farndale
runners.spotlight: Beat one home on her May debut but some improvement when fifth of ten at Leicester (also 7f, good) on Monday; this is a quick turnaround and she'll probably want further in time but still respected in a modest event.
runners.comment: Some improvement when fifth (7f, good) on Monday; respected in a modest event

runners.name: Bonjour Belle
runners.spotlight: By Without Parole; 15,000gns foal, 5,500gns yearling; seventh foal; dam was a Listed 2yo winner and placed in a Group 2 at three but she's yet to pass on those quality genes; her daughter still deserves a market check in this winnable event.
runners.comment: By Without Parole; 15,000gns foal, 5,500gns yearling; needs a market glance on debut

runners.name: Turbo Shandy
runners.spotlight: 27,000euros yearling by Kodiac; second foal; dam 7f AW 2yo winner (RPR 76), half-sister to AW winners Zafaranah (7f-8.6f) and Ummsuquaim (1m4f), out of 1m-1m2f winning half-sister to US 6f Grade 1 winner I'm The Tiger; introduced at a modest level so interesting to see how she goes in the betting.
runners.comment: 27,000euros yearling by Kodiac; interesting to see how she goes in the betting on debut

runners.name: Em's Dilemma
runners.spotlight: Bungle Inthejungle half-sister to winners Gaiety Musical (1m/8.6f AW; RPR 86), Izzer (5f/5.5f including 2yo/Qatar; 81) and The Mad Monk (7f AW; 61); dam maiden from family of Group 1 winners Dank (9.5f/1m2f) and Eagle Mountain (1m2f); stable has a fair line into this season's juvenile form and she needs monitoring in the market ahead of her debut.
runners.comment: Bungle Inthejungle half-sister to three winners; G1 winners in family; market check needed

runners.name: Liveadream
runners.spotlight: £20,000 breeze-up purchase who was 33-1 when beating two home at Redcar (6f, good to firm) on her introduction 18 days ago; may take a step forward for a yard going well.
runners.comment: 33-1 when beating two home 18 days ago; may step forward for a yard going well
runners.owner: Nick Bradley Racing 13 & P J Makin

runners.name: Poppy Foxy
runners.spotlight: 21,000gns yearling who showed a bit when 10-1 and sixth of 11 at Nottingham (6f, good) a fortnight ago; good draw and can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Showed a bit when 10-1 and sixth at Nottingham (6f, good) a fortnight ago; good draw

runners.name: Delete Delete
runners.spotlight: 20,000gns yearling; 20-1 when beaten a long way in similar event over 6f here a fortnight ago.
runners.comment: 20,000gns yearling; 20-1 when beaten long way in similar event here (6f) a fortnight ago
runners.owner: Team G Racing & Alfa Site Services Ltd

runners.name: Invicta Rose
runners.spotlight: 16,000euros yearling, £25,000 breeze-up 2yo; 16-1 when fourth of 11 in Salisbury novice (6f, soft) on Tuesday; surprising to see her turnaround so quickly and dropped into a seller as that debut wasn't devoid of promise.
runners.comment: 16-1 when fourth (6f, soft) on Tuesday; that debut wasn't devoid of promise; player

races.race_name: Colette, Lynn And Rachel's 50th Birthday Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Rastnet
runners.spotlight: Narrowly won Wolverhampton novice (9.5f, AW) in December and she's been placed in all four handicaps subsequently, including when third over C&D (good; reared start) on her turf debut last month; same mark and new cheekpieces might eke out more; considered.
runners.comment: Running well and new cheekpieces might eke out more; considered

runners.name: Vincent Rocks
runners.spotlight: Dual novice winner (1m, good to soft/AW) who suffered a heavy defeat on his handicap debut at Newbury (1m2f) in September and was beaten even further in the Spring Mile on his comeback in March; turned the ship around when third at Pontefract (1m, good; first-time cheekpieces) last time, however; now needs to build on that tackling this trip again.
runners.comment: Turned the ship around over 1m (first-time cheekpieces) latest; now needs to build on that

runners.name: Impartiality
runners.spotlight: Winner of his first three handicaps in 2025, including here (7.5f, good) at this meeting; also went close at Glorious Goodwood (1m, heavy) off 2lb higher in July; yet to get going this term but he's too well treated to ignore; first attempt at this trip.
runners.comment: Yet to get going this term but too well treated to ignore; first attempt at this trip

runners.name: Lexington Jet
runners.spotlight: Made his first appearance here a winning one when landing 7f handicap (good) a fortnight ago; always handy from a low draw that day and his stamina for this trip is in question; still not ruled out.
runners.comment: Won over 7f here latest; stamina for this trip is in question but not ruled out

runners.name: Ultrasoul
runners.spotlight: Seventh attempt when off the mark from the front in a novice at Kempton (7f, AW) in September and he followed up returned to handicaps when scoring at Nottingham (8.5f, good to soft) five weeks later; pedigree suggests this new trip will suit and he's respected on reappearance.
runners.comment: Won last two and new trip promises to suit on this return; player

runners.name: Sea Founder
