runners.spotlight: Windsor maiden winner on turf last April but he's been confined to AW for all six starts this year; gets a 3lb nudge for his win at Kempton (1m) last time; 1m2f no issue but this is a notch above the standard he usually plies his trade in.
runners.comment: 3lb nudge for Kempton (1m) win last time; trip no issue but this is warmer

runners.name: Due To Henry
runners.spotlight: Last won at Newbury (1m2f, soft) in September 2024; patchy form in nine subsequent starts includes a fifth of seven at Windsor (11.5f, good) last month; others hold stronger claims.
runners.comment: Patchy form since last win in September 2024; others hold stronger claims

runners.name: Pride Of Donegal
runners.spotlight: Steady improver on AW who won his third race in that sphere when making all at Wolverhampton in April; solid effort when third on his turf debut at Thirsk (1m4f, good) six days later and a heavy defeat last time was too bad to be true; probably has the pace to cope with this drop back in trip and he might bounce back.
runners.comment: Latest might be best ignored and he's well drawn to go forward; not ruled out

runners.name: Naval Command
runners.spotlight: Improved with each of last year's qualifying runs but he left Charlie Appleby for 80,000gns in February; made a disappointing handicap/stable debut when sixth of seven at Windsor (11.5f, good) last month; new headgear combination quickly deployed and he comes here with a question to answer.
runners.comment: Disappointing handicap/stable debut; new headgear combination quickly deployed

runners.name: Savvy Victory
runners.spotlight: Placed on both starts here and he continues to slide down the weights, but was beaten 17l into seventh of eight at Pontefract (1m2f, good) last time and will need to do a good deal better than that; no headgear today.
runners.comment: Continues to slide down the weights but will need to do better than last time

runners.name: Golden West
runners.spotlight: 2-2 as a juvenile but he didn't progress in 2024 and left Karl Burke for 25,000gns that October; failed to make the track last year and he's best watched unless the market suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: No progress in 2024 and didn't run last year; cursory market check required on yard debut

races.race_name: John Smith's Handicap

runners.name: Ruby's Profit
runners.spotlight: Just a respectable sixth when well drawn over C&D last month and again a beaten favourite when fourth of seven at Nottingham (5f, good) a fortnight ago; well suited by ground slower than good but probably needs some help from the new cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Running respectably but probably needs some help from the new cheekpieces

runners.name: Al Hussar
runners.spotlight: Yet to run a bad race after six starts this season, and kept on for last-gasp success in 17-runner Thirsk handicap (5f, good) a fortnight ago; also suited by this C&D and slow ground; stall 9 makes things awkward, though.
runners.comment: I good form this year and was a narrow Thirsk winner last time; stall 9 is awkward, though

runners.name: Tricky Tel
runners.spotlight: The only 3yo in the line-up; began career with two course wins (5.5f/6f, good) last spring and built upon his pleasing seasonal reappearance when scoring at Hamilton (6f, good to firm) last month; well drawn today and could still have more to offer if suited by this trip-and-ground combination.
runners.comment: Dual course winner as a 2yo and recently added to his tally at Hamilton

runners.name: Atomic Force
runners.spotlight: Won twice on AW in early part of this year and kept on well from the rear for creditable third in quite valuable C&D (good) contest last month; also suited by good to soft ground and has the early speed to race much closer to the pace from his favourable draw if that's the plan.
runners.comment: Creditable third in quite valuable C&D contest last month and well drawn today; a player

runners.name: Rosenpur
runners.spotlight: 6yo who posted career-best performance when dominating from the front over 6f here (good) last month; 5f on ground slower than good also suits but he's not ideally drawn and recent 4lb rise demands even more from him.
runners.comment: Career-best form when dominating from front here (6f) last month; 4lb rise demands more

runners.name: Copper Knight
runners.spotlight: Veteran with 19 wins on his CV, including three editions of this race and a narrow success at Beverley (5f, good to soft) last month; drawn on the outside today and doesn't have much margin for error off this mark but it won't be a surprise if he gives this a good shot.
runners.comment: Veteran who has won this race three times; drawn wide but might still give good account

runners.name: Blinky
runners.spotlight: Yet to strike form after two starts this season but now 5lb lower than when placed in Epsom's Dash (5f, good) just over a year ago and has won on good to soft ground; can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Yet to strike form after two starts this season but can't be ruled out off this mark

runners.name: Marty Hopkirk
runners.spotlight: Kept on to snatch fourth at Windsor (5f, good) last month but ran poorly over 6f a fortnight ago and has basically become disappointing; needs a good boost from the new cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Snatched fourth at Windsor last month but well held since; needs boost from new headgear

runners.name: Vintage Clarets
runners.spotlight: Gained two of his ten wins over this C&D and returns here after bouncing back to form with commendable second in last week's Epsom Dash (5f, good to soft); still well handicapped after 3lb rise; likely contender.
runners.comment: Commendable second in last week's Epsom Dash and also well suited by this C&D

runners.name: Changeofmind
runners.spotlight: Left disappointing seasonal debut behind when scoring at Pontefract (5f, good to soft) last month; not discredited in big field at Thirsk soon afterwards and has the inside draw here but others look better handicapped.
runners.comment: Won at Pontefract last month and has good draw here but others look better handicapped

races.race_name: Roodee Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: It's All About You
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in last month's Chester Plate (2m2f) but this race is easier and he's now only 1lb higher than when winning over C&D (good to firm) last summer; versatile regarding ground; contender if back on song.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten here last month but now only 1lb higher than for last summer's C&D win
runners.owner: A Baxter, S, Sowray, K Ward

runners.name: Solar Pass
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner (1m/1m4f); runner-up twice since upped to 2m, returned to turf and fitted with cheekpieces last month, more recently behind a progressive rival here (good; also suited by good to soft); good chance.
runners.comment: Chased home progressive rival over C&D a fortnight ago; good chance

runners.name: Action Pact
runners.spotlight: Stout stayer who was placed over C&D (good) in September and opened his account at Pontefract (2m2f, soft) the following month; made a low-key reappearance last month but has possibilities if that run brought him on.
runners.comment: Stout stayer who is suited by this C&D; a possible if last month's run brought him on

runners.name: Barenboim
runners.spotlight: Six-time AW winner, most recently off today's mark in January; struggled when back on turf this month, though, and ground slower than good will be a serious worry here.
runners.comment: Six-time AW winner; struggled when back on turf this month; ground a worry

runners.name: Kingdom Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Looked out of sorts when pulled up in two hurdle races this spring (wore cheekpieces last time) and his best Flat form has been shown on Polytrack; others have much less to prove.
runners.comment: Out of sorts over hurdles this spring and best Flat form was shown on Polytrack

runners.name: Pergamon
runners.spotlight: Not seen on the Flat since down-the-field finish in September but returned from break with two good-ground hurdle wins this spring; should cope if the ground remains slower than good here and reverts to this code on a good mark; interesting.
runners.comment: Dual hurdle winner this spring; reverts to the Flat on a good mark; interesting

runners.name: Heathen
runners.spotlight: Gained all five wins on AW but has made the frame here on both starts for current stable, more recently when staying-on third (behind Solar Pass) over C&D (good) on seasonal debut a fortnight ago; might not be ideally suited by the forecast slower ground today but still enters calculations.
runners.comment: Stayed on for third over C&D on last month's seasonal debut; enters calculations

runners.name: Savrola
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who did well during five-race campaign last season, most notably when gamely winning Northumberland Vase at Newcastle (2m, AW); also suited by good to soft turf but needs to bounce back from last month's disappointing reappearance run.
runners.comment: Did well last season but needs to bounce back from last month's disappointing reappearance

runners.name: Cascade Hall
runners.spotlight: Back in good form this season, most recently when making all to win by over 6l at Musselburgh (2m, good) eight days ago; 6lb higher in much more competitive race here but won't mind if the ground is much slower.
runners.comment: Won by over 6l at Musselburgh last week; 6lb higher in much stronger race here

runners.name: Dino Bellagio
runners.spotlight: Made all to win by 5l on heavy ground here (1m6f) in September and will warrant consideration if conditions become similarly testing today but his seasonal debut was disappointing and he's not one to rely upon.
runners.comment: Made all on heavy ground here in September but is too inconsistent to rely upon

runners.name: Fox Journey
runners.spotlight: Two good runs for new stable this season, the latter a third of 17 at York (1m4f, good) last month; should be fine back at 2m and can cope with ground slower than good; high on the list.
runners.comment: Began season with two good runs over 1m4f for new stable; 2m also suits; respected

races.race_name: Summer Saturday On 27th June Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Idyllic
runners.spotlight: Won twice last year around this trip (good to soft and good) and there was some encouragement when sixth of 12 at Thirsk (1m4f, good) on her reappearance in April; subsequently had wind surgery and she's tried in a hood (dam scored in one) today; not ruled out back down to her last winning mark.
runners.comment: Fair return in April; had wind surgery & hood goes on; not ruled out off last winning mark

runners.name: Newtown Duke
runners.spotlight: 1-2 on the AW and 0-9 on turf, though in the frame seven times as when third of 15 in apprentice handicap over C&D (good) on his reappearance last month; a couple of those in behind have won since and he makes the shortlist.
runners.comment: 0-9 on turf but good third of 15 over C&D (good) on return last month; makes the shortlist

runners.name: Kasgani
runners.spotlight: 0-5 in Ireland for Dermot Weld but he looked unlucky on his stable/handicap debut when third of 14 at Nottingham (1m2f) in April; once again got no luck in running over that C&D (also good) ten days ago and pedigree suggests the return to this trip should be no problem; big player if getting the breaks this time.
runners.comment: Looked unlucky on stable/handicap debut and no run again next time; big player

runners.name: Imperial Trooper
runners.spotlight: Won heavy-ground Ffos Las novice in 2024 for Ed Walker; solid run of form for this yard this year came to a screeching halt over C&D in May and it was the same story, despite market support, at Haydock last time; hard to recommend at present.
runners.comment: Solid run of form came to a screeching halt over C&D in May; same story last time

runners.name: Golspie
runners.spotlight: Captured a third success when winning at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) in December but he's still 3lb higher despite going 0-12 subsequently; has run well on plenty of those occasions, and today's conditions should be fine, but others are more interesting for win purposes.
runners.comment: Last won in December but 3lb higher despite 0-12 since; others more exciting for the win

runners.name: Shipshape
runners.spotlight: Running well since sent beyond 1m4f and he took those form figures to 2121 when landing small-field Hamilton handicap (1m5f, good to firm) 17 days ago; probably acts on softer than good and he can't be ignored in current mood, despite the drop back down in trip, for last year's winning yard.
runners.comment: 2121 since sent beyond 1m4f; can't be ignored in current mood despite drop back in trip

runners.name: Havachoc
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick of 1m4f AW wins in January/February; has continued to run well since and he sported first-time cheekpieces when just held over that C&D 11 days ago; 2lb 'wrong' but acts on turf and likely to go well again.
runners.comment: Continues to run well and wore cheekpieces when just held on AW latest; turf ok; contender

runners.name: Free Speech
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner who didn't get the breaks when sixth on her stable debut at Newcastle at the start of May and she went down narrowly at Wolverhampton (1m4f, AW) 17 days later; these conditions should be fine and she warrants consideration.
runners.comment: Head defeat on AW latest; these conditions should be fine and she warrants consideration

runners.name: Oman
runners.spotlight: Track regular who won here over 14.5f (good to soft) in 2021 and at this trip (good to firm) in 2024; recorded a sixth success when scoring at Goodwood (1m1f, good to soft) last week; beat one was chasing a hat-trick that day and a 3lb nudge looks manageable; not disgraced over an inadequate 7.5f at Chester on Friday.
runners.comment: Beat a hat-trick chaser last week; 3lb nudge looks okay; not disgraced here on Friday

runners.name: Spirit Of Acklam
runners.spotlight: Won at Ayr wearing cheekpieces in April and it wasn't a bad effort when fifth at Ripon (again 1m2f, good) 16 days ago; concerning that the run in between came over this C&D when beaten 24l and said not to have been suited by the track, however.
runners.comment: Won at Ayr 3 starts ago but had a C&D shocker next time when the track was blamed

runners.name: Helmsley
runners.spotlight: Left Ralph Beckett after 1m2f Redcar novice win (good to soft) in October; that form has not been reached in her six handicaps since but latest sixth of 17 at York (1m4f, good) was a fair effort from the widest stall one month ago; draw has been kinder today and she's edging down the weights; tongue-tie goes on.
runners.comment: Fair effort from tough stall latest; edging down the weights and draw is kinder today

runners.name: Wiltshire Wonder
runners.spotlight: Won two 1m4f handicaps at Southwell in late 2024 but he didn't match that form in three starts last year and left Archie Watson for 5,000gns in October; yard (also runs Shipshape) won this with a 33-1 shot last year and this one is likely to be a similar price.
runners.comment: Left Archie Watson for 5,000gns in October having lose his form; probably best watched

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Ardeen Joy
runners.spotlight: Not much promise in a maiden hurdle and bumper last year; improved in two hurdle runs this year especially at Limerick (2m4f) last time, challenging when falling at the last; should go close today if none the worse for that.
runners.comment: Challenging when falling at the last at Limerick; should go close today if none the worse

runners.name: Miss Jocund
runners.spotlight: Third in bumpers at Wexford and Ballinrobe last year before a respectable hurdling debut; beaten a long way on return at Killarney over 2m6f; lots more needed in cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way on return at Killarney over 2m6f; lots more needed in cheekpieces

runners.name: Spring Waltz
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; dam was a hurdle winner and from the family of winners; got off the mark on third point start when winning a mares' maiden at Ballindenisk; can run respectably on hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Won a mares' maiden point at Ballindenisk; can run respectably on hurdling debut

runners.name: Hathaways Cottage
runners.spotlight: Five-time point winner ran respectably on second hurdle start at Down Royal last time, beaten 15l; needs more to win but shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: Beaten 15l last time; needs more to win but shouldn't be far away

runners.name: Torque De Lune
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in two bumpers and was in rear when fell on hurdling debut; best watched for now.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two bumpers and in rear when fell on hurdling debut; best watched for now

runners.name: Everyday Life
runners.spotlight: The best of four point starts was when just denied in a mares' maiden in February on soft; has a decent pedigree and could run well on hurdling debut; the market can guide.
runners.comment: Second in a point last time; could run well on hurdling debut; the market can guide

runners.name: Rumble Demosa
runners.spotlight: Eleventh foal; half-sister to eight winners inc Hang In There (smart 2m-3m2f hurdle/chase) and Logical Song (useful 2m1f-3m4f hurdle/chase); dam French 2m1f hurdle winner; a sibling to some decent sorts and could run respectably on debut.
runners.comment: A sibling to some decent sorts and could run respectably on debut

runners.name: Brosna Queen
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper at Punchestown a year ago but was twice below form in that sphere since; the best of three maiden hurdle runs was at Sligo last time but was still beaten 15l; tough task conceding 8lb.
runners.comment: Best of three maiden hurdle runs was at Sligo last time but a tough task conceding 8lb now

runners.name: Vroomvroomboo
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in a handicap hurdle when last seen and rated 89 so has to find major improvement to score on return.
runners.comment: Well beaten in a handicap when last seen and rated 89 so has to find major improvement

runners.name: Bethpage Theatre
runners.spotlight: Fell on hurdling debut then has been well beaten twice since; will be of more interest in handicaps.
runners.comment: Fell on hurdling debut then well beaten twice since; will be of more interest in handicaps

races.race_name: Randox Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Garnetot
runners.spotlight: The best of eight hurdle runs came when runner-up in a Clonmel maiden over 2m4f last autumn; beaten when fell next time and was poor on return; blinkers need to work.
runners.comment: Was 2nd in a Clonmel maiden last autumn; below form twice since; blinkers need to work

runners.name: Arcland
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner is 1-17 over hurdles; beaten 7l on the Flat and here (2m3f) over hurdles latest starts; needs a bit more but can go well with blinkers added.
runners.comment: Beaten 7l latest hurdle start; needs a bit more but can go well with blinkers added
runners.owner: Exors of the Late E Blaney

runners.name: Warm In Gorey
runners.spotlight: Scored one of his three hurdle starts over C&D back in 2023; lost his form over fences last season but a better run in a Wexford claiming hurdle last time; blinkers retained; needs a bit more.
runners.comment: C&D winner; decent run in a claiming hurdle last time; blinkers retained; needs a bit more

runners.name: Cozone
runners.spotlight: Won over 2m6f at this track in March and his best run since was third at Limerick last time, also over 2m6f; can go well though this trip on good ground might be a bit sharp for him.
runners.comment: Won here in March and 3rd at Limerick last time, both over 2m6f; this might be a bit sharp

runners.name: Liceo
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; French Flat winner is a maiden after eight hurdle runs but some decent efforts including at Tramore last time; up 2lb for that but a repeat should see him go close.
runners.comment: Third at Tramore last time; up 2lb for that but a repeat should see him go close

runners.name: King Of The Greys
runners.spotlight: Showed ability on sole point start; the best of three maiden hurdle runs was when beaten 14l at Down Royal last time; needs more off this mark on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Beaten 14l in a maiden at Down Royal last time; needs more off this mark on handicap debut

runners.name: Solar Generation
runners.spotlight: Produced a much-improved effort on third hurdle start at Kilbeggan last time; hood on for handicap bow and one to consider, especially if strong in the market.
runners.comment: Hood on for handicap bow and one to consider, especially if strong in the market

runners.name: Typical Thomas
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner here in 2022 but hasn't won since taking a Cork chase in 2023; well beaten over fences at Tramore last time; others preferred.
runners.comment: Course winner was well beaten over fences at Tramore last time; others preferred

runners.name: Cooladdi
runners.spotlight: Finished third in a bumper and maiden hurdle last year on good ground in first two starts; not as good twice since on softer ground last December; has the ability to go well on handicap debut and worth a market check.
runners.comment: Below form when last seen; has the ability to go well on h'cap debut; worth a market check

runners.name: Rogue Sea
runners.spotlight: Flat winner in UK showed a hint of ability in four hurdle runs there; fell four out on stable/seasonal debut at Clonmel; only 10/1 that day so considered for a yard that does well here.
runners.comment: Fell on stable/seasonal debut at Clonmel; considered for a yard that does well here

runners.name: Emesteraye
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability in three maidens; solid effort on handicap/seasonal debut at Wexford, tiring late on; up 1lb but should have improved from that run and looks a big player for an in-form yard.
runners.comment: Solid effort on handicap/seasonal debut at Wexford; looks a big player for an in-form yard

runners.name: Black Soul
runners.spotlight: Point winner won a Down Royal maiden hurdle for former yard in 2025; chasing lately and was running a big race at Tramore when falling two out; poor at Kilbeggan since; needs his best.
runners.comment: Good and bad runs over fences last twice; needs his best back hurdling

runners.name: Goodie Girl
runners.spotlight: Beat Arcland when winning her third hurdle race over 2m3f here a year ago; fell after a break at Fairyhouse next time; well beaten on the Flat on recent return; needs her best.
runners.comment: Three-time hurdle winner; well beaten on the Flat on recent return; needs her best

races.race_name: Denvir's Of Downpatrick (Q.R.) Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Forever And A Day
runners.spotlight: Took a big step forward in third maiden hurdle start at Down Royal (2m4f, good) when runner-up behind an easy winner; didn't continue her progress at Wexford; could go well on handicap bow.
runners.comment: Took a big step forward when runner-up in third m'den but didn't build on that latest

runners.name: Western Model
runners.spotlight: Punchestown hurdles winner in May 2024 off 90; record of 0-9 chasing for Paul Nolan in recent times, although made the frame on several occasions; tailed off on stable debut back from a break at Limerick; entitled to improve.
runners.comment: Hurdles winner off 90 in 2024; tailed off on stable debut back from a break

runners.name: Hakensack
runners.spotlight: 0-9 over hurdles but good runs in December for James Hannon when a close third at Cork (2m4f, soft to heavy) and runner-up at Limerick (2m3f, soft to heavy); poor in final two starts for former trainer; still going okay when unseated at four out over fences on stable debut at Limerick; chance at his best.
runners.comment: 0-9 over hurdles but two good runs in Dec'; still going okay when UR over fences latest
runners.owner: Mrs J E Rothwell/Tipp Of Clare Syndicate/L Behan

runners.name: Mount Shenshan
runners.spotlight: Winner of a Clonmel 2m3f handicap hurdle in February last year (soft) off 6lb higher; fifth of 12 when last seen over hurdles at Limerick in April (2m4f, soft to heavy); creditable efforts over fences twice most recently.
runners.comment: Won off 6lb higher last year; creditable efforts over fences recently

runners.name: Artic Chief
runners.spotlight: 0-8 but best effort came on third handicap start here in May when a 1.25l third over C&D (good); not at same level in a similar contest last time.
runners.comment: 0-8 but best effort came over C&D in May when third; not at same level here latest

runners.name: San Franco
runners.spotlight: Placed in a bumper; dismal hurdles form in 2025; not much better on handicap debut at Punchestown.
runners.comment: Dismal hurdles form in 2025 and not much better on h'cap bow last month

runners.name: Hitthehayson
runners.spotlight: Winner of handicap hurdles last term at Roscommon and over C&D before recording wins over fences at Fairyhouse and Haydock; pulled up over fences at Kilbeggan and tailed off over hurdles at Wexford in most recent starts, however, runner-up finish at Down Royal over this trip in April gives him claims.
runners.comment: Dual wins over hurdles and fences last term; poor in two recent runs but runner-up prior

runners.name: Nakassama
runners.spotlight: Justified market support at Hexham this time last year (2m4f, good to soft) to open his account; poor in two chase runs in October, but was again well backed at Ayr in March when denied by a head in a first-time tongue tie (2m, soft, 9-4 fav'); return to further can help; big player.
runners.comment: Attracted market support at Ayr when denied by a head; big player up in trip

runners.name: Andyourbirdcansing
runners.spotlight: Sligo maiden hurdle winner in 2024 (2m2f, good); not a bad run on return at Clonmel, but didn't disprove stamina doubts when weakened into sixth at Ballinrobe (good) over this trip last time; down 3lb.
runners.comment: Didn't disprove stamina doubts when weakened into sixth over this trip last time

runners.name: Annie Questions
runners.spotlight: First reserve; belatedly off the mark in points last month; once placed over hurdles but that was two years ago; no impact in a handicap chase at Roscommon last time.
runners.comment: Reserve; belatedly off the mark in points last month but no impact in a h'cap chase latest

runners.name: Watty Show
runners.spotlight: Didn't show much in maidens for Gary McGill and similar story in four handicaps for this yard; can only be watched.
runners.comment: Didn't show much in m'dens and similar story in four h'caps for this yard

runners.name: Jolly Potter
runners.spotlight: Moderate French hurdles form; didn't make the track for Gordon Elliott; tailed off on belated Irish debut and beat few rivals at Kilbeggan; goes handicapping now.
runners.comment: Moderate hurdles form; goes handicapping now

runners.name: Hunting Gold
runners.spotlight: Best run was in a Naas academy hurdle on debut when fifth (2m1f, soft to heavy); moderate since, including when tailed off on handicap bow at Punchestown last month; cheekpieces on now.
runners.comment: Decent run on debut but moderate since inc' on h'cap bow last month

runners.name: Go Avel
runners.spotlight: Improved over moderate Sligo return to action when a 19.5l fourth (winner DSQ) at Limerick last time (2m4f, soft); needs to build on that.
runners.comment: Improved over moderate return to action when fourth last time; must build on it
runners.owner: Mrs Peter Casey & Mrs Paula Casey

runners.name: Thatsmyroadie
runners.spotlight: No sign of winning potential in maidens nor in two handicap starts.
runners.comment: No sign of winning potential in maidens nor in two handicap starts

runners.name: Steps In The Sand
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner, including at this venue in 2024; added third turf Flat win at Galway last summer; middling efforts in two Flat starts this year.
runners.comment: Dual hurdle winner inc' here in 2024; middling efforts in two Flat starts this year

runners.name: Beau's Candle
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; dual winning pointer in 2023; out of form in in points this year.
runners.comment: Reserve; dual winning pointer in 2023; out of form in in points this year.

races.race_name: Denvir's Of Downpatrick (Q.R.) Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Moonshine Girl
runners.spotlight: Winless from 18 starts (14 over hurdles); went closer than ever at Limerick over this trip (soft) last time when beaten just 1l in second under today's rider; chance.
runners.comment: 0-18 but went closer than ever at Limerick under today's ruder; chance

runners.name: Joshua Chamberlain
runners.spotlight: Easily won a Tramore handicap hurdle off 1lb higher in August 2023; well beaten back from over two and half years away at Punchestown; best watched.
runners.comment: Well beaten back from mammoth absence at Punchestown; best watched
runners.owner: J R Robinson & Mrs E Keane

runners.name: True Harmony
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner hadn't shown much under rules until a very positive second over 3m at Wexford last time (good to yielding); up 3lb but he should be thereabouts with a repeat of his last run.
runners.comment: Point winner ran best race under rules when second last time; chance with repeat

runners.name: Nothing Hectic
runners.spotlight: Won a Bellewstown maiden hurdle (good) over extended 2m6f last August; pulled up and beaten 20l in two handicap starts since then; may have needed latest, however, and may do better now; interesting jockey booking.
runners.comment: Won a m'den hurdle last Aug'; may have needed latest run and perhaps can do better

runners.name: Gangster Granny
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner added a first hurdling success when successful over C&D in May (good, 33-1) off 4lb lower; couldn't follow up in a similar contest here (good to yielding) last time when a 20l fifth.
runners.comment: Dual Flat winner added a hurdling success over C&D but couldn't follow up last time

runners.name: Georgia Celena
runners.spotlight: Off the mark over C&D last July (good), back from a break off 81 and good placed runs back here twice and at Down Royal; off since below her best over an extended 3m here in October (good to yielding); player if ready.
runners.comment: C&D winner in July and good placed runs after; player if ready

runners.name: Lady Fromentro
runners.spotlight: Best of three maiden runs came in a low-quality contest at Wexford last time, upped in trip to 2m4f and keeping on well to take the runner-up spot; goes handicapping now and lightly raced sort can't be discounted up in trip again.
runners.comment: Best m'den run came upped to 2m4f last time when second; not ruled out on h'cap bow

runners.name: Joya Del Mar
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; off the mark over hurdles at the 20th attempt over C&D last month; couldn't follow up at Sligo off 10lb higher mark next time
runners.comment: Reserve; scored at 20th attempt over C&D last month; struggled off revised mark latest
runners.owner: Mrs T Giles-Fahey & Miss S Mangan

runners.name: Little Boy Sivola
runners.spotlight: Hasn't troubled the judge in maidens or four handicaps; tailed off in two runs this year.
runners.comment: Hasn't troubled the judge in m'dens or four h'caps; tailed off in two runs this year

runners.name: Hey Babbs
runners.spotlight: Won a 2m4f Wexford handicap in July off this mark (good); lost her way since then until posting a promising third of 15 at Bellewstown in April (3m, yielding); cheekpieces are added to the tongue tie and she's a contender if backing up her latest run.
runners.comment: Winner off this mark last July; poor form until promising third last time; contender

runners.name: Another Glendaars
runners.spotlight: First reserve; just 5-1 at Limerick (2m4f, soft) two weeks ago when returning from a 530-day absence; tailed off on that occasion.
runners.comment: Reserve; 5-1 on return from a long absence at Limerick but tailed off

runners.name: Kofino
runners.spotlight: Last of 15 on handicap debut on heavy ground at Limerick and failed to complete back there last time; cheekpieces go on now.
runners.comment: Last on h'cap debut and failed to complete last time; cheekpieces on now

runners.name: Volez Vous
runners.spotlight: Won consecutive Clonmel handicap hurdles last year at 2m/2m3f on different ground; poor in starts over the winter and didn't improve for switch of yard when well beaten back at Clonmel last time.
runners.comment: Consecutive Clonmel winner last year but poor in recent starts inc' on stable debut latest

runners.name: Road To The Sea
runners.spotlight: Struggling when fell two out on Sligo handicap debut (2m2f, good); up in trip now but others more likely.
runners.comment: Struggling when fell on h'cap bow; up in trip now but others more likely

runners.name: Antoine De Paris
runners.spotlight: Won a Flat claimer last summer and holds a rating of 63 in that sphere; huge prices and never involved in four hurdle outings; worth a market check on handicap bow.
runners.comment: Flat winner has not been involved in four hurdle outings; worth a market check

runners.name: Sacramental
runners.spotlight: Has shown very little in four maidens; first handicap start and been off since January.
runners.comment: Has shown very little in four maidens; first handicap start and been off since January

runners.name: George's Angel
runners.spotlight: Didn't show much in a bumper and three maiden hurdles; makes handicap bow now.
runners.comment: Didn't show much in a bumper and three maiden hurdles; makes handicap bow now

races.race_name: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Chase

runners.name: Truckers Cruising
runners.spotlight: Point winner got off the mark under rules at Ayr (2m1f, heavy) in December when an easy winner over fences off 7lb lower; beaten when unseated at Hexham next time and could only manage sixth at Limerick in December (2m2f, soft to heavy); beaten 30l over hurdles latest; others more likely.
runners.comment: Won at Ayr off 7lb lower in Dec'; moderate form since inc' when beaten 30l over hurdles
runners.owner: Ed Mulligan & John Finnegan & Aileen Saunders & Ann Holton

runners.name: Doyen Magic
runners.spotlight: Won a Kilbeggan handicap chase (2m4f, good to yielding) in July last year off 87; runner-up on heavy ground at Clonmel and Limerick at 2m/2m2f before a lesser effort back at Limerick last time (2m4f, good to yielding); capable of better.
runners.comment: Won at Kilbeggan off 87 last summer; second twice on hvy but lesser effort last time

runners.name: Leave In Secret
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdles winner last season at Galway and Ballinrobe on goodish ground; creditable efforts in opening two chase runs but tailed off on handicap bow at Navan when last seen in December; may need this return.
runners.comment: Dual hurdles winner last season; creditable opening chase runs but tailed off on h'cap bow

runners.name: Lake Chad
runners.spotlight: In and out form this year; good second at Thurles (2m6f, soft) before tailed off at Wexford (2m4f, soft); continued wonderful Tramore course form when easily winning over 2m6f (good to yielding) in April, adding his fifth chase win (four at Tramore); beaten 31l off this mark back at favoured track last time.
runners.comment: In and out form this year; easy winner at favoured Tramore before 31l behind there latest

runners.name: Magic Day
runners.spotlight: Took runner-up spot in only point; won a Kilbeggan mares' handicap hurdle in April last year (2m4f, yielding) off 108; ran poorly on chasing debut at Wexford in October but was better at Tramore back over fences last time when third (2m6f, good to yielding); didn't jump with fluency that day and three horses in front fell at the second last.
runners.comment: Hurdles winner off 108 last year; okay effort in second beginners' last time

runners.name: Andys Flame
runners.spotlight: Point and hurdles winner added a chase success at Kelso off 113 in April last year (2m4f, good); well beaten next time at Cartmel; returned from almost a year off when tailed off at Kilbeggan last time; entitled to improve over that.
runners.comment: Added chase win last year; entitled to improve over poor comeback run

runners.name: Dorking Cock
runners.spotlight: 3-20 over fences but no win of any description since November 2023; soundly beaten in a hunter chase here in May (3m5f, good to yielding); others make more appeal.
runners.comment: 3-20 over fences but no win of any description since 2023; others more appealing

runners.name: Listentillitellyea
runners.spotlight: Has won over hurdles but is 0-10 over fences; good ground given as a possible excuse when tailed off at Ballinrobe back from an absence; same excuse given when tailed off in a Punchestown handicap last time (2m, good); down 4lb but others preferred.
runners.comment: Winner over hurdles is 0-10 over fences and poor in two runs this term

runners.name: Freddie Robdal
runners.spotlight: Three-time chase winner went close over hurdles twice over 2m at Thurles in November on different ground; no impression at Leopardstown over Christmas; unseen since pulled up back over fences at Thurles in February; has won following a similar break before but never from a mark this high, albeit rider's claim helps; trainer also runs Magic Day.
runners.comment: Three-time chase winner was PU when last seen in Feb' but has won after break before

runners.name: Parkgarve
runners.spotlight: 0-10 over hurdles but has gone closest at this track; weakened into sixth of 11 on chasing debut here in September (2m2f, yielding); similar effort back over fences at Tramore last time (2m6f, good); makes handicap chase debut now; others more likely.
runners.comment: 0-10 over hurdles but gone closest at this track; middling in two beginners' inc' here

runners.name: Driveonwill
runners.spotlight: Gained a first career win when successful in a C&D beginners' chase last time (good to yielding); rated 11lb higher than his hurdles mark following that but claimer reduces that burden; has his chance but overall form suggests following up won't be easy.
runners.comment: Gained first career win when successful in C&D beginners'; following up won't be easy

runners.name: Finnians Row
runners.spotlight: C&D victory came from the blue after being pulled up in his previous two starts; blinkers (retained) replaced the visor that day; four-time chase winner is up 7lb, has never won off a mark this high but his win was impressive and he was consistent last term.
runners.comment: Surprise C&D victor last time; has never won off mark this high but win was impressive

runners.name: King's Son
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in sole point; well held in three handicap hurdle runs, including when tailed off at Cork in April; down 4lb for chase bow; others preferred.
runners.comment: Runner-up in sole point but poor in three h'cap hurdle runs; down 4lb for chase bow

runners.name: Ringdufferin
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdles winner for Gordon Elliott in 2023; out of form for former yard but there were positives to take from fourth of 12 at Tramore in April (2m6f, yielding); didn't back that up back over hurdles at Kilbeggan; a bit hard to trust now.
runners.comment: Positives in his stable debut over fences but didn't back it up over hurdles latest

races.race_name: C. Russell Auto Sales Beginners Chase

runners.name: Huntsgrove
runners.spotlight: Has beaten precious few rivals in four chase starts; failed to complete at Tramore last month when badly hampered at the first (2m6f, good); others preferred.
runners.comment: Has beaten precious few rivals in four chases; PU after hampered last time

runners.name: Coppola
runners.spotlight: Maiden was a runner-up over hurdles in opening three starts since switching to current yard; always behind in first-time cheekpieces (now left off) at Kilbeggan last time; goes chasing now but record of 0-8 in point-to-points suggests it may not transform her; rated 85 over hurdles.
runners.comment: Three-time runner-up over hurdles since switch to this yard; 0-8 record in points

runners.name: Saunton Surf
runners.spotlight: Debut point and bumper winner was 0-13 over hurdles; sent off favourite for chasing debut here over an extended 2m3f when headed in the final strides; big chance once again up in trip.
runners.comment: Debut point/bumper winner was well backed on chase debut here when headed in final strides

runners.name: Tropical Image
runners.spotlight: Sole career win came at Listowel over hurdles in September (2m6f, good to yielding); okay chase debut at Killarney when sixth of 14 (2m5f, good to yielding) and improved on that when beaten just half a length back at Listowel last time (2m3f, yielding), outpaced before keeping on well; this trip looks more suitable and she's a player.
runners.comment: Listowel hurdles winner was just 0.5l behind on second chase start back there last time

runners.name: Kalsman
runners.spotlight: Added second hurdles win when successful at Down Royal in April (2m4f, good); not at the same level in two starts since; holds a hurdles rating of 99 heading into this chasing debut.
runners.comment: Added second hurdles win in April but not at same level in two runs since; chasing debut

runners.name: Coolboy Christy
runners.spotlight: Some decent runs in defeat over hurdles recently before sent chasing over an extended 2m3f here last time when well beaten behind Saunton Surf, albeit keeping on; more needed.
runners.comment: Well beaten behind Saunton Surf over 2m3f here last time; more needed

runners.name: Welcome Back
runners.spotlight: Ran well in a Navan maiden hurdle in 2024 and winner of his sole point next time; hasn't matched those efforts since, although has had an interrupted career; perhaps chasing can spark a return to form; rated 100 over hurdles.
runners.comment: Good early-career form inc' point win; below that level recently; maybe chasing can spark

runners.name: Schiehallion
runners.spotlight: 0-19 at the age of nine; broke poor run of form back from a break when runner-up over 3m at Bellewstown in April (yielding); pulled up at Punchestown next time; tries chasing now but overall form reduces appeal; rated 94 over hurdles.
runners.comment: 0-19; broke poor run of form when second in April but PU next time; goes chasing now

runners.name: Anodandawink
runners.spotlight: Pulled up and distant runner-up in two point-to-points; best effort over hurdles came on second handicap start at Kilbeggan in April when third (3m1f, good to yielding); sent off favourite there last time back to 2m3f but was well beaten; sent chasing now.
runners.comment: Point form just okay; third on second h'cap hurdle start but couldn't back it up last time

races.race_name: Kevin Ross Bloodstock Handicap Chase

runners.name: Toor Moon
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden has been poor on recent starts over hurdles and fences so isn't easy to fancy.
runners.comment: Longstanding maiden has been poor on recent starts and isn't easy to fancy

runners.name: Malton Groove
runners.spotlight: Maiden in all spheres after 31 starts; has been well beaten on recent starts and isn't easy to fancy on that basis.
runners.comment: Maiden has been well beaten on recent starts and isn't easy to fancy on that basis

runners.name: Thehairyfella
runners.spotlight: Sole win came over C&D last year off 3lb lower; was hit and miss since but can improve from recent comeback at Cartmel when beaten 15l; a player.
runners.comment: Sole win came over C&D last year off 3lb lower; can improve from recent comeback

runners.name: Jimli's Cave
runners.spotlight: Sole hurdles win came at this track in October 2024 off 4lb higher; was well beaten back over fences here last time and has to do much better.
runners.comment: Sole hurdles win came at this track; was well beaten back over fences here last time

runners.name: U Asking Me
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way on return over hurdles last time; however, has a shout on his best chase runs last year (twice third); blinkers replace cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way on return over hurdles last time; has a shout on best chase runs

runners.name: Miss Lia
runners.spotlight: Hurdle winner has run well in defeat at this venue, notably when second in March; poor at Wexford last time; decent run on sole chase start last year; needs best.
runners.comment: Hurdle winner has run well in defeat at this venue; poor last time; second chase start
runners.owner: Sussex By The Sea Syndicate/David L'Estrange

runners.name: Big Island
runners.spotlight: Hurdle winner (1-15) is 0-9 chasing but has run some solid races in defeat; ran well enough at Tramore last time on first run for 285 days; considered with improvement to come.
runners.comment: Hurdle winner is 0-9 chasing but ran well enough at Tramore last time; considered

runners.name: Doyen Wolf
runners.spotlight: Maiden has only had seven runs in an interrupted career; was 3rd over hurdles at Sligo and can be involved if repeating that on chase bow.
runners.comment: Maiden was 3rd over hurdles at Sligo and can be involved if repeating that on chase bow

runners.name: The Rebel County
runners.spotlight: Hunter chase winner at Tipperary last year has been hit and miss since; beaten 18l in a Tramore handicap last time with cheekpieces added; has to do more.
runners.comment: Hunter chase winner last year has been hit and miss since; beaten 18l at Tramore last time

runners.name: Ferdia
runners.spotlight: Mostly poor since returning from a huge absence last winter but a bit better at Tramore last time; others preferred.
runners.comment: Mostly poor since return last winter but a bit better at Tramore last time

runners.name: Fr Gilligansvoyge
runners.spotlight: Record of just 1-43 over fences but placed lots of times; decent efforts last twice at Limerick and Tramore; needs more from 3lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Just 1-43 over fences but decent efforts last twice; needs more from 3lb out of the h'cap

runners.name: Shantou Show
runners.spotlight: Dual hunter chase winner in 2023 has been regressive since last autumn but this better ground is a help and he could produce an improved performance.
runners.comment: Dual hunter chase winner has been regressive since last autumn; this better ground a help

runners.name: Sampoet
runners.spotlight: Point winner has run well at times in defeat over fences; not a bad hurdle run four days ago at Sligo; needs to get back to his best returned to fences.
runners.comment: Point winner has run well at times over fences and not a bad hurdle run last time

races.race_name: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP INH Flat Race

runners.name: Hurricane Leaf
runners.spotlight: Respectable effort on debut at Listowel last year but was tailed-off at Sligo on return; has to resume progress in a major way.
runners.comment: Tailed-off at Sligo on return; has to resume progress in a major way

runners.name: Katie Can't Count
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-sister to bumper, 2m Flat (RPR 87) and useful 2m2f-3m hurdle winner My Sister Sarah; dam placed French 2m1f hurdle, half-sister to smart 2m-2m5f hurdle/chase winner J'Y Vole; well-bred mare is a late-starter but has to be respected given her connections, especially if strong in the market.
runners.comment: Debutante has to be respected given her connections, especially if strong in the market

runners.name: Duck Hunter
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to several useful jumps winners; pulled-up on recent point start but started favourite that day so better was expected; not one to rule out on that basis.
runners.comment: Pulled-up on point start but was fav' that day so better was expected; not one to rule out

runners.name: Flashy Fred
runners.spotlight: Ran respectably on point debut beaten 9l by an easy winner who made 100,000euros since; is by a quality sire and related to plenty of jumps winners so one to consider; trainer is 3-8 in bumpers.
runners.comment: Beaten 9l on point debut by an easy winner; related to plenty of jumps winners; considered

runners.name: Mevoila A Vassy
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; half-brother to French 2m4f hurdle winner Peace Garden; dam well held 1m1f/1m2f in France, half-sister to smart 2m hurdle/chase winner Peace And Co; has a decent French pedigree and is worth a market check on debut.
runners.comment: Has a decent French pedigree and is worth a market check on debut

runners.name: Mule Skinner
runners.spotlight: Beaten 7l on point debut in March but was 31l behind next time the following month; is by a top sire and cost 42,000euros as a 3yo so is likely capable of running well.
runners.comment: Best of two point starts was on debut; is by a top sire so likely capable of running well

runners.name: Ruled By The Sun
runners.spotlight: Tailed-off in testing ground on debut at Limerick; will have to improve hugely for this livelier surface with a tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Tailed-off in testing ground on debut; has to improve hugely for this livelier surface

runners.name: Corway
runners.spotlight: Ran respectably on debut in a Kilbeggan bumper over 2m; the tongue-tie worn then is retained; comes from a speedy Flat damline so no guarantee this longer trip will suit but a player if he stays.
runners.comment: Ran respectably on debut in a Kilbeggan bumper over 2m and is a player if he stays

races.race_name: Andy Calvert Celebration Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Malangen
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D in 2024 from a mark 13lb higher and has steadily been returning to form this season; made much of the running when a solid second over 2m4f here last week and with Jack Stenhouse able to claim his full allowance another bold bid looks likely back at 2m.
runners.comment: C&D winner off 13lb higher in 2024; good second here last week and respected back at 2m
runners.owner: T Hunter & D A Harrison Racing

runners.name: Character Testing
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after 16 starts over hurdles, though he has plenty of placed form and looked set to score before being caught close home in a Cartmel seller (2m1f, good) 17 days ago; requires more back in handicap company but has solid place claims.
runners.comment: 16-race maiden but consistent placer; caught late in seller latest and has place claims

runners.name: Newport
runners.spotlight: His sole win from 22 hurdle starts came at Musselburgh (2m, soft) in December but he has otherwise been a reliable performer and posted several solid efforts after wind surgery in January; has to be forgiven a below-par run at Kelso last time, where he weakened quickly, but could have a part to play.
runners.comment: 2m hurdle winner and generally reliable; could bounce back from a below-par run latest

runners.name: National Question
runners.spotlight: Won three times last season (2m1f-2m4f), most recently at Carlisle on good to soft ground in March; although only sixth off this mark over 2m4f last month he was not beaten far and remains one to consider.
runners.comment: Three wins last season; fair sixth latest and shortlisted if avoiding soft ground

runners.name: Snapaudaciaheros
runners.spotlight: Benefited from the drop back to 2m and first-time cheekpieces when scoring over C&D in April but never travelled with the same zest when returned here the following month; soft ground a possible excuse that day and she is not without claims if rediscovering that earlier form.
runners.comment: C&D winner in April in first-time cheekpieces and soft ground may excuse latest effort

runners.name: Les's Legacy
runners.spotlight: Successful over 2m4f here nearly a year ago but has struggled to recapture that form since; shaped with a little more promise when fading into fourth over 2m5f at Kelso last month, but he'll need to build on that and this drop back to 2m may not play to his strengths.
runners.comment: Won over 2m4f here last year but regressed; better effort latest but drop to 2m a worry

runners.name: Zephlyn
runners.spotlight: Below par on the AW this year and again failed to fire when last of five on the Flat at Carlisle (2m, good to firm) two weeks ago; however, his earlier fourth on handicap hurdle debut at Perth (2m4f, good) was more encouraging and gives him a chance of making the frame now down in trip.
runners.comment: Below par on AW and Flat latest but earlier handicap hurdle fourth at Perth offers hope

runners.name: Bobby Socks
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 2m4f in 2023 for Olly Murphy but his career has been punctuated by lengthy absences since; shaped as though retaining ability when returning from a break in November 2024 and again in two subsequent starts after a second wind operation, but absent again since February 2025; makes stable debut and not ruled out if fully tuned up.
runners.comment: Dual 2m4f winner in 2023; long absences since but hard to dismiss if retaining ability
runners.owner: Ursa Major, D Mullen And Partners

runners.name: Curious Mrs Fox
runners.spotlight: Placed on her first two starts over hurdles at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft) and Market Rasen (2m, good) but failed to build on that when well beaten on handicap debut at Fakenham last month, where the 2m4f may have stretched her stamina; a 3lb drop helps, but she still has something to prove.
runners.comment: Placed twice over hurdles; disappointing on handicap debut and has questions to answer

runners.name: Stay In The Game
runners.spotlight: Opened his account at the seventh attempt over hurdles in a match at Huntingdon (2m, good to firm) last month and bettered that effort when a close third over C&D seven days ago off the same mark; Paddy Hanlon's 5lb claim is unavailable here, but another effort of that calibre would put him firmly in the mix.
runners.comment: Won at Huntingdon and then a close C&D third last week; firmly in the mix again

races.race_name: Clean Sweep Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Obsessedwithyou
runners.spotlight: Four-time chase winner and rated higher in that sphere; however, she is 2-14 over hurdles and has been found a good opportunity to add another success as she goes unpenalised for her wins in junior hurdles; the one to beat on these terms.
runners.comment: Better over fences than hurdles but has a very good chance on these terms.
runners.owner: Awr Consultancy Ltd & The Folly Partners

runners.name: Nobotheratall
runners.spotlight: Carlisle bumper winner in October who followed up when making all to land a Kelso novice hurdle (2m, good) last month at the third attempt in this sphere; had things his own way that day but still commands respect under a penalty in a race lacking depth.
runners.comment: Won Kelso novice hurdle at third attempt, making all, and respected under penalty.

runners.name: Autumn Getaway
runners.spotlight: Has offered little in two bumper runs and both starts over hurdles, all at three-figure odds.
runners.comment: Has offered little in two bumper runs and both starts over hurdles, all at big odds.
runners.owner: Mrs K Elliott & Mrs E J Quinlan

runners.name: All About Moi
runners.spotlight: Has shown ability in two of his three bumper starts, most recently when a staying-on fourth at Warwick (2m, good) in October; makes both hurdling and stable debut and is likely to improve with time.
runners.comment: Shown ability in bumpers, staying-on fourth last time; makes hurdle/stable debut.

runners.name: Ribble River
runners.spotlight: Three-time Flat winner at 1m3f-1m4f but has yet to replicate that form over hurdles, being soundly beaten in both completed starts, on good ground; may do better in time but can only be watched at present.
runners.comment: Three-time Flat winner but yet to translate that to hurdles; best watched at present.
runners.owner: Hambleton Racing Mrs C Clark Miss A Lee

runners.name: Scopic
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in a Sedgefield bumper on debut in April (2m1f, good) and fared no better when beaten 54l by Nobotheratall on last month's hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Well beaten in bumper and no better on hurdle debut

runners.name: Steps In Time
runners.spotlight: Fair Flat handicapper up to 1m2f for David O'Meara; 150-1 and beaten 30l on hurdle/stable debut at Kelso (2m, good) last month, though that run may have been needed after 301 days off.
runners.comment: Fair Flat handicapper but well beaten on hurdle/stable debut after long absence.

runners.name: Colinski
runners.spotlight: Modest Flat winner who failed to last home over C&D on hurdle debut and unseated mid-race at Cartmel 17 days ago; should do better in time but others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Flat winner who isn't particularly solid on his hurdles efforts

runners.name: Mr Ramoon
runners.spotlight: Shown some ability in three Irish points but was beaten 32l on hurdle debut at Newcastle in January (extended 2m4f, good to soft); drops further in trip after a break but likely one for further down the line.
runners.comment: Shown point ability but well beaten on hurdle debut; drop in trip unlikely to help.

races.race_name: Tileform Handicap Chase

runners.name: Divas Doyen
runners.spotlight: Just 2-26 over fences, but has struck here before and has stamina to spare as a 2m4f winner; back below her last winning mark and forgiven her latest effort (badly hampered by faller); prospects in a weak race.
runners.comment: Forgiven latest effort (hampered); below last winning mark and has stamina to spare.

runners.name: Ghasham
runners.spotlight: Encouraging start to chasing career, keeping on behind a subsequent winner over C&D before finishing closer still at Cartmel latest (2m1f, good); this would be no stronger than either race, and mark still unchanged.
runners.comment: Still on same mark as for second places on each chase start so far (one C&D); chance.

runners.name: Chicago Storm
runners.spotlight: Two handicap hurdle wins for Daisy Hitchins last year (1m7f/2m3f, good to soft) included one off 8lb higher, and an inconsistent profile since the autumn does at least include a Tapeta Flat success and a serviceable chase debut; some positives ahead of stable debut.
runners.comment: Dual handicap hurdle winner in 2025; a bit less consistent latterly; stable debut.

runners.name: Mr Globalist
runners.spotlight: 0-38 across both codes, not for the first time doing too much in front when a weakening fourth in a Cartmel selling hurdle latest; untried over fences, and unseated on sole pointing start (Flat bred).
runners.comment: Did too much in front in Cartmel selling hurdle latest and now 0-38 overall; chase debut.

runners.name: Bentley Road
runners.spotlight: First ever drop to 2m looks worth a try, having not lasted home in two 3m hunter chases latterly (one here); risks attached, however, having run out twice in points (ahead when exited at the last over 2m4f in March), and may have competition if intent on front-running again.
runners.comment: Drop to 2m looks worth a try; may have competition for the lead; has run out twice.

runners.name: Cross Of Redmoor
runners.spotlight: One second place from nine otherwise mostly poor efforts in Irish points, and operating below a winning level over hurdles back in the autumn; limited appeal on chase debut from 7lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: Mostly poor efforts in Irish points; below a winning level over hurdles last autumn.

races.race_name: Lisa Bond Celebration Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Johnson's Blue
runners.spotlight: On the face of it work on to reverse recent C&D form with Perseus Way and Sunset Hill despite a swing in the weights, but course record prior to that read four wins and (off 8lb higher) a short head second in this race last year ahead of two reopposing rivals; respected.
runners.comment: Record of 111123 here, albeit behind two reopposing rivals latest; respected again.

runners.name: Well Educated
runners.spotlight: A head away from securing a fifth course win here last Saturday (2m); can number two C&D successes among that haul and respected again, for all that his record in the past three editions of this race reads 655.
runners.comment: Almost gained another course win here last weekend (2m); record in this race reads 655.

runners.name: Passing Diamond
runners.spotlight: Return to forcing tactics has reaped dividends, both recent course wins (2m/2m4f, good) gained making all; starts handicap life in a higher grade than he absolutely has to, and the early lead will likely be contested, but his mark isn't punitive by any means; remains of interest.
runners.comment: Made all twice here last month; pace rivalry awaits, but initial mark looks workable.

runners.name: Perseus Way
runners.spotlight: 5lb higher mark than for last month's soft-ground C&D victory over Sunset Hill and Johnson's Blue still reads favourably next to his 2022-23 peak RPRs; Flat scorer at Hamilton since (1m5f, good), and plainly on fine terms with himself again; player.
runners.comment: Beat Sunset Hill and Johnson's Blue over C&D (soft) before Flat win latest; thriving.
runners.owner: J Monkhouse R De Robeck A Miller

runners.name: Miss Maverick
runners.spotlight: Only win away from Uttoxeter came over today's C&D in May 2025, and she was a good third in this race soon after (split Johnson's Blue and Well Educated) despite pulling; well held off career-high mark latest.
runners.comment: Good third in this last year and has won over C&D; well held off career-high mark latest.

runners.name: Imperial Data
runners.spotlight: Happiest hunting ground is Cartmel, but a record of 225244P around today's C&D includes a neck second in this race in 2024 off 5lb lower, with Well Educated 23l behind; miles off his best in one start apiece at each of these venues so far this term, however.
runners.comment: Almost won this in 2024 off 5lb lower; well below par on both starts this term (one C&D).

runners.name: Sunset Hill
runners.spotlight: Couldn't hold on having been committed some way out on each recent C&D start, though split Perseus Way and Johnson's Blue in the first (soft) and recorded her best ever good-ground form in the latest; thereabouts.
runners.comment: Back to form if unable to hold on in either recent C&D start (soft, good); thereabouts.

runners.name: Cannock Park
runners.spotlight: One-time Formby Hurdle third who didn't kick on switched to fences; forfeited all chance at the start in a debuted hood (discarded) on sole sighting since December 2024, and present ability level conjectural.
runners.comment: Lost all chance at start on only run since late 2024; present ability level conjectural.

races.race_name: drainrat.co.uk Rat Entry Diagnosis Specialist Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Divilabother
runners.spotlight: C&D winner on stable debut in August and has shown fair form since, though he needs to be forgiven a below-par effort here last time over 2m4f; possible danger if returning to form now back up in trip.
runners.comment: C&D winner; fair form since but below par last time and needs to bounce back.

runners.name: Campaign Trail
runners.spotlight: Has improved with each run since returning from a break and stepping up in trip, with cheekpieces added to a tongue-tie; made all at Hereford (3m1f, good) last month and a 6lb rise looks manageable; leading contender.
runners.comment: Progressive since return; made all at Hereford and a 6lb rise looks manageable; player.

runners.name: Lets Mingle
runners.spotlight: 2m4f maiden hurdle winner at Perth (good) just over a year ago and has held his form respectably in handicaps since without looking especially well treated; faded late on his only previous attempt at 3m under rules, though he was a point winner at the trip on soft ground and has place claims.
runners.comment: Point and 2m4f maiden hurdle winner who's been consistent in handicaps since; respected.

runners.name: Supreme Yeats
runners.spotlight: Veteran who returned to form when landing a gamble at Newton Abbot (good; cheekpieces added) last month and followed up there 21 days later off a 6lb higher mark; now up to a career-high mark off a further 5lb rise but remains unexposed as a stayer.
runners.comment: Veteran back to form with Newton Abbot double; up another 5lb but unexposed as a stayer.
runners.owner: The Old Stag Racing Sy Partnership

runners.name: Beny Nahar Road
runners.spotlight: 0-9 over hurdles and has the stronger form over fences but is accordingly handicapped and didn't run badly back over hurdles last month, where lack of a recent run told late; could be involved with that outing behind him.
runners.comment: 0-9 over hurdles and stronger over fences, but not disgraced back from a break last month.

runners.name: Wee Alki
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D hurdle winner, including last month, and not disgraced at Cartmel 17 days ago off this mark; return here is a plus, but he is still to win off a mark this high.
runners.comment: Dual C&D hurdle winner, including last month; fair Cartmel run and return here a plus.

runners.name: Billy Boi Blue
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdles winner, most recently at Ayr (3m, good to soft) in April last year, but pulled up twice since returning from a break this spring and again ran below form when fifth of six, beaten 21l, at Huntingdon last month; best watched for now.
runners.comment: Three-time hurdles winner but out of form since return; best watched for now.

runners.name: Pinot Rouge
runners.spotlight: Below par since last June's C&D win off 5lb higher until a better effort when fourth in an Ayr (3m, heavy) Class 2 in April, but a heavy defeat since leaves him with plenty to prove.
runners.comment: C&D winner off 5lb higher but mostly below par since; plenty to prove.

races.race_name: Stan Hayhurst Memorial Handicap Chase

runners.name: Killybegs Jet Lady
runners.spotlight: Had two of these behind (including Passengerontheship, one of three next-time winners in the race) when second over C&D in early May (good); easy Kelso success since was the form equal of that effort, if too full of early errors for maximum comfort; career-high mark now.
runners.comment: Ahead of Passengerontheship when second here in May (C&D); won despite errors latest.

runners.name: Dr Shirocco
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-17 around Hexham includes one C&D chase win two years ago (good); hasn't hit the line strongly on any of his three most recent visits, losing second to Killybegs Jet Lady in the middle one of these, and surprising if nothing is going better at the end here.
runners.comment: Two years since C&D win here; third behind Killybegs Jet Lady (second) here two runs ago.

runners.name: A Year Older
runners.spotlight: Better round of jumping in taking a 2m6f Limerick handicap last month (good), and his subsequent Punchestown second off 5lb higher (3m1f, good to yielding) is stronger form still; more on here off topweight but retains lowish mileage for his age; chance.
runners.comment: Limerick win (2m6f) and Punchestown second (3m1f) latterly; still lowish mileage.

runners.name: Passengerontheship
runners.spotlight: Two recent 2m4f wins here (good) owed to the return to positive tactics down in trip; does stay further, though didn't when pulling up behind Killybegs Jet Lady and Dr Shirocco over C&D early last month.
runners.comment: Won last two, both over 2m4f here; pulled up behind two of these over C&D beforehand.

runners.name: She Won't Mind
runners.spotlight: 2m4f maiden point winner for James Owen three years ago, but recent efforts have reaffirmed that staying that and longer trips is otherwise often beyond her; 3lb drop is not persuasive; tongue tie retained.
runners.comment: 2m4f point winner in 2023, but struggling to stay that trip or further since; down 3lb.

runners.name: Croagh Patrick
runners.spotlight: Winner here in the past, and obviously weighted to strike again off his new career-low mark; just 3-38 over fences, however, and recent return after 14 months off (stable debut) wasn't majorly encouraging.
runners.comment: Career-low mark at his disposal, but chasing strike-rate only 3-38; poor recent return.

runners.name: The Kings Soldier
runners.spotlight: Faring better since the switch to handicap hurdles, if again shaping as if the sharp course was against him when 12l third at Fakenham latest (3m, good); ought to find Hexham a better suit switching to fences, and one half-brother made a winning chase debut.
runners.comment: Sharp course against him at Fakenham latest; more in his favour for this chasing debut.

races.race_name: Raymond Forster 70th Birthday Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 2 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Loman Lady
runners.spotlight: Dam won a 3m point here and has three bumper scorers among her siblings; appreciated either or both a four-month break and better ground when 4l second on last month's Irish point return (good to yielding), and since purchased for £10,000; wouldn't need to be a world-beater to oblige here.
runners.comment: Improvement when second on final Irish point start; dam won a point here; player.

runners.name: Getaway Tobin
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to Monkfish; shaped with more purpose second time out when a staying-on 7l fourth at Kilbeggan latest (1m7f, good to yielding); greater emphasis on stamina round here could see her upgrade again.
runners.comment: Stayed on from 2f out when fourth at Killarney latest; stiffer track could well suit.

runners.name: Author Dora
runners.spotlight: Authorized mare from an immediate family of Turkish Flat winners from 5f-9.5f; yard picks up occasional winners in this sphere, and starts this one off at a realistic level.
runners.comment: Immediate family of Turkish Flat winners from 5f-9.5f; starts off at a realistic level.

runners.name: John Wemmick
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal of a Brazilian 1,000 Guineas/Oaks winner who has already thrown several 6f-1m2f scorers, two of them useful; yard's first National Hunt runner since 2020-21.
runners.comment: Half-sibling to 6f-1m2f scorers, two useful; yard's last National Hunt runner in 2020-21.

runners.name: Finjimk
runners.spotlight: Unlikely the best will be seen of him under rules until encountering a trip and obstacles, but neither recent Irish point start was especially encouraging; yard 0-6 with its British raiders.
runners.comment: Didn't complete either Irish point start this spring; yard 0-6 with its British raiders.

runners.name: River Ore
runners.spotlight: Fair marker laid on last month's Killarney debut (2m1f, good to yielding); 19l sixth of six at Plumpton since owed in part to a lengthy battle for the lead, and there's possible competition in that regard here too.
runners.comment: Chance on form of Killarney debut fourth; contesting the lead compromised him latest.

runners.name: Ebchester
runners.spotlight: 12-1, refusal to settle caught him up when a weakening fourth on last month's Kelso debut (2m, good); may upgrade if helping himself more.
runners.comment: Refusal to settle caught him up when fourth on Kelso debut (2m, good); may do better.

runners.name: Budworth Boy
runners.spotlight: 20-1, made most but ultimately didn't last out on April's Kelso bumper debut (2m, good), beaten 19l; will need to tease out resources longer over this more exacting 2m.
runners.comment: Made most before weakening into a 19l fourth on Kelso debut; a stiffer 2m awaits.

races.race_name: Frasers Plus Designer Outlet Swindon Apprentice Handicap (Bath Summer Stayers' Series Qualifier)

runners.name: The Craftymaster
runners.spotlight: Added another two AW wins to his haul this winter; rated 7lb lower on turf but with some justification given his record on grass, including a heavy C&D defeat in April; not solid.
runners.comment: Would be a red-hot favourite were this run on AW but his turf record lags well behind
runners.owner: R Miles, V Miles, T Stamp & Partner

runners.name: Sapphire Sirocco
runners.spotlight: Won a 2m AW handicap for Pat Phelan in November; similar form when third at Chepstow on her recent stable debut (1m4f, good to firm); leading contender back up in distance.
runners.comment: 2m AW win in November; third (1m4f) on stable debut last month; contender back up in trip

runners.name: Man Of The Sea
runners.spotlight: Veteran whose two 2025 wins were at this track over 1m6f and 1m5f; not at his best so far this year and while this ease in grade will help, he wouldn't want the ground to be too slow.
runners.comment: Two course wins last year; not shone yet in 2026 and could do with the ground drying out

runners.name: Everest
runners.spotlight: Three good runs at Lingfield this year, including a 1m4f classified win; not at her best here three weeks ago (1m6f, good to firm) but this is weak and she is one to consider.
runners.comment: Not at her best here last time (1m6f) but this year's AW form brings her right into it
runners.owner: Alan Jamieson Site Services & G L Moore

runners.name: Corsican Caper
runners.spotlight: Five-time Flat winner; ended losing run when winning a small-field novice handicap hurdle three weeks ago; has the ability to play a big part but breaking on terms is an issue for him.
runners.comment: Slow-starter; hurdles win three weeks ago confirmed his wellbeing; has ability to feature

runners.name: Mind Hunter
runners.spotlight: Modest form in two minor handicap hurdles this year; not easily recommended back on the Flat.
runners.comment: Modest hurdle form this year; easy enough to swerve back on the Flat

runners.name: Lamlash
runners.spotlight: Poor form on the Flat and over hurdles; new trip not enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Poor form on the Flat and over hurdles; new trip not enough to tempt
runners.owner: H Maye And The Joe Tickle Racing Club

runners.name: Nora Barnacle
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in all eight starts (6f-13.3f), recording a peak RPR of 43; the new, longer trip needs to spark serious improvement.
runners.comment: Unplaced in all eight starts (6f-13.3f), recording a peak RPR of 43

runners.name: Emperor Dream
runners.spotlight: He has not offered much on the mainland but he did finish third in Jersey (1m5f, good) when last seen in April 2025; extreme stamina test worth a try but the absence is offputting.
runners.comment: Finished 3rd in Jersey (1m5f, good) when last seen 418 days ago; new trip worth exploring

races.race_name: Designer Outlet Style Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Havana Sprite
runners.spotlight: Promising start to her career when fourth of eight at Newmarket last month; beaten at odds-on at Yarmouth 17 days later but the winner could be useful and it was still a good effort; sets a good standard for the newcomers to aim at.
runners.comment: Two good runs last month and she sets a good standard for the newcomers to aim at

runners.name: Naval Cop
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-sister to 5f/6f winner Equity Law (including 2yo; RPR 91); dam 5f winner (RPR 83), half-sister to useful winners Desert Cop (5f/6f), Desert Law (5f/6f including 2yo) and Top Cop (5.7f/6f), out of 5f 2yo Listed winner; stable's 2yo runners at this track have been profitable to back down the years and she is an intriguing newcomer.
runners.comment: Bred for speed and yard's 2yos at this track have been profitable to back down the years

runners.name: Fillipas
runners.spotlight: £56,000 yearling; third foal; half-sister to winners Solar Aclaim (5f/6f including 2yo; RPR 97) and Nacho Nacho Nacho (7f AW; 71); dam placed 6f (71), half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 3 winner Alsindi; stable 1-11 with 2yos this year, the winner having had the benefit of a run.
runners.comment: £56,000 yearling; half-sister to two winners; stable's 2yos usually better for a run

runners.name: Toolatetoeducate
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-sister to 5f AW/5.7f turf winner Toolatetonegotiate (RPR 85); dam 5f/5.7f winner (including AW; 65), half-sister to winners Pick A Little (5f-1m) and Sartori (5f/6f); likely to benefit from this initial experience.
runners.comment: Half-sister to winning sprinter Toolatetonegotiate (RPR 85); longer-term prospect

runners.name: Marralyn
runners.spotlight: Her second run (6f, good to soft) was better than her debut but she will need another good jolt of improvement to take this; stable won the corresponding race 12 months ago.
runners.comment: Second run was better than her debut but she'll need another big leap forward to win

races.race_name: Pockets Townhouse Bath Handicap (Bath Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Legendsoftheland
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark in Wolverhampton classified event (6f) in March and bettered that form with victory in 13-runner handicap here (5.7f) 22 days ago; up 4lb but he can go well again.
runners.comment: Advanced his form with win over 5.7f here 22 days ago; up 4lb but he can go well again

runners.name: Son Of Astar
runners.spotlight: Scored three times (including over C&D) in 2025; back to his best in refitted blinkers with head second of nine in 5f Chepstow handicap a week ago; up 3lb but holds very good claims with talented 3lb claimer Jack Callan up.
runners.comment: C&D scorer; resurgent Chepstow second week ago; very good claims with Jack Callan up

runners.name: Darkened Edge
runners.spotlight: C&D scorer who resumed from eight months off with a solid third of seven over C&D last month; should strip sharper here; can't be discounted.
runners.comment: C&D scorer; solid third over C&D last month; should strip sharper here so not discounted
runners.owner: Mrs N Davies And Chris Mason

runners.name: Scent Of May
runners.spotlight: Gained a first win (at the fourth attempt) in 5f Chepstow maiden in August but she has failed to build on it, last of 12 in 5.7f handicap here 22 days ago; cheekpieces are now reached for.
runners.comment: Scored at Chepstow in August but she has failed to build on it, last here 22 days ago

runners.name: Harry Mole
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who took a step forward with second of 12 in 5f Thirsk handicap eight days ago; not taken lightly despite a 2lb weights rise.
runners.comment: Took step forward with second at Thirsk eight days ago; up 2lb but not taken lightly

runners.name: Dark Sun
runners.spotlight: Is 0-18 but she arrives in good nick, cheekpieces fitted when fourth of 13 in 6f Brighton handicap 15 days ago; not out of things with headgear retained.
runners.comment: Is 0-18 but she arrives in good nick; not out of things with headgear retained

runners.name: Sisters In The Sky
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who comes here in decent nick, fourth of nine in 5.7f classified event here eight days ago; not out of things back in handicap company.
runners.comment: C&D winner who comes here in decent nick; not out of things back in handicap company

runners.name: Phoenix Beach
runners.spotlight: On a long losing sequence and he came in a below-par seventh of 12 in 5.7f handicap here 22 days ago; yet to score on turf too.
runners.comment: On long losing sequence and a below-par seventh of 12 in 5.7f handicap here 22 days ago

runners.name: Call Time
runners.spotlight: C&D scorer but he's yet to fire in a pair of handicaps over C&D and at Chepstow this season; others appeal more.
runners.comment: C&D scorer but yet to fire in a pair of handicaps over C&D and at Chepstow this season
runners.owner: The 19th Hole Syndicate & J S Moore

runners.name: Macarone
runners.spotlight: Bagged a pair of 6f Wolverhampton handicaps for Ivan Furtado last autumn; ended his time with that yard below his best though and he's fared no better in 5f/6f Chepstow handicaps for his new handler recently.
runners.comment: Yet to hit top form in 5f/6f Chepstow handicaps for his new handler recently
