runners.name: Coco Hill
runners.spotlight: Without a victory since 2022 and she arrives under a cloud, beating just one home in 5.7f classified event here eight days ago.
runners.comment: Arrives under a cloud, beating just one home in 5.7f classified event here eight days ago

runners.name: Symbol Of Hope
runners.spotlight: Gained the last of his three C&D victories in 2024; rejoined former yard and got back on track with third of nine in 5.7f classified event here eight days ago; considered.
runners.comment: C&D scorer; rejoined former yard and back on track with third here latest; considered

runners.name: Titus Thor
runners.spotlight: Yet to show any promise in his five runs to date, ninth of 12 in 5.7f handicap here 22 days ago; easy to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Yet to show any promise in his five runs, ninth of 12 in 5.7f handicap here 22 days ago

runners.name: Coast
runners.spotlight: It's now 28 runs since her last success but she arrives in decent form, not enjoying the smoothest of passages when sixth of nine in 5.7f classified event here eight days ago; no forlorn hope.
runners.comment: It's now 28 runs since her last success but she arrives in decent form; no forlorn hope

races.race_name: Marsha Naify British EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Secret Harvest
runners.spotlight: Came through from off the pace to lead close home at Leicester (6f, good to firm) 19 days ago, only to be picked off by an even faster finisher; open to improvement and she won't have to find much of it; might want the ground to dry out though (withdrawn due to soft ground on intended debut).
runners.comment: Promising second at Leicester on debut; open to improvement and she won't need much of it

runners.name: Doodling
runners.spotlight: Her first two starts (6f, good to firm and 1m, AW) were promising but her progress has stalled since; drops in trip with a point to prove; cheekpieces worn last time are quickly discarded.
runners.comment: Not kicked on from her first two starts (6f and 1m); drops in trip with a point to prove

runners.name: Velvet Goldmine
runners.spotlight: 28-1 when a well-beaten eighth of 11 in a Newmarket fillies' maiden (7f, good) last October; better effort when third of six at Salisbury on her reappearance (6f, good to firm; well backed); just about sets the standard and she remains capable of better.
runners.comment: Third at Salisbury on recent reappearance; sets the standard with improvement expected

runners.name: Clementines Star
runners.spotlight: Perhaps a shade disappointing on her second 2yo start but her keeping-on third of 12 at Newcastle (6f) was a promising debut; withdrawn after getting upset in the stalls at Wolverhampton in April (9-2 at time of withdrawal); not discounted now switched to turf.
runners.comment: Promising debut last September; no progress on 2nd start (both 6f, AW); can do better

runners.name: Dark Angel Light
runners.spotlight: 60,000gns yearling; first foal of a 5f/6f winner (including 2yo/Listed; RPR 107); doesn't have a daunting standard to aim at on debut and she is an interesting candidate.
runners.comment: Dam a Listed winner (RPR 107); starts out at a realistic level and she's one to consider

runners.name: Navona Grey
runners.spotlight: She made an encouraging start to her career when fifth of 11, beaten just under 5l, at Chester two weeks ago (7f, good); the sixth home in that race has since won and she is open to improvement but dropping in trip isn't sure to suit.
runners.comment: Recent Chester debut was encouraging but over 7f; not sure to appreciate the drop in trip

runners.name: Honky Tonk Girl
runners.spotlight: Ninth foal; half-sister to seven winners including Clitheroe (Graded stakes-placed 6f/7f; RPR 95), Expensive Date (5f/6f including 2yo; 94) and Dr Strangelove (6f; 92); dam 6f/7f winner (77), half-sister to UAE 6f Group 1 winner Muarrab; starts out at a realistic level and she's worth a market check.
runners.comment: Half-sister to seven winners out of a 6f-7f winner; worth a market check at this level

runners.name: Whazzimo
runners.spotlight: Modest form in two 6f fillies' maidens this year; minor handicaps more suitable after this.
runners.comment: Modest form in two 6f fillies' maidens this year; minor handicaps more suitable after this

runners.name: Lavender Bloom
runners.spotlight: Signs of promise in two fillies' events this spring but she finished behind Secret Harvest at Leicester 19 days ago and may do so again.
runners.comment: Signs of ability in two runs this spring but has work to do with Secret Harvest on latest

runners.name: I Can Dream
runners.spotlight: Dropped right away in a 6f fillies' novice at Windsor (good to firm; 25-1) on debut 12 days ago; needs a big step forward.
runners.comment: Well-beaten 25-1 shot on recent Windsor debut; needs to have learned a lot from the run

runners.name: Miss Gold
runners.spotlight: Ran green and never in contention in a 6f fillies' maiden at Lingfield (AW) last September; has left Eve Johnson Houghton and new stable quick to fit headgear; likely best watched.
runners.comment: Down the field in one 2yo run for Eve Johnson Houghton; new yard quick to fit headgear

races.race_name: Support Ben Saunders Foundation Handicap

runners.name: Endofastorm
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who was runner-up twice here (including this trip) on firm in April; not at the same level twice since, although a slow start offers an excuse on the first occasion.
runners.comment: Runner-up twice here this spring and another who has to enter calculations

runners.name: Belle Amie
runners.spotlight: Consistent maiden for David Menuisier; won a 1m4f classified race on her debut for this yard and then ran pretty well in a Windsor handicap (11.5f, good to firm); on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Made a winning yard debut on AW and creditable fourth on turf since; player

runners.name: Blue Hero
runners.spotlight: Nine-time course scorer, most recently last August; reappearance fourth over 1m2f here was respectable and has to enter calculations.
runners.comment: Nine-time course scorer who should be sharper for reappearance; enters calculations

runners.name: Basilette
runners.spotlight: Bumper/hurdles winner (good/good to soft) who has been in good form back on the Flat, winning over C&D (firm) and third over 1m6f back here (good to firm) since; another 2lb higher now.
runners.comment: C&D win last month and third over 1m6f here since; respected

runners.name: Dogged
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner on turf, but offered no obvious promise on his recent first outing for Tony Carroll, taking his losing run up to 22.
runners.comment: No obvious promise on recent first run for Tony Carroll and losing run now 22

runners.name: Virtual Hug
runners.spotlight: Belatedly opened his Flat turf account over C&D (good) last month; respectable third to Basilette here on firm since; course record reads 423213 so expect him to be in the shake-up.
runners.comment: Solid Bath record includes a win over this trip last month; third to Basilette since
runners.owner: Mike Harris Racing & Nikki Evans Racing

runners.name: Ottoman
runners.spotlight: Successful off this mark at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm) last June; may have needed the outing when last of eight on reappearance back at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm again) 18 days ago but needed to see more to be confident about his prospects this afternoon.
runners.comment: Won off this mark last June but finished last on recent reappearance

runners.name: My O My
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden who plugged on for third over 1m2f here (firm) last time; fifth over C&D (also firm) prior to that; needs to find a bit more.
runners.comment: Nine-race maiden who has yet to make a significant impact in handicaps

runners.name: Onlyriversrunfree
runners.spotlight: 0-8 but has shown improved form when placing on AW at Lingfield (1m4f classified) and Southwell (2m handicap) this spring; looked in control for much of the home straight at Southwell, suggesting this mark is exploitable; well held previous attempt on turf but too soon to suggest she won't prove fully effective on the grass.
runners.comment: Player if she can transfer her improved AW form this spring back to turf

runners.name: Highland Harvey
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden; competitive in two 1m4f AW races (classified/handicap) for Denis Quinn; shouldn't be far away if in similar form after a break.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish maiden; placed in two 1m4f AW races for Denis Quinn; claims if in same form

runners.name: Ship To Shore
runners.spotlight: Both wins on all AW but runner-up three times on testing turf; has a reduced mark but that's as a result of him being off for 595 days; the betting should help guide to expectations.
runners.comment: On a good mark but betting perhaps the best guide after his long absence

runners.name: Master Dancer
runners.spotlight: Hurdle/chase winner (good/good to soft) who has belatedly switched to the Flat; struggled at massive prices in maiden/novices on first three starts but showed he can make an impact in low-grade handicaps when third of 11 at Kempton (1m4f) last month; considered.
runners.comment: Hurdle/chase winner; third on 1m4f Flat handicap debut last month; considered

runners.name: All About George
runners.spotlight: Has fewer convictions than most of these but made no impact in handicaps here (1m, good to soft) and at Lingfield (1m4f, AW) last autumn; first outing for eight months.
runners.comment: Low mileage but well held in two handicaps last autumn; off eight months

races.race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Handicap

runners.name: Swiped
runners.spotlight: Blinkered and took a big step forward on his handicap debut with neck second of 14 at Salisbury (7f) three weeks ago, finishing well having been hampered at halfway; big player up 2lb with this step up to 1m also a plus.
runners.comment: Blinkered and improved second at Salisbury latest; big player with step up to 1m a plus

runners.name: Aneirin's Sword
runners.spotlight: A dual AW winner (around 1m) early in 2026 but his more recent form is less encouraging, eighth of 13 in 1m Kempton handicap 24 days ago; needs to take a step forward.
runners.comment: A dual AW winner (around 1m) early in 2026 but his more recent form is less encouraging

runners.name: Denby's Dream
runners.spotlight: In good form without winning for her current yard (runner-up over 1m here) until going too freely and beating one in 1m Kempton handicap last month; she's worth another chance.
runners.comment: In good form without winning until too free at Kempton latest; she's worth another chance

runners.name: Tough Date
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in C&D maiden in April but he has failed to kick on sent handicapping, cheekpieces tried when seventh of 12 at Salisbury (1m2f) three weeks ago; more is required despite a falling mark.
runners.comment: Runner-up in C&D maiden but he has failed to kick on sent handicapping; more is needed

runners.name: Musical Soldier
runners.spotlight: Opened his account at Wolverhampton (7f) in May and has continued in good form, second of eight in another 7f handicap there 11 days ago; not taken lightly nudged up 1lb.
runners.comment: Scored at Wolverhampton (7f) in May and has continued in good form; not taken lightly

runners.name: Bosom Pals
runners.spotlight: Gained a first success in 7f Thirsk nursery in August; resumed from eight months off with an encouraging fourth of seven in 1m Nottingham handicap last month; needs considering with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Encouraging reappearance fourth at Nottingham; needs considering with cheekpieces added

runners.name: Footstepinthewoods
runners.spotlight: Took a step forward when fifth of 14 in 7f Salisbury handicap three weeks ago; can't be discounted off a 2lb lower mark in his bid for a maiden victory.
runners.comment: Took a step forward when fifth in 7f Salisbury handicap three weeks ago; not discounted

runners.name: India Gold
runners.spotlight: Gained a first win in 7f Musselburgh maiden in November; not disgraced when ninth of 13 in 1m Newmarket handicap on her return last month, set a lot to do; should strip sharper here so not out of things.
runners.comment: Not disgraced when ninth at Newmarket on return, set a lot to do; not out of things

runners.name: Leia Antilles
runners.spotlight: Little promise shown in her four runs to date, beating only one home on her handicap debut at Chepstow (1m2f) 15 days ago; cheekpieces go on now.
runners.comment: Little promise shown in four runs, beating one on her handicap debut at Chepstow latest

runners.name: Time For Action
runners.spotlight: Gained a first win over C&D in October but she was well below that form on both her subsequent runs, beating one in 1m Newcastle nursery in December; needs to hit the ground running after a six-month absence.
runners.comment: Gained a first win over C&D in October but well below that form on both subsequent runs

runners.name: Barbuda Bay
runners.spotlight: Debut runner-up in 7f Lingfield maiden in August but she has failed to build on it, sixth in 1m2f maiden there in April; may still do better back in trip now handicapping though.
runners.comment: Debut runner-up at Lingfield but she has failed to build on it; may do better back in trip

runners.name: Solar Phoenix
runners.spotlight: Been brought along steadily, a patiently-ridden sixth of ten in 7f AW Lingfield maiden in November; possibilities on his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Been brought along steadily, Lingfield sixth in November; possibilities on handicap debut

runners.name: Bird Of War
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with breakthrough success in 1m Newcastle nursery in November; went too freely on his reappearance when eighth of 12 at Nottingham (1m) in April; can take a step forward now.
runners.comment: Too free on reappearance when eighth at Nottingham; can take a step forward now

runners.name: Thisonesforyou
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start to life in handicaps at Leicester (1m) but he went keenly and failed to build on it with fifth of nine over C&D later in October; has since been gelded and could still do better on his seasonal return.
runners.comment: Scored at Leicester but only fifth over C&D after; since gelded and could still do better

races.race_name: Best Football Betting Offers At freebets.com Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Fighting Queen
runners.spotlight: Won a C&D handicap (firm) last May; mixed bag since then and she was a remote 14th of the 15 runners at Windsor three weeks ago; now tried in a tongue-tie.
runners.comment: C&D win last May but it has been a mixed bag since then; now tried in a tongue-tie

runners.name: Time After Time
runners.spotlight: Wide-margin classified win at Chepstow last July and she followed up in a Class 5 handicap at Haydock two weeks later (both 1m on good ground); well beaten in two runs since, including last month's return from 259 days on the sidelines; drops in class and could resume her progress.
runners.comment: Two 1m wins last summer; big player on that form and could leave reappearance behind her

runners.name: King Of Speed
runners.spotlight: His last four wins have come on AW at Wolverhampton; six C&D runs have yielded formfigures of 243234; third at Wolverhampton 12 days ago (for today's rider) and he should be in the thick of the action once again.
runners.comment: Six C&D runs have seen him finish no worse than 4th; good AW run latest; in the mix again

runners.name: Thapa Vc
runners.spotlight: Won two fast-ground C&D handicaps last season, both off higher marks than today's; threatening a win in recent starts and Jack Callan takes off a handy 3lb; unraced on slower than good ground.
runners.comment: Two C&D wins off higher marks last year; threatening of late; unraced on slower than good

runners.name: Terries Royale
runners.spotlight: Won twice over a mile in 2024, earning a peak BHA rating of 77; less good since, including in five runs for Tony Carroll; today's new tongue-tie isn't enough to tempt.
runners.comment: On the downgrade and he has suffered heavy defeats on last two runs; now tongue tied

runners.name: Eye Of The Water
runners.spotlight: Has plenty of C&D form to his name, including two wins, but his losing run is approaching three years; place claims but likely vulnerable for win purposes.
runners.comment: Conditions will suit but losing run stretches back to August 2023; place chance

runners.name: Drafted
runners.spotlight: Has run some fair races in defeat for his new stable but he is essentially regressive; surprising if there weren't a few stronger in today's field.
runners.comment: He has run some fair races for his new stable but he looks regressive; others are stronger

runners.name: Mishy's Star
runners.spotlight: Placed in a 7f classified event at Southwell in February but she has followed that with two heavy defeats, including C&D latest; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Two heavy defeats have followed her third-place finish in a 7f AW classified event

runners.name: Daddy Chill
runners.spotlight: Left Andrew Balding's yard (still runs in his colours) after two encouraging runs in early 2025; poor form this year and opposable despite his basement mark.
runners.comment: Little to cheer about in 2026 and he remains opposable despite a reduced mark

runners.name: Dappled Light
runners.spotlight: Won two 1m handicaps last May, including here, and back on the scoresheet in a C&D handicap three weeks ago (good to firm); that form is already working out well and a 4lb rise isn't beyond him; probably wouldn't want the ground to be worse than good.
runners.comment: Two-time C&D winner, including last month (form franked); good or faster ground ideal

runners.name: Naval Ensign
runners.spotlight: Got up late to win a 7f AW handicap for Jack Jones in January; only 1lb higher today and her new trainer is quick to fit a tongue-tie.
runners.comment: 7f AW win for Jack Jones in January; starts out for new yard with a tongue-tie added

races.race_name: Best Football Betting Offers At freebets.com Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Nammos
runners.spotlight: 20-1 for her stable debut here last month but she kept on well to see off nine rivals (1m, firm); similar form when second of four back here 18 days ago; feasible mark still but ground slower than good would be a concern.
runners.comment: Two good C&D runs (firm) for new stable; ground slower than good would be a concern

runners.name: Danehill Star
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins over further this year; his fourth over C&D last month has been well advertised since and he should be breaking his turf duck sooner rather than later.
runners.comment: Two AW wins over further this year; solid C&D 4th last month; should be involved

runners.name: Revich
runners.spotlight: No win since September 2023 but he is operating off a much-reduced mark now and he emerged from the pack to take a never-nearer third here eight days ago (1m2f, good); might need them to go too fast up front to win but he holds each-way claims all the same.
runners.comment: On lowly mark now and his strong-finishing 3rd here last week was more encouraging; chance

runners.name: Galactic Glow
runners.spotlight: Four course wins over 1m2f on fast ground; three solid efforts in defeat this year, again over 1m2f, and he has handled slow ground in the past; unlikely to be far away back at 1m.
runners.comment: Four course wins over 1m2f; three good efforts this summer; contender back down in trip

runners.name: Brinton
runners.spotlight: Overcame a slow start to win a C&D handicap (firm) in April; completely blew the start at Wolverhampton last month and was always behind thereafter; has the ability to pop up again but too risky for comfort.
runners.comment: Increasingly tricky at the start; this C&D winner now comes with too much risk attached

runners.name: Freedom Bay
runners.spotlight: 0-12 and she was out of form last autumn; another heavy defeat followed on her seasonal return eight weeks ago (first run after wind surgery); now sports a tongue-tie and cheekpieces; not a solid option.
runners.comment: 12-race maiden; ended 2025 quietly and again well beaten on seasonal debut (after wind op)

runners.name: King David
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f handicap at Kempton in September 2024 when trained by Marco Botti; there have been some good efforts in defeat for Gay Kelleway this year but he has been disappointing in two of his last three starts; has form at this trip but hasn't convinced as a strong stayer; visor the accessory of choice today.
runners.comment: Just one win from 23 starts and this trip perhaps stretches him

runners.name: Victory Sound
runners.spotlight: Having tumbled down the weights for this yard he offered much more when third of 11 at Lingfield (1m, AW) 12 days ago; 1lb lower today and dangerous if reproducing that form back on turf.
runners.comment: Signs of a revival when 3rd at Lingfield (1m, AW) 12 days ago; dangerous if reproducing it

runners.name: Pop Favorite
runners.spotlight: Eight times an AW winner but he has run just three times on turf and offered little on each occasion; plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Eight-time AW winner but yet to offer much on turf

runners.name: James Park Woods
runners.spotlight: Won twice in the space of three days last September, over C&D and then following up at Brighton (both on slower than good); yet to shine this year and the ground may not be soft enough for him to show his best.
runners.comment: C&D win last September and followed up at Brighton 48 hours later; not shone yet in 2026

races.race_name: Peter Watson Memorial Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Tellherthename
runners.spotlight: Won twice over hurdles for Ben Pauling; not disgraced in either the County Hurdle or the Swinton for his new trainer this year and made a winning return to fences when finishing alone at Cartmel (2m1f, good) three weeks ago; that win should have done his confidence a world of good and he should take all the beating.
runners.comment: Made a winning return to fences when finishing alone at Cartmel three weeks ago

runners.name: King Ulanda
runners.spotlight: Dual winner over hurdles and made a winning chase debut at Bangor (2m1f, good) four weeks ago; that race did fall apart with none of his rivals running their races and he has been whacked up 10lb as a result; that should still have given his confidence a boost.
runners.comment: Dual winner over hurdles and made a winning chase debut at Bangor four weeks ago

runners.name: Tom Doniphon
runners.spotlight: Won twice over hurdles at Taunton over 2m3f for previous yard; after a promising chase debut at Aintree in October he took a backward step at Exeter the following month; returns from a seven-month break with new trainer but may need to go back up in trip to find a winning opportunity.
runners.comment: After a promising chase debut at Aintree he took a backward step at Exeter last autumn

runners.name: Ballykinlar
runners.spotlight: Won three times (2m2f, 2m4f) when trained in Ireland last summer; unplaced in three valuable handicap hurdles (2m4f-2m7f) for new trainer this spring; the drop back in trip for his chasing debut may not be in his favour.
runners.comment: Unplaced in three valuable handicap hurdles for new trainer this spring; chase debut

runners.name: Ice In The Veins
runners.spotlight: Won twice over hurdles for Dan Skelton last November; pulled up on his chase debut after reported to have bled from the nose at Bangor four weeks ago and subsequently sold for £5,000; regular hood is left off on stable debut and best to proceed with caution.
runners.comment: Pulled up on his chase debut having bled from the nose at Bangor four weeks ago

races.race_name: For Soldiers For Life Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Ladies Day
runners.spotlight: Point winner who ran to a useful level in bumpers without success; got off the mark on her second run over hurdles when scoring at Bangor (2m, good) three weeks ago; strong claims.
runners.comment: Got off the mark over hurdles on her second run in this sphere at Bangor three weeks ago

runners.name: Eternal Angel
runners.spotlight: Had some fair form for Nicky Henderson without success but was unplaced in three outings last summer when trained in Ireland; sold for £19,000 last October and dropped in distance to get off the mark on his stable debut in a Huntingdon maiden (2m, good to firm) last month; incurs a 7lb penalty but should run well.
runners.comment: Got off the mark on his stable debut in a Huntingdon maiden last month

runners.name: L'Amalric
runners.spotlight: Failed to complete in four Irish maiden points but did little wrong when third of seven on his stable debut in a bumper at Southwell (2m, good) in March; made a winning hurdles debut at Worcester (2m, good) last month and should have bags of improvement in him; contender.
runners.comment: Made a winning hurdles debut at Worcester last month

runners.name: Knolton Park
runners.spotlight: Showed nothing more than minor promise in three bumpers for David Loughnane in early part of last season; beaten 48l on hurdles debut at Bangor (2m, good) four weeks ago and likely to need time.
runners.comment: Beaten 48l on hurdles debut at Bangor four weeks ago and likely to need time
runners.owner: Mrs A Bannister-Bell & Mrs J Price

runners.name: Scoro
runners.spotlight: 1-12 in points and makes hurdles debut after a 12-month break; best watched.
runners.comment: 1-12 in points and makes hurdles debut after a 12-month break; best watched

runners.name: Pretty Darn Quick
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on both hurdles starts and easy to dismiss.
runners.comment: Tailed off on both hurdles starts and easy to dismiss

races.race_name: Groundshore Cup Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Saucats
runners.spotlight: 0-22 but showed marked improvement in first-time cheekpieces when runner-up at Newton Abbot (2m2f, good) 17 days ago; steps back up in trip and should go close if able to reproduce his best form.
runners.comment: 0-22 but showed marked improvement when runner-up at Newton Abbot 17 days ago

runners.name: Onehundredneighty
runners.spotlight: Showed modest form for Max Young, including in two handicaps; well-backed but could only manage fourth at Warwick (2m, good; 2-1) last month on stable debut; steps back up in trip and not one to give up on.
runners.comment: Well-backed but could only manage fourth at Warwick last month on stable debut

runners.name: Saint Vic
runners.spotlight: 0-13 but ran well on his second race back after a year's absence when runner-up here (2m7f, good) last month; fair fifth at Worcester since and the drop back in trip may be in his favour.
runners.comment: Ran well on his second race back after a year's absence when runner-up here last month

runners.name: Kayla's Friend
runners.spotlight: Fair form in three maiden hurdles; first-time tongue-tie is now applied; no surprise were she to show significant improvement on this handicap debut for top yard.
runners.comment: Fair form in three maiden hurdles; handicap debut

runners.name: Winston's Oath
runners.spotlight: Best run over hurdles when third of six at Fakenham (2m, good) in April; hampered by faller when beaten 36l back at Fakenham (2m4f, good) 13 days ago on handicap debut and may be worth another chance.
runners.comment: Hampered by faller when beaten 36l back at Fakenham 13 days ago on handicap debut

runners.name: Babychino
runners.spotlight: Has shown just modest form and needs improvement.
runners.comment: Has shown just modest form and needs improvement

runners.name: Roland Garros
runners.spotlight: Has shown promise in five runs over hurdles, but was beaten 35l on handicap debut at Wetherby (2m, good to soft) in March; has won low-grade handicap on the Flat since; first-time blinkers replace cheekpieces; steps up in trip and not out of this.
runners.comment: Beaten 35l on handicap debut at Wetherby in March; has won on Flat since

runners.name: Mya Du Clos
runners.spotlight: Yet to be placed but ran her best race when fourth of seven at Ffos Las (2m4f, good to soft) last month; place claims.
runners.comment: Yet to be placed but ran her best race when fourth at Ffos Las last month

runners.name: Ballynoe Boy
runners.spotlight: Placed in both bumpers and has made the frame in his last two runs in handicaps over hurdles; down 1lb and wouldn't need to improve much more to be competitive.
runners.comment: Placed in both bumpers and has made the frame in last two runs in handicaps over hurdles

runners.name: The Brave Guy
runners.spotlight: Placed in a heavy-ground Irish point in March 2024; well held in three runs over hurdles; major improvement required on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Well held in three runs over hurdles; major improvement required on handicap debut

runners.name: No Risk Today
runners.spotlight: Won an Irish mares' maiden point on debut (good to yielding) last year; comfortably accounted for in two bumpers and three hurdles for this trainer; steps up in trip on handicap debut and may well show improvement for top yard.
runners.comment: Well held in two bumpers and three hurdles for this trainer; handicap debut

runners.name: Sims' Law
runners.spotlight: Has struggled in his three races to date; handicap debut needs to bring about major improvement.
runners.comment: Has struggled in his three races to date; handicap debut

runners.name: Fane Court
runners.spotlight: Landed small-field point last November but only modest form over hurdles; pulled up on chase debut last time; has switched yards and best watched for now.
runners.comment: Landed small-field point last November but only modest form over hurdles

runners.name: Celine Man
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 8yo who offered little in a pair of 2m Huntingdon handicaps for his current yard in February; easy to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Offered little in a pair of 2m Huntingdon handicaps for his current yard in February

races.race_name: #Moogangetsknighted Novices' Handicap Chase

runners.name: Let's Sail Away
runners.spotlight: Point (2m4f, good to soft) and handicap hurdle (2m2f, good) winner who ran well in defeat last season on all four chase starts; should go close if ready to roll after seven months off.
runners.comment: Ran well in defeat last season on all four chase starts

runners.name: Jo Arengeot Bey AA
runners.spotlight: Point winner who got off the mark at the third time of asking over fences over C&D (good) last month, finding plenty when challenged; there should be more to come.
runners.comment: Got off the mark at the third time of asking over fences over C&D last month

runners.name: Daring Plan
runners.spotlight: 0-18 under rules but has gone close on several occasions; runner-up at Fontwell (2m5f, good to soft) in March; unseated around halfway last time; place claims.
runners.comment: 0-18 under rules but has gone close on several occasions; runner-up at Fontwell in March
runners.owner: Miss Jane Edgar & D V Gardner

runners.name: Lynsey Larue
runners.spotlight: Dual Irish point winner; off the mark at the sixth attempt over fences at Ffos Las (3m, good to soft) last month in first-time tongue-tie (retained); should have more to offer with that first win under her belt.
runners.comment: Off the mark at the sixth attempt over fences at Ffos Las last month

runners.name: Twp Stori
runners.spotlight: Has won twice over hurdles; never threatened when third of four finishers on his second chase start, at Fontwell (3m2f, good) last month in cheekpieces (left off here); needs to do more.
runners.comment: Never threatened when staying on third on his second chase start at Fontwell last month

runners.name: Brave Move
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame in three of his eight starts over hurdles; sports first-time cheekpieces on chasing debut and may well show improvement over this new discipline.
runners.comment: Has made the frame in three of his eight starts over hurdles; chase debut

runners.name: R Bernard
runners.spotlight: No joy when tried over fences in 2023; won twice over hurdles at Stratford in spring 2024; returns to fences after a sharpener over hurdles at Cartmel last month but hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Returns to fences after a sharpener over hurdles at Cartmel last month but hard to fancy

runners.name: Izzy's Grey
runners.spotlight: 0-16 and has attracted support without delivering on his last two starts over hurdles; makes chasing debut and may have more to offer.
runners.comment: 0-16 and has attracted support without delivering on his last two starts over hurdles

runners.name: Mr McWhinny
runners.spotlight: Has struggled under rules in Ireland; hard to know what to make of facile win in Irish point (3m, good) last month after ten-month absence; market will be best guide on stable debut.
runners.comment: Facile winner of Irish point last month after ten-month absence; stable debut

runners.name: Crest Of Valour
runners.spotlight: It's been underwhelming stuff thus far but has time on his side and may well show improvement on this chase debut with first-time blinkers added.
runners.comment: It's been underwhelming stuff thus far but may well show improvement on this chase debut

races.race_name: 4 Oaks Group Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Imperial Bede
runners.spotlight: Landed his second win over fences, at Catterick (2m3f, soft) in February with the assistance of first-time cheekpieces (retained since); ran well when runner-up at Haydock (2m4f, good) in April but emptied quickly at Warwick last month; claims if putting his best foot forward.
runners.comment: Ran well when runner-up at Haydock in April but emptied quickly at Warwick last month.

runners.name: Mighty Fleur
runners.spotlight: Beaten 25l on her chasing debut at Newcastle (2m7f, good to soft) in January but has blossomed since switching back to hurdles in cheekpieces (retained here), making all at Fakenham and Cartmel last month; this is tougher but he's bang in form and should set them all a merry gallop on this second stab at chasing.
runners.comment: Made all over hurdles at Fakenham and Cartmel last month; returns to chasing,

runners.name: A Dublin Job
runners.spotlight: Won three consecutive races for Harry Derham in 2024/25; last of six on stable debut over C&D (good; 40-1) last month following ten-month absence; best left alone at present.
runners.comment: Last of six on stable debut over C&D last month following ten-month absence.

runners.name: Mary
runners.spotlight: Both wins over hurdles when making all; has been ridden with more restraint since returning this year and was still in contention when falling at the last fence on her return to chasing at Bangor (2m4f, good) three weeks ago; looks sure to be thereabouts as long as her fall has left no ill-effects.
runners.comment: Fell at the last when still there on her return to chasing at Bangor three weeks ago.

runners.name: Ridin Solo
runners.spotlight: Only won a Sedgefield maiden in nine races over hurdles; won his only point and has finished third on both starts over fences; returns from seven-month break and not out of this.
runners.comment: Has finished third on both starts over fences; returns from seven-month break.

runners.name: Order Of The Dance
runners.spotlight: Scored over hurdles on his stable debut at Ffos Las (2m4f, good) in October when sporting first-time tongue-tie (retained since); pulled up six days later but has possibilities on chasing debut if able to overcome eight-month absence.
runners.comment: Scored over hurdles on his stable debut at Ffos Las in October; chase debut.

races.race_name: Noble Minds Counselling Services Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Loro White
runners.spotlight: Showed significant improvement, following wind surgery, to get off the mark at Perth (2m4f, good to soft) last month, on her handicap debut in first-time cheekpieces (retained); just a 5lb rise looks generous and she should have no problem with this step up in trip; solid claims.
runners.comment: Showed significant improvement to get off the mark at Perth last month.

runners.name: Parikarma
runners.spotlight: Has three wins over hurdles but not firing on all cylinders since returning from a lengthy layoff in February; steps up in trip but others preferred.
runners.comment: Has three wins over hurdles but not at her best since returning from a lengthy layoff.

runners.name: Edna E Mode
runners.spotlight: 0-18 under rules but ran her usual solid race when third of ten at Worcester (2m7f, good) 16 days ago in a returning visor (retained); looks sure to give another good account but doesn't appeal for win purposes.
runners.comment: 0-18 under rules but ran her usual solid race when third at Worcester 16 days ago.

runners.name: My Lovely Horse
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in three of her six attempts over hurdles; steps up in trip but hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Pulled up in three of her six attempts over hurdles; steps up in trip but hard to fancy.

runners.name: Shinealight
runners.spotlight: Landed three-runner maiden Irish point in May last year; finished a remote third of seven finishers over C&D (good) last month; closest she's finished under rules was a 7l-defeat when fourth at Cartmel (2m6f, good) last time; first-time cheekpieces go on but she probably needs to come down a few more pounds.
runners.comment: Closest she's finished under rules was when beaten just over 7l at Cartmel last time.

runners.name: Mistral Blue
runners.spotlight: Dual 2m3f winner on good ground; decent effort when third of seven finishers at Market Rasen (2m4f, good) 16 days ago but is still 4lb above her last winning mark and needs some respite from the handicapper.
runners.comment: Decent effort when third at Market Rasen 16 days ago.

runners.name: Dontworrywegotthis
runners.spotlight: Low-level maiden who's faced this sort of test on five of her last six starts and is 12lb out of the handicap this time.
runners.comment: Low-level maiden who's faced this sort of test on five of her last six starts.

races.race_name: QuinnBet Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Twist Of Fatecatch
runners.spotlight: Sole success came in a Fontwell maiden last April; has run with credit on several occasions, such as when third at Worcester (2m7f, good) last month; swaps cheekpieces for a visor and shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: Has run with credit on several occasions, such as when third at Worcester last month.

runners.name: Orange Diamond
runners.spotlight: Generally consistent, he has four wins over hurdles at his name; ran his usual honest race when fourth at Bangor (2m3f, good) three weeks ago; won't be far away but may be a tad high in the weights.
runners.comment: Ran his usual honest race when fourth at Bangor three weeks ago.

runners.name: Chillhi
runners.spotlight: Is struggling to recapture his best form at present and yet another switch in headgear tried with cheekpieces replacing blinkers.
runners.comment: Is struggling to recapture his best form at present.

runners.name: Havefunontherun
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden last spring and was beaten a neck on his handicap debut at Southwell (2m4f, good to soft) in November; two lesser runs since and first-time cheekpieces are now added to the tongue-tie worn last time; has something to prove.
runners.comment: Beaten a neck on his handicap debut at Southwell in November; two lesser runs since.

runners.name: Lightfingered Jack
runners.spotlight: 0-5 but decent form when placed at Aintree (2m4f, good to soft) on his debut last November and when third at Warwick (2m3f, good) in April; pulled up lame on his handicap debut at Worcester (2m7f, good) last month and may warrant another chance.
runners.comment: Pulled up lame on his handicap debut at Worcester last month.

runners.name: Faded Fantasy
runners.spotlight: Has four wins over hurdles but last success was last August off 7lb lower; needs to come down a few pounds before winning again.
runners.comment: Has four wins over hurdles but last success was last August off 7lb lower.

runners.name: Mexican Stan
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at the ninth attempt when stepping up in trip at Warwick (3m1f, good) last month; didn't fare too badly when fourth at Worcester (2m7f, good) since and won't be far away.
runners.comment: Got off the mark at the ninth attempt when stepping up in trip at Warwick last month.

runners.name: Roaring Home
runners.spotlight: Has returned from a short break in fine fettle and recorded back-to-back wins at Chepstow (2m7f, good) in April and Aintree (2m4f, good) last month; must have a good chance of landing the hat-trick here.
runners.comment: Has returned from a short break in fine fettle and recorded wins at Chepstow and Aintree.

runners.name: Belvedere Blast
runners.spotlight: All of his seven wins have been at either Hexham or Market Rasen; never really threatened when a distant third on his first run for six months over C&D (good) nine days ago; best to wait until he runs over his favoured tracks.
runners.comment: Never really threatened when a distant third over C&D nine days ago.

runners.name: Sauce Of Life
runners.spotlight: Unbeaten in bumpers (2-2) and very lightly raced for his age; won a hurdle handicap at Doncaster (2m4f, good to soft) in December 2024 but he's had only two runs since and has struggled in both; has something to prove after another absence and he needs first-time cheekpieces to work.
runners.comment: Won a hurdle handicap at Doncaster in December 2024 but he's had only two runs since.
runners.owner: Mrs I C Sellars And Miss D C Sellars

races.race_name: British EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B, C & D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive) (Div I)

runners.name: Bin Waary
runners.spotlight: Had a wide trip but showed ability when fourth on his debut at Kempton (6f, AW) last month; however, he failed to build on that when a 10l third of six at Lingfield (7f, good) 26 days ago and better is needed.
runners.comment: Showed ability on his debut (6f, AW) but failed to build on that at Lingfield 26 days ago

runners.name: Alfey Moon
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on his debut at Chepstow (6f, good to soft) but did show more in a steadily run race at Ffos Las (7.5f, good to soft) nine days ago; probably flattered to finish as close as he did on that occasion and better is needed.
runners.comment: Showed more at Ffos Las nine days ago but that was a steadily run race; better needed

runners.name: Duke Of Burgundy
runners.spotlight: February foal by Belardo; third foal; dam 1m/1m2f winner (including 2yo; RPR 99), half-sister to useful winners Third Dimension (7f-9.5f) and Heading North (7f 2yo), out of 1m winning half-sister to Listed winners Heavenly Whisper (1m) and Gipsy Moth (5f); paper appeal and stable can ready a newcomer; market support a plus for this likely debutant.
runners.comment: February foal; dam 1m/1m2f winner (including 2yo; RPR 99); stable can ready a newcomer

runners.name: Akaraka
runners.spotlight: March foal; 50,000gns yearling; Ghaiyyath colt; fourth foal; half-brother to 12.4f AW winner Mon Etoile (RPR 65); dam French/Spanish Listed-placed 10.7f-1m4f winner (98), closely related to winners Saikung (1m) and Gloss (6f/6.5f), out of 1m3f/1m4f Flat and 2m hurdle winner; pedigree suggests he may want longer trips but the market should be informative on his debut.
runners.comment: 50,000gns yearling; Ghaiyyath colt; dam French/Spanish Listed-placed 10.7f/1m4f winner

runners.name: Rasananda
runners.spotlight: February foal by Ardad; 28,000gns yearling; third foal; half-sister to 7f AW winner Soho Square (RPR 75); dam unraced half-sister to useful winners Lat Hawill (7f 2yo/9.4f), William Bligh (7f 2yo/9.5f) and Chocala (1m6f-2m), out of 1m4f Listed winner; the sole filly and others make stronger appeal.
runners.comment: February foal; filly by Ardad; 28,000gns yearling; half-sister to 7f AW winner Soho Square

runners.name: Eighth Immortal
runners.spotlight: April foal by Naval Crown; £40,000 breeze-up 2yo; fifth foal; half-brother to 6f/7f 2yo winner Tuwajeri (including AW/KSA); dam 6f 2yo winner (RPR 81), half-sister to 8.5f/1m2f Group 3 winner Lady Gloria and Listed-placed 1m winner Light And Shade; watch the betting on his debut.
runners.comment: £40,000 breeze-up buy; half-brother to 6f/7f 2yo winner Tuwajeri; watch the betting

runners.name: Turtle Beach
runners.spotlight: March foal by Palace Pier; 40,000gns yearling; fourth foal; half-brother to 6f AW winner Angel Of Rain (RPR 77); dam 9.4f AW winner (86), half-sister to winners Raffle Prize (5f/6f 2yo Group 2) and Misty Dancer (useful 1m2f), out of 7f Group 3 winner; best watched on his debut.
runners.comment: Palace Pier colt; 40,000gns yearling; half-brother to 6f AW winner Angel Of Rain (RPR 77)

runners.name: Dr Rascal
runners.spotlight: April foal by Sottsass; second foal; half-brother to 5f 2yo winner Miss Rascal (RPR 88); dam unraced half-sister to Group-placed 1m1f winner Up And Under, out of Listed-placed 1m2f winning sister to US 1m 2yo Grade 1 winner Line Of Duty; half-brother was fifth in the Queen Mary as a 2yo and he's one to note on his debut, particularly if the market speaks favourably.
runners.comment: April foal; by Sottsass; half-brother was fifth in the 2024 Queen Mary; interesting

runners.name: Hamdani Mokhater
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on his debut at Yarmouth (6f, good to firm) in April and it was the same story at Beverley (7.5f, good to firm) 17 days ago; stable won this last year but he's likely one for further down the line.
runners.comment: Well beaten at Yarmouth (6f) and Beverley (7.5f); likely one for further down the line

races.race_name: British EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B, C & D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive) (Div II)

runners.name: Regal Chita
runners.spotlight: Showed plenty of promise when keeping on for third at Newmarket (6f, good) on his debut last month and again showed ability when filling the same position at Nottingham (6f, good) 13 days ago; steps up in trip (dam won over 7f) and holds claims.
runners.comment: Third at Newmarket and Nottingham (6f, good) last month; steps up in trip with claims

runners.name: Time Saxon Warrior
runners.spotlight: Looked in need of the experience on his debut at Bath (5.5f, firm; slowly away and ran green) and still looked to be learning when an encouraging third of 12 at Windsor (6f, good to firm) three weeks ago, shaping as if this step up in trip would suit; big player.
runners.comment: Still looked to be learning when a promising third at Windsor; steps up to 7f; big player

runners.name: Vegas Lights
runners.spotlight: February foal; filly by Dandy Man; 20,000euros foal and £48,000 yearling; first foal; dam unraced sister to smart 8.6f-2m winner Wonder Legend, half-sister to 10.5f winner Wonder Angel, out of unraced half-sister to 1m2f Listed winner Alsace Lorraine; represents powerful connections and the betting should be informative.
runners.comment: Filly by Dandy Man; £48,000 yearling; dam unraced half-sister to smart 8.6f-2m winner

runners.name: Confernation
runners.spotlight: Out of a half-sister to 7f/1m Listed winner Doom; missed the break slightly but did show promise when fourth of eight on his debut at Beverley (7.5f, good to firm; 4-1) 17 days ago; open to progress.
runners.comment: Missed the break but did show promise on his debut at Beverley (good to firm) last month

runners.name: Sheephavenbay
runners.spotlight: April foal by Bated Breath; ninth foal; closely related to 11.6f/1m4f winner Sigrid Nansen (RPR 69), half-brother to winners Atrium (7f/1m including 2yo/AW; 105), Milltown Star (6f-1m including 2yo/AW/Listed; 101) and Valkyrie Storm (1m; 79); dam 6.3f/7f winner (including 2yo/AW; 75); lots to like on paper and he's interesting on his debut.
runners.comment: April foal; closely related to a winner and half-brother to four others; interesting

runners.name: Vinery
runners.spotlight: February foal; gelding by Massaat; fifth foal; half-brother to 7f/1m winner Almost An Angel (including AW; RPR 73); dam 6f-1m5f maiden (56), half-sister to prolific 5f winner Vintage Clarets, out of 1m winner; market may be the best guide on his debut.
runners.comment: Gelding by Massaat; half-brother to 7f/1m winner Almost An Angel; market may guide
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late G E Dudfield

runners.name: Ignite The Fire
runners.spotlight: March foal by Ulysses; fourth foal; half-brother to 1m6f/2m AW winner Forglen (RPR 64); dam tough 5f/6f winner (including 2yo/AW; 89), out of 7f/1m winner; best watched on debut unless the market speaks favourably.
runners.comment: March foal; dam tough 5f/6f winner; best watched on debut unless the market speaks

runners.name: Crown Ranger
runners.spotlight: Well backed but disappointed when last of six on his debut at Southwell (7f, AW; 5-4) 23 days ago, dropping away after setting a steady gallop; switches to turf and it will be interesting to see if his supporters return.
runners.comment: Well backed but disappointed on his debut at Southwell (AW) 23 days ago; switches to turf

races.race_name: WM Installations Ltd Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: El Nay
runners.spotlight: Placed on all four starts, most recently when third at Chepstow (1m2f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; does not appear to be progressing and ideally needs to learn to settle better but he'll be fine over this shorter trip and probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Does not appear to be progressing but has been placed on all four starts; in the mix again

runners.name: Lucky Camino
runners.spotlight: Looked a shade unlucky not to make winning debut at Nottingham (8.3f, good to soft) last month and and again ran well in defeat over that C&D (good) 12 days later; strong claims.
runners.comment: Went close on both starts at Nottingham (8.3f) last month; strong claims

runners.name: A'Alby
runners.spotlight: 1,000gns 2yo; third foal; half-brother to 7f AW winner Creme De Cacao (RPR 76); dam 6f 2yo winner (including Listed; 100); seems unlikely to meet the standard required on debut.
runners.comment: 1,000gns 2yo; half-brother to a fair 7f AW winner; probably best watched on debut

runners.name: Freddy Fearless
runners.spotlight: 11,000euros yearling; first foal; dam unraced, out of half-sister to 6f Group 1 winner Tante Rose; considered only if lively in the betting before debut.
runners.comment: 11,000euros yearling; considered only if lively in the betting before debut

runners.name: Wyvill
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; half-brother to winners Daonethatgotaway (1m/1m4f; RPR 76), Maverick Style (7f AW 2yo; 58) and Maamur (Spanish 7f/1m); dam placed 7f/1m (74), half-sister to 2yo Group 3 winners Brown Sugar (6f) and Burnt Sugar (5f/6f); trainer has only occasional first-time-out winners.
runners.comment: Out of a well-connected mare but stable has only occasional first-time-out winners

runners.name: Electric Dreams
runners.spotlight: Made the frame on debut at Kempton (1m, AW) in April and probably ran to similar level when seventh at Newbury (1m2f, good) last month but is not yet living up to his pedigree and has a bit to find.
runners.comment: Shaped with promise on both starts (1m/1m2f) but has a bit to find

runners.name: Forever Endless
runners.spotlight: Out of a Listed sprint winner; 80-1 and probably flattered by proximity to principals when never-dangerous fifth of six in slowly run contest at Nottingham (8.3f, good) last month.
runners.comment: Never-dangerous fifth of six in slowly run contest at Nottingham (8.3f) last month

races.race_name: SignFab Celebrating 25 Years Handicap

runners.name: Jersey Maverick
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 16 starts came at Epsom (7f, good to firm) last July; went close at Southwell (7f, AW) in April and did best of those ridden prominently when eighth of 22 at York (7f, good) three weeks ago; this drop back in trip shouldn't inconvenience him and he's considered.
runners.comment: Went close on the AW in April and did best of those ridden prominently latest; back to 6f
runners.owner: Tim Cottier, Clark, Spence And Fellowes

runners.name: Koffee And Kale
runners.spotlight: Off the mark by a short head in a 5f novice (good to soft) in October and subsequently gelded; two creditable efforts since returning from a break and steps back up to 6f with place claims.
runners.comment: Won here over 5f by a short head in October; two creditable efforts since being gelded

runners.name: Numero Vingt
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden at Chelmsford (AW) in February and followed up in a Lingfield (AW) novice next time; well held in three handicaps since and returns to turf for the first time since his debut having been dropped another 3lb.
runners.comment: Won two AW events in February but well held in three handicaps since; dropped another 3lb

runners.name: Angels' Share
runners.spotlight: Won well over 7f at Lingfield (AW) on two occasions last August/September (maiden/handicap) but she's been well beaten on three turf starts since, including over this trip at Haydock (soft) 23 days ago; best watched until returning to the AW.
runners.comment: Won twice over 7f at Lingfield (AW) last year but struggled in three turf starts since

runners.name: Fenlander
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 17th attempt when winning on good to firm over this C&D 19 days ago, beating seven rivals; back up 4lb but he's been placed off higher marks in the past and could go well.
runners.comment: Off the mark when winning over C&D last time; up 4lb but has been placed off higher marks

runners.name: Obsidian Verse
runners.spotlight: Ran below market expectations on his handicap debut at Lingfield (7f, AW) in March but posted an improved effort when winning over 6f at Southwell (AW) 44 days ago; needs to transfer that form to turf but this well-related gelding could have more to offer.
runners.comment: Showed improved form when winning on the AW at Southwell; needs to transfer that to turf

runners.name: Mumayaz
runners.spotlight: Won comfortably after travelling strongly over this C&D (good to firm) last month but his jockey reported he never travelled when fourth of nine under a penalty at Salisbury (6f, good) 30 days ago; now 2lb higher but he can't be dismissed.
runners.comment: Won over C&D last month but never travelled when fourth at Salisbury last time; 2lb higher

runners.name: Cypriot Diaspora
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner from 32 starts, the latest success coming at Ffos Las (7.5f, soft) last August; below her best in three starts this year and this drop back to 6f isn't certain to suit, but she is back on a handy mark.
runners.comment: 5-32; below her best in three starts this year and drop back to 6f isn't certain to suit

runners.name: Under The Twilight
runners.spotlight: Five wins on turf have been at Windsor and Salisbury, the latest off a 2lb higher mark than today's; runner-up over this C&D (good to firm) in April before another solid effort at Windsor (6f, good to firm) 33 days ago; drops in grade with claims.
runners.comment: Runner-up over C&D April and it was another solid effort last time; drops in grade; claims

races.race_name: Go West Play Live @leicesterraces Saturday 4th July Handicap

runners.name: Divot
runners.spotlight: 15-race maiden but he has been in the first three on six occasions, including when third of eight at Lingfield (1m4f, AW) in April; however, he was behind Maywedance in first-time cheekpieces (discarded) at Kempton 24 days ago and is yet to prove effective on turf.
runners.comment: 0-15; behind Maywedance at Kempton last time and is yet to prove effective on turf

runners.name: Lawmans Blis
runners.spotlight: Won on his second start for this stable (and second run since wind surgery) at Catterick (1m4f, good) last month and did best of those from off the pace when fourth in a classified at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) 11 days ago; stepping back up in trip looks a good move and he's a contender.
runners.comment: Did best of those from off the pace over 1m2f last time; step back up to 1m4f will suit

runners.name: Naughty Niall
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner, the most recent coming at Wolverhampton (1m4f, AW) in November; runner-up there over 9.5f in April but he was well beaten at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) three weeks ago and needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Three-time winner; runner-up at Wolverhampton in November but well beaten on turf latest

runners.name: Jack Andrea
runners.spotlight: Won at Southwell in March and April (1m3f/1m4f) before another solid effort when third there 46 days ago; still seeking his first win on turf but has each-way claims in the returning cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Won at Southwell in March and April (1m3f/1m4f); still seeking his first win on turf
runners.owner: J Rayment, J Stephenson & R Eddery

runners.name: Maywedance
runners.spotlight: 0-10 on turf but returned to form to win at Kempton (1m4f, AW) in refitted cheekpieces 24 days ago; her only other win also came over that C&D and she may be vulnerable back on turf off this 3lb higher mark.
runners.comment: 0-10 on turf but returned to form when winning at Kempton; may be vulnerable back on turf

runners.name: Kameel
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner for his previous stable; has tumbled in the weights since joining this yard last summer and ran well in the added visor at Redcar (1m2f, good to firm) 19 days ago, shaping as if this step back up to 1m4f would suit; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Ran well in the added visor at Redcar (1m2f) last time; steps back up to 1m4f; shortlisted

runners.name: Lednikov
runners.spotlight: Six of his seven wins have been on the AW at Southwell, the most recent in March off a 4lb lower mark; below his best in three starts since and others are preferred now he switches back to turf.
runners.comment: Six of his seven wins have been on the AW; below his best in recent starts; back on turf

runners.name: The Bay Warrior
runners.spotlight: All seven wins have been on the AW (1m2f-1m4f) and he ran creditably on his last two starts; however, he's yet to finish in the first three in five starts on turf and others are preferred; cheekpieces are back on for the first time since 2024.
runners.comment: All seven wins have been on the AW (1m2f-1m4f); yet to finish in the first three on turf

runners.name: Cartwheel
runners.spotlight: AW novice winner in October 2024 but she's mostly struggled since finishing seventh in the Nell Gwyn last year; has tumbled in the weights and took a step back in the right direction when third here (1m2f, good to firm; first-time tongue-tie) 18 days ago; claims if seeing out this longer trip.
runners.comment: Took a step back in the right direction here (1m2f) last time; steps up in trip

races.race_name: Best Ticket Prices Online At leicester-racecourse.co.uk Handicap

runners.name: That's Enough
runners.spotlight: Showed improved form but couldn't quite get by Vega's Virtue when runner-up on her turf debut at Ripon (1m, good) 16 days ago; 1lb better off with that rival and makes the shortlist.
runners.comment: Couldn't quite get by Vega's Virtue at Ripon last time but that was an improved effort
runners.owner: Commercium II And Whitsbury Manor Stud

runners.name: Vega's Virtue
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the sixth attempt with an improved effort when upped to 1m at Ripon (good; first-time cheekpieces) 16 days ago, holding off the challenge of That's Enough; back up 2lb but he's open to further progress at this trip.
runners.comment: Off the mark with an improved effort at Ripon (1m) in first-time cheekpieces; up 2lb

runners.name: Cobalt Comet
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden but he finished third at Southwell (1m, AW) in April and Carlisle (1m, soft) last month; respectable effort when fifth of eight at Ripon (1m, good) ten days ago and has an each-way chance.
runners.comment: Respectable effort when fifth of eight at Ripon last time and has each-way claims

runners.name: Quick Turn
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the seventh attempt with an improved effort at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW) in March but he was soundly beaten at Kempton (1m, AW) 24 days ago; turf is a question mark.
runners.comment: Off the mark when winning at Wolverhampton in March but soundly beaten at Kempton latest

runners.name: Beau Chevalier
runners.spotlight: Has shown little in three novice events (7f/1m; AW/good to firm turf) and gelded since last seen 42 days ago; blinkers go on for his handicap debut (dam won in first-time blinkers) but much better is needed.
runners.comment: Shown little in three novice events; gelded and has blinkers added for his handicap debut

runners.name: Talking In Kode
runners.spotlight: Did best of the rest behind a 2-13 shot in a maiden at Beverley (7.5f, good to firm) but she was beaten 6l when sixth of eight on her handicap debut at Ripon (1m, good) ten days ago; may yet do better.
runners.comment: Beaten 6l when sixth of eight on her handicap debut at Ripon but may yet do better

runners.name: Gifts Differing
runners.spotlight: Fifth in two 1m AW novice/maiden events in February/March but failed to build on those efforts when beating only one rival home on her handicap debut at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) two months ago; drops back to 1m with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Beat only one rival home on her handicap debut at Lingfield in April; cheekpieces added

runners.name: Avispado
runners.spotlight: Took a step forward from his previous two efforts when fifth of eight in a novice at Kempton (7f, AW) 24 days ago and now has the hood back on (worn on his debut); bred to be suited by this return to 1m but he will need to settle better on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Took a step forward last time but needs to settle better on this h'cap debut; hood back on

races.race_name: Heather Small Live @leicesterraces Saturday 4th July Handicap

runners.name: Oasis Sunrise
runners.spotlight: Posted her best effort when beating seven rivals at Chepstow (good to firm) on her first try over 1m4f 15 days ago, winning comfortably; unexposed over this sort of trip but she's now in a higher grade following a 6lb rise in the weights.
runners.comment: Posted her best effort when winning over 1m4f at Chepstow; up in grade after a 6lb rise

runners.name: Happy Chandler
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who got off the mark when winning over this C&D (good to firm) in April; runner-up on his next two starts, including here 18 days ago, and he can't be dismissed despite stepping up in class.
runners.comment: Runner-up in both starts since winning over C&D in April; up in class; can't be dismissed

runners.name: Goldie Trickett
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; solid third of ten at Southwell (1m, AW; first-time hood) in April but she didn't see this trip out when behind Jack Sparowe here on her next start; again well held in first-time cheekpieces (discarded) over 1m at Kempton 17 days ago and others make more appeal.
runners.comment: 0-7; well held over 1m at Kempton last time and didn't stay over this C&D last month

runners.name: Everyoneknowsadave
runners.spotlight: All three wins have been on the AW but has been placed on turf on three occasions, including when running on for third at Pontefract (1m2f, good) 11 days ago; makes appeal.
runners.comment: All three wins have been on the AW but ran well at Pontefract last time; makes appeal

runners.name: Sansanetti
runners.spotlight: Progressive last year, winning four times over 1m2f (AW/good to firm turf); weak in the betting and left the impression her first start for 225 days was needed when sixth of eight at Newmarket (1m2f, good) last month; may take a step forward.
runners.comment: Progressive last year, winning four times over 1m2f; needs to build on her reappearance

runners.name: Law Supreme
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner, the latest coming at Bath (1m, good) in April off a 2lb lower mark; had an excuse when below his best at Thirsk and bounced back to form when third of seven behind Jack Sparowe at Yarmouth (1m2f, good) 24 days ago; could go well.
runners.comment: 2lb above his last winning mark and bounced back to form when third at Yarmouth last time

runners.name: Lesrico
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 11 starts came in game fashion on soft ground at Doncaster (1m2f) in October and he ran a fine race when runner-up behind Blue Wonder at Haydock (1m2f, good to soft) on his reappearance 22 days ago, coming clear of the rest; on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Sole win came on soft; ran well when second to Blue Wonder at Haydock last time

runners.name: Blue Wonder
runners.spotlight: Stepped up on her reappearance effort when winning at Haydock (1m2f, good to soft) 22 days ago, pulling clear of the rest with Lesrico who was runner-up; back up 5lb but she could have a bit more to offer over this trip.
runners.comment: Stepped up on her return when winning at Haydock (good to soft) 22 days ago; back up 5lb

runners.name: Jack Sparowe
runners.spotlight: Ended a lengthy losing run when winning in first-time blinkers at Yarmouth (1m2f, good) last month and ran another good race when second of 12 at Wolverhampton (1m4f, AW) 15 days ago; up another 2lb but he's a lively contender.
runners.comment: First-time blinkers when winning at Yarmouth and ran well on the AW over 1m4f last time

runners.name: Bearwith
runners.spotlight: Nine-time winner, the most recent coming on the AW at Newcastle in February off a 1lb lower mark; beaten 1l when runner-up at Nottingham (1m2f, good) in April and his last two efforts have been respectable; each-way chance.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Nottingham in April and two respectable efforts since; each-way chance

races.race_name: Trackside NZ J.J. Atkins (Group 1) (Turf)

runners.name: Berzelius
runners.spotlight: Wore them down late this track last start. Distance increase suits. Right in the mix again.
runners.owner: Kia Ora 2009, W K Loughnan Et Al

runners.name: Tron Bolt
runners.spotlight: Aiming for hat-trick. Impressive coming from back half to score at Rosehill last start. Should appreciate stepping up in distance. Stays under strong notice for this.

runners.name: Martist
runners.spotlight: Great effort in the Spirit Of Boom Classic Doomben two back. Ridden in back half, strong late to fill the minors BRC Sires' Produce this venue. Been thereabouts here. Commands attention here.
runners.owner: S J Kirkham & D S Lewis

runners.name: Kickup Rocky
runners.spotlight: Closed off late from the tail here. Usually gets back. Query wet. Rise to 1600m a new challenge.
runners.owner: Mrs C M Mayne & N C Mayne

runners.name: Voynichese
runners.spotlight: On a short back-up. Latest conquest Country Boosted Mdn at Albury 26 May. Strong Open 1500 win from midfield this track last outing. Could give some cheek at odds.
runners.owner: N J Olive, M L Motum Et Al

runners.name: Dream Roca
runners.spotlight: Terrific in the Spirit Of Boom Classic Doomben two back. Sent out $18 when unable to threaten this venue. Likes some juice in the track. Ready for the mile now.
runners.owner: P N Watson, Mackellar Syndicate Et Al

runners.name: Cormier
runners.spotlight: Handily placed and gave his all at Rosehill in only his second race appearance when in the market. Could get into the placings.
runners.owner: Arrowfield Pastoral Pty Ltd & Pinecliff Racing

runners.name: Stormy Marco
runners.spotlight: Good debut at Hawkesbury 2 May. Not disgraced in BRC Sires' Produce this venue in only his second race appearance at $11. Can fill the minors here.

runners.name: Heatstroke
runners.spotlight: Got back, cut loose late to fill the minors at Rosehill at his second race start. Others more persuasive.
runners.owner: W Freedman, S Smith Et Al

runners.name: Silken Salute
runners.spotlight: Dual Eagle Farm winner in first campaign. Placed Ken Russell Memorial Classic at Gold Coast 9 May. Battled home without looking a threat this venue. More ground a plus.
runners.owner: Magic Bloodstock Racing, Cambridge Stud Et Al

runners.name: Vanzadee
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Never a threat after showing no early speed from the barriers this venue at second run back. Lacks appeal here.
runners.owner: P V Carr, S A Stewart Et Al

runners.name: Macedon Mission
runners.spotlight: Started $91 when safely held in Open company this venue. Has an adverse draw. First try at 1600m. Hard to warm to.
runners.owner: C A Bain, P L Pender Et Al

runners.name: Glenorchy
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Stationed around midfield, positive response gaining late ground this venue at his second race start. Has an adverse draw. Place preferred option.
runners.owner: Star Thoroughbreds Pty Ltd, Paramount Racing & O Enriquez

races.race_name: Ladbrokes Stradbroke Handicap (Group 1) (Handicap) (Turf)

runners.name: Regal Award
runners.spotlight: Won in decisive fashion this venue at last start this journey. Tackling stronger company.
runners.owner: Ms D S Sealey, G & C Pastoral Co Pty Ltd & V A Oldfield

runners.name: Headley Grange
runners.spotlight: In touch and fought on dauntlessly this track. Handles the sting out of the ground. A distance specialist. Deep into his campaign and can challenge again.
runners.owner: R J Manwaring, S C Foley Et Al

runners.name: Spicy Martini
runners.spotlight: Fresh. Solid performance in defeat in the Doomben 10,000 at Doomben at second run back. Since trialled at Gold Coast 2 Jun. Sharper now and could add some value to exotics.
runners.owner: Miss H R Penfold, A R E Investments Et Al

runners.name: Tuned
runners.spotlight: Won the 3&4yo Gateway Eagle Farm 13 Dec. Valiant when just denied the minors at Doomben in second run from spell. Tackling stronger line-up.
runners.owner: Trilogy, S A McQueen Et Al

runners.name: Sixties
runners.spotlight: Ran on late from the back this track at last start this journey. Others with more appeal.

runners.name: Warnie
runners.spotlight: Fresh. Won the 3yo+ SWP Pakenham 13 Dec. Started $11 when safely held in Open company in rain affected going at Doomben at second run back. Since trialled at Gold Coast 2 Jun.
runners.owner: Bennett Racing Pty Ltd, D Phillips Et Al

runners.name: Abounding
runners.spotlight: Closed strongly from inside midfield to prevail in final bounds at Doomben. Strikes a harder race.
runners.owner: First Light Racing Pty Ltd, G J Walker Et Al

runners.name: Splash Back
runners.spotlight: Annexed the Victory Stakes at Eagle Farm 2 May. Made late rally from the back here. Usually gets back. Hard to warm to.
runners.owner: Ronball, C McGregor & Miss Z J McGregor

runners.name: Skyhook
runners.spotlight: Took out the Hawkesbury Guineas at Hawkesbury 2 May. On speed, outgunned late but had enough in the tank for second Fred Best Classic here over this distance. Could add some value to exotics.
runners.owner: Ryan & Alexiou Racing, Stallion Match Et Al

runners.name: Payline
runners.spotlight: Fresh. Long time since last saluted. Raced in back half and was never in a position to mount a serious challenge at Doomben. Since trialled at Doomben 2 Jun.
runners.owner: Mrs C L Munce, T D Chivers Et Al

runners.name: Yellow Brick
runners.spotlight: The Archer winner Rockhampton 5 May. Did little this venue second-up. Has a tricky draw. Looking elsewhere.
runners.owner: Mrs L J Sears, Ms M L Sears & Mrs S E Wee

runners.name: Transatlantic
runners.spotlight: Led and brave in defeat here at second run back after speccing from $14 into $11. On pacer. At optimum distance. Sharper now and has some claims.
runners.owner: Mrs Z A Daly, Segenhoe Thoroughbreds Australia Et Al

runners.name: Estadio Mestalla
runners.spotlight: 4 weeks since last run. Ran on late from the back at Scone second-up. Had two trials since. First time around Eagle Farm.
runners.owner: NSW Tycoons, M Galanos Et Al

runners.name: Von Hauke
runners.spotlight: Latest conquest Golden Mile at Bendigo 2 May. Last at turn, ran on late this venue. Awkwardly drawn. Others with stronger claims.
runners.owner: Barree Pastoral Co Pty Ltd & Imperial Racing Stable

runners.name: King Of Roseau
runners.spotlight: Very tame performance (wide barrier) this venue as an odds drifter. Others with stronger claims.
runners.owner: C T Hay, P M O'Shea Et Al

runners.name: Sepals
runners.spotlight: Comfortably beaten this track. Has an adverse draw. Others more persuasive.
runners.owner: C I Brown, H A Brown Et Al

runners.name: Cifrado
runners.spotlight: Patiently handled, set alight and went down narrowly in the BRC Sprint at Doomben 23 May. Since trialled at Toowoomba Inner 2 Jun. Others rate stronger.

runners.name: Private Eye
runners.spotlight: Experienced campaigner. Great effort in the Hall Mark Stakes Randwick two back. Close up in sound performance Kingsford Smith Cup this track at second run back following a market drift. Breaks from a low barrier and under less weight.
runners.owner: Proven Thoroughbreds, P Yates Et Al

runners.name: Desert Lightning
runners.spotlight: Latest conquest The Goodwood at Morphettville 9 May. Not disgraced this track. Strong wet stats. Not out of this.

runners.name: Fangirl
runners.spotlight: Ultra professional mare with a lethal turn of speed and unshakable tenacity. Won the 3yo+ WFA Randwick 20 Sep. Kept coming and just fell short here in second run from spell. Fitter for those two runs and ready to show best.

runners.name: Another Wil
runners.spotlight: Tough and honest when just behind the minors in the Kingsford Smith Cup this venue at second run back. Has early speed. Hard to warm to.
runners.owner: Halo Racing Services Pty Ltd, Mrs Janice McKenna Et Al

runners.name: Rothfire
runners.spotlight: Won the Doomben 10,000 at Doomben two back 16 May. Quietly handled in back half, shaken up and finished full of running here. Major player here.
runners.owner: Mrs V A Heathcote, Miss S R Freeman Et Al

runners.name: Welwal
runners.spotlight: Fresh. Took out the Doncaster Prelude at Rosehill 28 Mar. Made late rally from the back at Doomben. Has a tricky draw.
runners.owner: A J Gauci, Mrs E Gauci Et Al

races.race_name: Sunbird Griffin Plate (Maiden) (Course C+3) (Turf)

runners.name: Sharp Planet
runners.spotlight: Trial winner; definite chance on his debut second over C&D and he had a wicked draw over the 6f 13 days ago (behind some of these); stall 1 isn't absolutely ideal here over the straight 5f but it's a small field.
runners.comment: Promising debut and he had a wicked draw over the 6f 13 days ago
runners.owner: Yiu King Chuen, Chino Yiu Chin Hang & Chris Yiu Chun Hang

runners.name: Dragon Generation
runners.spotlight: No better than sixth in his three trial runs so wouldn't be the obvious answer to this.
runners.comment: No better than sixth in his three trial runs so wouldn't be the obvious answer to this

runners.name: Kyoto Spring
runners.spotlight: Has finished last in his three trials since January and makes limited appeal.
runners.comment: Has finished last in his three trials since January and makes limited appeal
runners.owner: Li Wai Yin & Gina Li Wai Shuen

runners.name: Jedi Spurs
runners.spotlight: Has won both his trials, the latest here on turf when making all; could be interesting.
runners.comment: Has won both his trials, the latest here on turf when making all; could be interesting

runners.name: Chancheng Sparkle
runners.spotlight: Form figures of 67734 in trials but made a promising debut over 6f here two weeks ago, finishing just behind Quantum Wukong; he finished that race well so dropping back to 5f is a slight concern, but can run well.
runners.comment: Made a promising debut over 6f here two weeks ago

runners.name: Show Me Your Love
runners.spotlight: Has won a trial but held by some of these on his two runs here and has work to do.
runners.comment: Has won a trial but held by some of these on his two runs here and has work to do

runners.name: Quantum Wukong
runners.spotlight: Form in trials nothing to boast about but he did mighty well to finish fourth and ahead of three of these when drawn wide over 6f here 13 days ago; has to prove he's quick enough for 5f but has to be feared.
runners.comment: Ahead of three of these when drawn wide over 6f here 13 days ago

races.race_name: Shearwater Handicap (Class 5) (Course C+3) (Turf)

runners.name: Von Baer
runners.spotlight: Won in Britain but no joy as yet in Hong Kong and was behind Robot Knight and Von Chaleur in his penultimate race.
runners.comment: No joy in Hong Kong and was behind Robot Knight and Von Chaleur in his penultimate race

runners.name: Dash
runners.spotlight: Reserve; placed over C&D in March and feasible excuses since on account of 7f and a bad draw; not without hope.
runners.comment: Reserve; placed over C&D in March and feasible excuses since

runners.name: Verbier
runners.spotlight: He seems fine from 6f to 1m1f; didn't run too badly from a bad draw over 7f here last time and this mark isn't beyond him.
runners.comment: Didn't run too badly from a bad draw over 7f here last time and this mark isn't beyond him

runners.name: Country Dancer
runners.spotlight: Last of two wins was over C&D in January 2025; has dropped to what should be a good mark but recent efforts haven't been persuasive.
runners.comment: Dropped to what should be a good mark but recent efforts haven't been persuasive

runners.name: Handsome Blond
runners.spotlight: Very opposable on his track form and on what he's shown in trials; well drawn but the positives stop there.
runners.comment: Very opposable on his track form and on what he's shown in trials

runners.name: Viva Chaleur
runners.spotlight: Only sixth in a recent trial but had been in a good run of form and he has a 4lb pull with Robot Knight on their close encounter on dirt here last month; enters the reckoning.
runners.comment: He has a 4lb pull with Robot Knight on their close encounter on dirt here last month

runners.name: Robot Knight
runners.spotlight: Narrow winner (from Viva Chaleur) on dirt last month and that second career success had been coming; up 6lb with Britney Wong again claiming 7lb but he's well drawn for a prominent racer.
runners.comment: Narrow winner on dirt last month and that second career success had been coming

runners.name: The All Rounder
runners.spotlight: Has the form in Class 4s to feature at this level but never got seriously involved in this grade last time out and bit to prove at the minute.
runners.comment: Never got seriously involved in this grade last time out and bit to prove at the minute

runners.name: Yee Cheong Raider
runners.spotlight: Reserve; competitive over 6f and 7f here this year and, although a beaten favourite last time, he looks a winner waiting to happen off this mark.
runners.comment: Reserve; beaten fav last time but looks a winner waiting to happen off this mark
runners.owner: Choi Hiu Fung & Choi Chun Lung

runners.name: Aquaman
runners.spotlight: Down to Class 5 level for the first time but it looks warranted and he's drawn wide; best run to date was over 7f.
runners.comment: Down to Class 5 level for the first time but it looks warranted and he's drawn wide
runners.owner: Steve Chan Chi Fung & Terry Ng Ting Chi

runners.name: Money Tycoon
runners.spotlight: Another class-dropper with questions to answer, though there was some promise in his penultimate race over C&D and he ran over 7f last time.
runners.comment: Questions to answer, though there was some promise in his penultimate race over C&D

runners.name: Always Fluke
